{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:26:30.664Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_pqUH39N7jtVqQ3F1BWCNoEo0",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 9cce5e\nWall time: 0.0525 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2723\nOutput:\n# CODING_AGENT.md — Spring & Java Coding Standards\n\n> Read this file before writing any production code.\n> These are the coding standards for this codebase. Every class, method, and annotation\n> must follow these rules. When in doubt, refer to `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` for\n> where code lives and this file for how to write it.\n\n---\n\n## Before Writing Any Code — Checklist\n\n- [ ] Read `PRODUCT.md` — confirm which module owns this feature, check the inter-module event map and ACL facade names\n- [ ] Read the confirmed `SPEC.md` for this feature — domain model, API, business logic, error handling\n- [ ] Read `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` — confirm which package and layer each class belongs to\n- [ ] Read `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` — testing implementation/execution/logging ownership is delegated to the Test Agent\n\n### Scope Boundary for CODING_AGENT\n\n- CODING_AGENT owns **production code quality and architecture compliance**.\n- CODING_AGENT does **not** own end-to-end test authoring/execution workflow by default.\n- Test creation, execution, and failure/fix logging are owned by `agents/TEST_AGENT.md`.\n- If explicitly asked to write tests in the same task, follow `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` and coordinate with TEST_AGENT standards.\n\n---\n\n## Java & Spring Version Baseline\n\n```\nJava:          21 (use records, sealed classes, pattern matching, text blocks where appropriate)\nSpring Boot:   3.5.11\nSpring:        6.x\nMapStruct:     1.5.x\nLombok:        use sparingly — prefer Java records for DTOs and value objects\nJPA:           Jakarta Persistence (jakarta.persistence.*) — NOT javax.persistence\nFlyway:        version managed in parent pom.xml only\n```\n\n---\n\n## General Coding Rules\n\n### Immutability First\n\nPrefer immutable objects. Use Java records for DTOs, events, and value objects.\nOnly use mutable classes when the lifecycle explicitly requires mutation (e.g. JPA entities).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — record for response DTO\npublic record BookingResponse(UUID id, String status, UUID userId) {}\n\n// ✅ Correct — record for event payload\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(UUID bookingId, UUID userId, UUID showSlotId) {}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — mutable class for a DTO\npublic class BookingResponse {\n    private UUID id;\n    public void setId(UUID id) { this.id = id; }\n}\n```\n\n### No Nulls in Public APIs\n\nUse `Optional<T>` for values that may be absent. Never return `null` from a public method.\nNever accept `null` as a valid input — validate at the boundary with `@NotNull`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\npublic Optional<Booking> findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — null return leaks nullability into callers\npublic Booking findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id).orElse(null);\n}\n```\n\n### Use `Money` for All Monetary Values\n\nNever use `double`, `float`, or raw `BigDecimal` for monetary amounts.\nAlways use the `Money` value object from `shared/common/domain/Money`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nprivate final Money price;\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nprivate BigDecimal price;\nprivate double price;\n```\n\n### Logging\n\nUse SLF4J with `@Slf4j` (Lombok). Never use `System.out.println`.\nLog at the correct level:\n\n| Level | When to Use |\n|---|---|\n| `TRACE` | Verbose debug — method entry/exit during development only |\n| `DEBUG` | Internal state during a complex operation |\n| `INFO` | Significant business events: booking confirmed, payment received |\n| `WARN` | Recoverable issues: seat lock expired, retry attempted |\n| `ERROR` | Unrecoverable failures: payment charged but DB write failed |\n\nAlways include structured context. Use MDC for `traceId` and `requestId` (configured in `shared/config/TracingConfig`).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed. bookingId={} userId={} amount={}\", bookingId, userId, amount);\nlog.error(\"DB write failed after payment charge. bookingId={} paymentIntentId={}\", bookingId, paymentIntentId, e);\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nSystem.out.println(\"booking done\");\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed: \" + bookingId); // string concatenation, not parameterised\n```\n\n---\n\n## Inter-Module Communication Patterns\n\nModules communicate via REST (sync queries) or Spring Events (async commands). Never import another module's @Service directly (exception: `admin/` may import module `@Service` for READ-only operations).\n\n**Pattern 1: REST Query** — Module B needs data from upstream Module A\n```java\n// ✅ Call REST API\nprivate final RestClient restClient;\npublic Payment getPaymentDetails(UUID paymentId) {\n    return restClient.get(\"/api/payments/{id}\", paymentId, Payment.class);\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import @Service\nprivate final PaymentService paymentService; // WRONG\n```\n\n**Pattern 2: Spring Events** — Module A publishes, others listen\n```java\n// Module A publishes\neventPublisher.publishEvent(new PaymentConfirmedEvent(bookingId, amount));\n\n// Module B listens (delegates immediately to service)\n@Component\npublic class PaymentConfirmedListener {\n    @TransactionalEventListener(phase = TransactionPhase.AFTER_COMMIT)\n    public void onPaymentConfirmed(PaymentConfirmedEvent event) {\n        bookingService.confirmBooking(event.bookingId());\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never call service directly from event trigger\n// ❌ Never block the listener — delegate immediately\n```\n\n**Pattern 3: Shared Contracts** — Enums, DTOs, base classes in `shared/common/` only\n```java\n// ✅ Define once in shared/common/enums/\npublic enum BookingStatus { PENDING, CONFIRMED, CANCELLED, REFUNDED }\n\n// ❌ Never duplicate in multiple modules\n```\n\n**Pattern 4: Shared Reader Interfaces (hot-path in-process reads)** — For high-frequency reads where HTTP overhead is unacceptable, define a read-only interface in `shared/common/service/` and implement it in the owning module. The consuming module injects the `shared/` interface — never the owning module's concrete `@Service`.\n\n```java\n// In shared/common/service/ — interface only, no implementation\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);  // per-line-item data\n\n    /**\n     * Returns cart-level header metadata.\n     * Used by promotions.CouponEligibilityService to validate coupon eligibility\n     * (orgId, slotId, couponCode, expiresAt, currency).\n     * Throws ResourceNotFoundException if cart does not exist.\n     */\n    CartSummaryDto getCartSummary(Long cartId);\n}\n\n// In booking-inventory/service/ — owns the data, implements the contract\n@Service\n@Transactional(readOnly = true)\npublic class CartSnapshotReaderImpl implements CartSnapshotReader {\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n    public List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId) {\n        return cartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId).stream()\n            .map(mapper::toDto).toList();\n    }\n}\n\n// In payments-ticketing/service/ — injects shared interface only, never the impl directly\n@Service\npublic class PaymentService {\n    private final CartSnapshotReader cartSnapshotReader; // ✅ shared interface\n    // ❌ private final CartSnapshotReaderImpl ... // WRONG — direct module import\n}\n```\n\nCurrent shared reader interfaces:\n- `SlotSummaryReader` → implemented in `scheduling`, consumed by `booking-inventory`\n- `SlotPricingReader` → implemented in `scheduling`, consumed by `booking-inventory`\n- `CartSnapshotReader` → implemented in `booking-inventory`, consumed by `payments-ticketing` (payment confirmation hot path) and `promotions` (coupon eligibility via `getCartSummary()`)\n- `PaymentConfirmationReader` → implemented in `payments-ticketing`, consumed by `booking-inventory` `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog`\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facades — The Only Door to External Systems\n\n**HARD RULE: No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly. All calls go through shared/eventbrite/service/ facades only.**\n\n### The 10 Eventbrite Facades (All in `shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n| Facade | Used By | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| EbEventSyncService | discovery-catalog, scheduling, engagement | Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull catalog |\n| EbVenueService | scheduling | Create/update venues; list org venues |\n| EbScheduleService | scheduling | Create recurring event schedules |\n| EbTicketService | discovery-catalog, booking-inventory | Create/update ticket classes; check availability |\n| EbCapacityService | admin/ | Update event capacity tiers |\n| EbOrderService | admin/ (reporting only) | Read EB orders for organizer-side admin reporting only — **NOT used in FR5/FR6 payment flows**; Stripe owns all payments |\n| EbAttendeeService | engagement | Get attendees; verify attendance for reviews (FR8 only) |\n| EbDiscountSyncService | promotions | Create/update/delete discount codes |\n| EbRefundService | — (inactive) | Retained for legacy compatibility only — **do NOT call for any refund**; all refunds via `StripeRefundService` |\n| EbWebhookService | mock-eventbrite-api only | Mock/testing webhook registration and delivery simulation (NOT production) |\n\n### Correct Pattern\n\n```java\n// ✅ Inject facade, call it\n@Service\npublic class EventService {\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventService; // from shared/\n    \n    public Event createEvent(EventRequest request) {\n        Event event = new Event(request.name(), request.venue());\n        eventRepository.save(event); // save internally first\n        \n        EbEventResponse ebResp = ebEventService.createEvent(\n            mapper.toEbCreateRequest(event));\n        event.setEventbriteEventId(ebResp.id()); // store EB ID\n        eventRepository.save(event);\n        return event;\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import and call HTTP client directly\nprivate final EventbriteWebClient ebClient;\npublic void createEvent(...) {\n    ebClient.post(\"/events\", ...); // VIOLATES HARD RULE #2\n}\n```\n\n### Critical Constraints (These Don't Exist on Eventbrite)\n\n- **No user creation API** — Users 100% internal; link post-purchase via order email\n- **No order creation API** — Orders are created internally in your `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed. The EB Checkout Widget is **removed** — do NOT use or reference it.\n- **No single-order cancel** — Only bulk event cancel; per-order buyer cancellation uses `StripeRefundService` — no EB call involved\n- **No seat lock API** — Entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal Redis + Spring State Machine\n- **No refund submission API** — All refunds submitted via `StripeRefundService` (`POST /v1/refunds`). `EbRefundService` is **inactive** — do NOT call it for any refund flow.\n- **No conflict validation API** — Turnaround gaps enforced entirely in internal DB\n- **No reviews API** — Reviews 100% internal; Eventbrite attendance verification only (EbAttendeeService, engagement module, FR8 only)\n\n**See docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md for full constraints and workarounds.**\n"
  }
}
```

    "output": "Chunk ID: 9cce5e\nWall time: 0.0525 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2723\nOutput:\n# CODING_AGENT.md — Spring & Java Coding Standards\n\n> Read this file before writing any production code.\n> These are the coding standards for this codebase. Every class, method, and annotation\n> must follow these rules. When in doubt, refer to `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` for\n> where code lives and this file for how to write it.\n\n---\n\n## Before Writing Any Code — Checklist\n\n- [ ] Read `PRODUCT.md` — confirm which module owns this feature, check the inter-module event map and ACL facade names\n- [ ] Read the confirmed `SPEC.md` for this feature — domain model, API, business logic, error handling\n- [ ] Read `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` — confirm which package and layer each class belongs to\n- [ ] Read `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` — testing implementation/execution/logging ownership is delegated to the Test Agent\n\n### Scope Boundary for CODING_AGENT\n\n- CODING_AGENT owns **production code quality and architecture compliance**.\n- CODING_AGENT does **not** own end-to-end test authoring/execution workflow by default.\n- Test creation, execution, and failure/fix logging are owned by `agents/TEST_AGENT.md`.\n- If explicitly asked to write tests in the same task, follow `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` and coordinate with TEST_AGENT standards.\n\n---\n\n## Java & Spring Version Baseline\n\n```\nJava:          21 (use records, sealed classes, pattern matching, text blocks where appropriate)\nSpring Boot:   3.5.11\nSpring:        6.x\nMapStruct:     1.5.x\nLombok:        use sparingly — prefer Java records for DTOs and value objects\nJPA:           Jakarta Persistence (jakarta.persistence.*) — NOT javax.persistence\nFlyway:        version managed in parent pom.xml only\n```\n\n---\n\n## General Coding Rules\n\n### Immutability First\n\nPrefer immutable objects. Use Java records for DTOs, events, and value objects.\nOnly use mutable classes when the lifecycle explicitly requires mutation (e.g. JPA entities).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — record for response DTO\npublic record BookingResponse(UUID id, String status, UUID userId) {}\n\n// ✅ Correct — record for event payload\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(UUID bookingId, UUID userId, UUID showSlotId) {}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — mutable class for a DTO\npublic class BookingResponse {\n    private UUID id;\n    public void setId(UUID id) { this.id = id; }\n}\n```\n\n### No Nulls in Public APIs\n\nUse `Optional<T>` for values that may be absent. Never return `null` from a public method.\nNever accept `null` as a valid input — validate at the boundary with `@NotNull`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\npublic Optional<Booking> findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id);\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — null return leaks nullability into callers\npublic Booking findById(UUID id) {\n    return bookingRepository.findById(id).orElse(null);\n}\n```\n\n### Use `Money` for All Monetary Values\n\nNever use `double`, `float`, or raw `BigDecimal` for monetary amounts.\nAlways use the `Money` value object from `shared/common/domain/Money`.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nprivate final Money price;\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nprivate BigDecimal price;\nprivate double price;\n```\n\n### Logging\n\nUse SLF4J with `@Slf4j` (Lombok). Never use `System.out.println`.\nLog at the correct level:\n\n| Level | When to Use |\n|---|---|\n| `TRACE` | Verbose debug — method entry/exit during development only |\n| `DEBUG` | Internal state during a complex operation |\n| `INFO` | Significant business events: booking confirmed, payment received |\n| `WARN` | Recoverable issues: seat lock expired, retry attempted |\n| `ERROR` | Unrecoverable failures: payment charged but DB write failed |\n\nAlways include structured context. Use MDC for `traceId` and `requestId` (configured in `shared/config/TracingConfig`).\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed. bookingId={} userId={} amount={}\", bookingId, userId, amount);\nlog.error(\"DB write failed after payment charge. bookingId={} paymentIntentId={}\", bookingId, paymentIntentId, e);\n\n// ❌ Wrong\nSystem.out.println(\"booking done\");\nlog.info(\"Booking confirmed: \" + bookingId); // string concatenation, not parameterised\n```\n\n---\n\n## Inter-Module Communication Patterns\n\nModules communicate via REST (sync queries) or Spring Events (async commands). Never import another module's @Service directly (exception: `admin/` may import module `@Service` for READ-only operations).\n\n**Pattern 1: REST Query** — Module B needs data from upstream Module A\n```java\n// ✅ Call REST API\nprivate final RestClient restClient;\npublic Payment getPaymentDetails(UUID paymentId) {\n    return restClient.get(\"/api/payments/{id}\", paymentId, Payment.class);\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import @Service\nprivate final PaymentService paymentService; // WRONG\n```\n\n**Pattern 2: Spring Events** — Module A publishes, others listen\n```java\n// Module A publishes\neventPublisher.publishEvent(new PaymentConfirmedEvent(bookingId, amount));\n\n// Module B listens (delegates immediately to service)\n@Component\npublic class PaymentConfirmedListener {\n    @TransactionalEventListener(phase = TransactionPhase.AFTER_COMMIT)\n    public void onPaymentConfirmed(PaymentConfirmedEvent event) {\n        bookingService.confirmBooking(event.bookingId());\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never call service directly from event trigger\n// ❌ Never block the listener — delegate immediately\n```\n\n**Pattern 3: Shared Contracts** — Enums, DTOs, base classes in `shared/common/` only\n```java\n// ✅ Define once in shared/common/enums/\npublic enum BookingStatus { PENDING, CONFIRMED, CANCELLED, REFUNDED }\n\n// ❌ Never duplicate in multiple modules\n```\n\n**Pattern 4: Shared Reader Interfaces (hot-path in-process reads)** — For high-frequency reads where HTTP overhead is unacceptable, define a read-only interface in `shared/common/service/` and implement it in the owning module. The consuming module injects the `shared/` interface — never the owning module's concrete `@Service`.\n\n```java\n// In shared/common/service/ — interface only, no implementation\npublic interface CartSnapshotReader {\n    List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId);  // per-line-item data\n\n    /**\n     * Returns cart-level header metadata.\n     * Used by promotions.CouponEligibilityService to validate coupon eligibility\n     * (orgId, slotId, couponCode, expiresAt, currency).\n     * Throws ResourceNotFoundException if cart does not exist.\n     */\n    CartSummaryDto getCartSummary(Long cartId);\n}\n\n// In booking-inventory/service/ — owns the data, implements the contract\n@Service\n@Transactional(readOnly = true)\npublic class CartSnapshotReaderImpl implements CartSnapshotReader {\n    private final CartItemRepository cartItemRepository;\n    public List<CartItemSnapshotDto> getCartItems(Long cartId) {\n        return cartItemRepository.findByCartId(cartId).stream()\n            .map(mapper::toDto).toList();\n    }\n}\n\n// In payments-ticketing/service/ — injects shared interface only, never the impl directly\n@Service\npublic class PaymentService {\n    private final CartSnapshotReader cartSnapshotReader; // ✅ shared interface\n    // ❌ private final CartSnapshotReaderImpl ... // WRONG — direct module import\n}\n```\n\nCurrent shared reader interfaces:\n- `SlotSummaryReader` → implemented in `scheduling`, consumed by `booking-inventory`\n- `SlotPricingReader` → implemented in `scheduling`, consumed by `booking-inventory`\n- `CartSnapshotReader` → implemented in `booking-inventory`, consumed by `payments-ticketing` (payment confirmation hot path) and `promotions` (coupon eligibility via `getCartSummary()`)\n- `PaymentConfirmationReader` → implemented in `payments-ticketing`, consumed by `booking-inventory` `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog`\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facades — The Only Door to External Systems\n\n**HARD RULE: No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly. All calls go through shared/eventbrite/service/ facades only.**\n\n### The 10 Eventbrite Facades (All in `shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n| Facade | Used By | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| EbEventSyncService | discovery-catalog, scheduling, engagement | Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull catalog |\n| EbVenueService | scheduling | Create/update venues; list org venues |\n| EbScheduleService | scheduling | Create recurring event schedules |\n| EbTicketService | discovery-catalog, booking-inventory | Create/update ticket classes; check availability |\n| EbCapacityService | admin/ | Update event capacity tiers |\n| EbOrderService | admin/ (reporting only) | Read EB orders for organizer-side admin reporting only — **NOT used in FR5/FR6 payment flows**; Stripe owns all payments |\n| EbAttendeeService | engagement | Get attendees; verify attendance for reviews (FR8 only) |\n| EbDiscountSyncService | promotions | Create/update/delete discount codes |\n| EbRefundService | — (inactive) | Retained for legacy compatibility only — **do NOT call for any refund**; all refunds via `StripeRefundService` |\n| EbWebhookService | mock-eventbrite-api only | Mock/testing webhook registration and delivery simulation (NOT production) |\n\n### Correct Pattern\n\n```java\n// ✅ Inject facade, call it\n@Service\npublic class EventService {\n    private final EbEventSyncService ebEventService; // from shared/\n    \n    public Event createEvent(EventRequest request) {\n        Event event = new Event(request.name(), request.venue());\n        eventRepository.save(event); // save internally first\n        \n        EbEventResponse ebResp = ebEventService.createEvent(\n            mapper.toEbCreateRequest(event));\n        event.setEventbriteEventId(ebResp.id()); // store EB ID\n        eventRepository.save(event);\n        return event;\n    }\n}\n\n// ❌ Never import and call HTTP client directly\nprivate final EventbriteWebClient ebClient;\npublic void createEvent(...) {\n    ebClient.post(\"/events\", ...); // VIOLATES HARD RULE #2\n}\n```\n\n### Critical Constraints (These Don't Exist on Eventbrite)\n\n- **No user creation API** — Users 100% internal; link post-purchase via order email\n- **No order creation API** — Orders are created internally in your `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed. The EB Checkout Widget is **removed** — do NOT use or reference it.\n- **No single-order cancel** — Only bulk event cancel; per-order buyer cancellation uses `StripeRefundService` — no EB call involved\n- **No seat lock API** — Entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal Redis + Spring State Machine\n- **No refund submission API** — All refunds submitted via `StripeRefundService` (`POST /v1/refunds`). `EbRefundService` is **inactive** — do NOT call it for any refund flow.\n- **No conflict validation API** — Turnaround gaps enforced entirely in internal DB\n- **No reviews API** — Reviews 100% internal; Eventbrite attendance verification only (EbAttendeeService, engagement module, FR8 only)\n\n**See docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md for full constraints and workarounds.**\n"
  }
}
```
---

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:26:30.670Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_44b2SdDnWKB2ZoBsxI3pZ1cl",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 9cde08\nWall time: 0.0524 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3259\nOutput:\n# Modular Monolith — Canonical Folder Structure Template\n\n> **Purpose:** This document is the canonical reference for building any new feature or module\n> in this codebase. Every agent, developer, or tool that adds code MUST follow this structure\n> exactly. It enforces high cohesion, low coupling, and zero cyclic dependencies by design.\n\n---\n\n## Core Architectural Rules (Read Before Writing Any Code)\n\n### Rule 1 — Dependency Direction is One-Way and Downward Only\n```\nshared/          ← no dependencies on any module (foundational)\n    ↓\n[module-A]       ← may depend on shared only\n    ↓\n[module-B]       ← may depend on shared + module-A\n    ↓\n[module-C]       ← may depend on shared + module-A + module-B\n    ↓\nadmin/           ← thin orchestration layer, depends on all modules but owns no domain\n    ↓\napp/             ← entry point only, no business logic\n```\n**A module NEVER imports from a module below it or at the same level.**\n**If two modules need to talk, they do so through Spring Events — never direct service calls.**\n\n### Rule 2 — Three Ways Modules Communicate (Pick the Right One)\n| Communication Type | When to Use | Mechanism |\n|---|---|---|\n| Query (sync, read-only) | Module B needs data that Module A owns | Module B calls Module A's **public API endpoint** (HTTP), never imports Module A's service class directly |\n| Command (async, fire-and-forget) | Module A completed something and others should react | Module A publishes a **Spring ApplicationEvent**; interested modules register a listener |\n| Shared contract | Enums, base classes, standard DTOs used by 2+ modules | Lives in `shared/common/` only — never duplicated |\n\n### Rule 3 — The ACL is the Only Door to External Systems\nAny external API (Eventbrite, payment gateway, SMS provider, etc.) MUST be accessed\nexclusively through the `shared/` ACL package. No module ever calls an external API directly.\nIf a new external integration is needed, it goes in `shared/` first, then modules consume it\nvia the ACL facade services.\n\n### Rule 4 — A Module Owns Its Own Database Tables\nNo two modules share a JPA entity or database table. If Module B needs data from Module A's\ntable, it calls Module A's API — it does NOT import Module A's `@Entity` class or `@Repository`.\nViolating this rule creates hidden coupling at the database layer.\n\n### Rule 5 — The `admin/` Module Owns No Domain\nThe admin module only orchestrates and aggregates. It delegates all writes to the owning module.\nIt never defines its own `@Entity` or owns a database table. It is a read + orchestration layer.\n\nFor inter-module access, admin follows this rule:\n- **Reads** — admin MAY import a module's `@Service` bean directly (same JVM, no overhead)\n- **Writes** — admin MUST call the owning module's REST API (never bypass the module's own validation, events, and business rules)\n\n### Rule 6 — Mapping Logic Belongs in the Mapper Layer Only\nConverting domain objects to API response DTOs and request DTOs to domain objects must be done\nexclusively in the `mapper/` package. Controllers and services must never call `.builder()` on a\nresponse DTO directly or manually map fields.\n- **Controllers** — call service, return result. No mapping.\n- **Services** — orchestrate domain + infra. No mapping.\n- **Mappers** — the only place field translation happens. Use MapStruct.\n\n---\n\n## Project Layout\n\n```\n[project-root]/\n├── pom.xml                    ← Parent POM. All dependency versions managed here only.\n├── shared/                    ← Internal library. Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

Never imports from any module.\n├── [module-name]/             ← One folder per bounded context.\n├── admin/                     ← Thin orchestration layer. Owns no domain, no tables.\n└── app/                       ← Spring Boot entry point. Zero business logic.\n```\n\n---\n\n## shared/ Structure\n\nCross-cutting concerns only. Every module depends on this.\n\n```\nshared/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/shared/\n    ├── [external-system-acl]/     ← One package per external system (e.g. razorpay/, eventbrite/)\n    │   ├── client/                ← Raw HTTP calls only\n    │   ├── config/                ← WebClient bean, rate limiter config\n    │   ├── dto/request/           ← External system's raw request shapes\n    │   ├── dto/response/          ← External system's raw response shapes\n    │   ├── mapper/                ← Translates external DTOs → internal domain objects\n    │   ├── service/               ← THE ONLY THING MODULES CALL. One service per capability.\n    │   ├── webhook/               ← Receives inbound webhooks, dispatches as Spring Events\n    │   └── resilience/            ← Circuit breaker + fallback config per external system\n    │\n    ├── security/\n    │   ├── JwtTokenProvider       ← Generate + validate JWTs\n    │   ├── JwtAuthenticationFilter← Spring Security filter, registered once\n    │   └── SecurityConstants      ← Token TTLs, header names, role constants\n    │\n    ├── common/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   │   ├── BaseEntity         ← id, createdAt, updatedAt — all entities extend this\n    │   │   └── Money              ← Value object for all monetary amounts. Never use primitives.\n    │   ├── dto/\n    │   │   ├── ApiResponse        ← Standard success response wrapper\n    │   │   ├── PageResponse       ← Paginated list wrapper\n    │   │   └── ErrorResponse      ← Standard error shape\n    │   ├── enums/                 ← Enums shared across 2+ modules only\n    │   └── exception/\n    │       ├── GlobalExceptionHandler  ← @ControllerAdvice. One handler for the entire app.\n    │       ├── ResourceNotFoundException   ← 404\n    │       ├── BusinessRuleException       ← 422\n    │       └── IntegrationException        ← 502\n    │\n    └── config/\n        ├── RedisConfig            ← Shared Redis bean\n        ├── ElasticsearchConfig    ← Shared ES client bean\n        ├── CacheConfig            ← Cache names and TTLs\n        ├── AsyncConfig            ← Thread pool for @Async\n        ├── OpenApiConfig          ← Springdoc global config\n        ├── WebConfig              ← Global CORS + request logging. Never use @CrossOrigin on controllers.\n        └── TracingConfig          ← MDC traceId on every log line. Essential for prod debugging.\n```\n\n---\n\n## [module-name]/ Structure\n\nOne folder per bounded context. Name reflects the domain, not the tech.\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/            ← HTTP endpoints only. No business logic. Delegates to service.\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/           ← [Action][Resource]Request\n    │       └── response/          ← [Resource]Response. Flat, serializable, no JPA proxies.\n    │\n    ├── domain/                    ← Pure business logic. No Spring, no JPA, no infrastructure.\n    │                                Entities have behavior (confirm(), cancel()) not just getters.\n    │\n    ├── service/                   ← Orchestrates domain + repositories + ACL + events. @Service beans.\n    │                                One service per capability group. Methods = use cases not CRUD.\n    │\n    ├── repository/                ← @Repository interfaces only. No other module imports these.\n    │\n    ├── mapper/                    ← Domain ↔ DTO translation only. Use MapStruct.\n    │\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── published/             ← Events this module fires. Past tense. Primitives/IDs only.\n    │   └── listener/              ← Events this module reacts to. No logic — delegate to service.\n    │\n    ├── statemachine/              ← OPTIONAL. Only if 4+ states with conditional transition guards.\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineConfig\n    │   ├── [Resource]State\n    │   ├── [Resource]Event\n    │   ├── [Resource]StateMachineService\n    │   ├── guard/\n    │   └── action/\n    │\n    └── exception/                 ← OPTIONAL. Module-specific exceptions only.\n```\n\n---\n\n## admin/ and app/\n\n```\nadmin/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/admin/\n    ├── api/controller/            ← Admin-only endpoints (@PreAuthorize ROLE_ADMIN)\n    └── service/                   ← Aggregates reads across modules. Delegates writes to module APIs.\n\napp/\n└── src/main/java/com/[org]/\n    ├── [ProjectName]Application   ← @SpringBootApplication. One class, nothing else.\n    └── resources/\n        ├── application.yml\n        ├── application-local.yml\n        ├── application-prod.yml\n        └── db/migration/          ← Flyway migrations. Global version sequence. Never modify existing files.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns — Never Do These\n\n| Anti-Pattern | Why It's Wrong | Correct Approach |\n|---|---|---|\n| Module A imports `@Service` from Module B | Direct coupling — changes in B break A at compile time | Module A calls Module B's REST endpoint or listens to its Spring Event |\n| Module A imports `@Entity` or `@Repository` from Module B | Hidden DB coupling — schema changes in B break A silently | Module A calls Module B's API to get the data it needs |\n| Putting Eventbrite/Razorpay HTTP calls inside a module's service | Scattered integration — if the API changes, you hunt across modules | All external calls go through `shared/[system-acl]/service/` facades only |\n| Sharing a database table between two modules | Breaks module autonomy — both modules must coordinate schema changes | Each module owns its own tables; sync via events or API calls |\n| Putting business logic in a `@RestController` | Untestable and leaks domain logic into the HTTP layer | Controller delegates immediately to service; service owns all logic |\n| Creating an `[Entity]Service` that just wraps CRUD | Anemic domain model — no real encapsulation | Put behavior on the entity itself; service orchestrates multi-step use cases |\n| Publishing a Spring Event that contains a JPA `@Entity` | Entity may be a lazy-loaded proxy; causes serialization failures | Events carry only primitive values, IDs, or immutable value objects |\n| Adding a new enum to `shared/common/enums/` for a single-module concept | Pollutes shared space with module-specific concerns | Single-module enums stay inside that module's `domain/` package |\n| Writing business logic in `admin/` | Admin owns no domain; it becomes a second copy of truth | Admin delegates writes to the owning module's REST API; only reads and aggregates |\n| Admin calling a module's `@Service` for writes | Bypasses the module's own validation, events, and business rules | Admin must call the owning module's REST API for all write operations |\n| Mapping fields inside a `@Service` or `@RestController` | Pollutes orchestration/HTTP layers with translation logic; hard to test | All domain ↔ DTO mapping goes in `mapper/[Resource]Mapper` only |\n| Using `@CrossOrigin` on individual controllers | Scattered CORS config — inconsistent across modules | Define CORS once in `shared/config/WebConfig` |\n\n---\n\n## Event Naming Conventions\n\n```\nPublished events  →  Past tense + \"Event\" suffix\n                     [WhatHappened]Event\n                     e.g. BookingConfirmedEvent\n                          SeatReleasedEvent\n                          UserRegisteredEvent\n                          PaymentFailedEvent\n\nListeners         →  [TriggerEvent]Listener\n                     e.g. PaymentFailedListener (inside booking-inventory module)\n                          BookingConfirmedListener (inside engagement module)\n                          CartAssembledListener (inside payments module)\n\nEvent payload     →  Only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects.\n                     NEVER include @Entity objects in events.\n                     NEVER include mutable collections.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Module pom.xml Dependency Rules\n\n```xml\n<!-- Every module pom.xml follows this exact pattern -->\n<dependencies>\n\n  <!-- 1. Always: shared internal library -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>shared</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 2. Only upstream modules (modules this one reads from) -->\n  <!-- Example: booking-inventory reads from discovery-catalog -->\n  <dependency>\n    <groupId>com.[org]</groupId>\n    <artifactId>[upstream-module]</artifactId>\n  </dependency>\n\n  <!-- 3. NEVER depend on a peer or downstream module -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on admin/ -->\n  <!-- NEVER depend on app/ -->\n\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n---\n\n## Flyway Migration Naming\n\n```\nV[auto-increment]__[verb]_[table_name].sql\n\nExamples:\n"
  }
}
```

{

All cross-module data comes via REST or Spring Events.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 3 — Architecture & File Structure\n\n### Architecture Pattern\n**Modular Monolith (fixed).** `engagement` is a leaf module — it depends on `shared/`, `payments-ticketing`, and `identity` (reads via REST). No module below it in the dependency order; no shared reader interface needs to be published FROM `engagement`.\n\n### Module Dependency Position\n```\nshared/ → ... → payments-ticketing → promotions → engagement → admin/ → app/\n```\n`engagement` sits at the top of the user-facing stack. It depends on `payments-ticketing` (booking verification) and `identity` (user display name) via REST only. It never provides in-process interfaces to admin or app beyond its REST API.\n\n---\n\n### `engagement/` File Layout\n\n```\nengagement/\n└── src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/\n    ├── api/\n    │   ├── controller/\n    │   │   ├── ReviewController.java\n    │   │   │     POST   /api/v1/engagement/reviews\n    │   │   │     GET    /api/v1/engagement/reviews/me\n    │   │   │     GET    /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/{eventId}\n    │   │   │     GET    /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/{eventId}/summary\n    │   │   └── AdminModerationController.java\n    │   │         GET    /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews               (ROLE_ADMIN)\n    │   │         PUT    /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews/{id}/moderate (ROLE_ADMIN)\n    │   └── dto/\n    │       ├── request/\n    │       │   ├── ReviewSubmitRequest.java       (eventId, rating, title, body)\n    │       │   └── ModerationDecisionRequest.java (decision, reason)\n    │       └── response/\n    │           ├── ReviewResponse.java            (id, eventId, rating, title, body, status, publishedAt, reviewerDisplayName)\n    │           ├── ReviewSummaryResponse.java     (averageRating, totalReviews, distribution)\n    │           └── AdminReviewResponse.java       (id, eventId, userId, rating, title, body, status, submittedAt, autoModerationAttempts, lastAutoDecision)\n    ├── domain/\n    │   ├── Review.java                            (@Entity — extends BaseEntity)\n    │   ├── ReviewEligibility.java                 (@Entity — extends BaseEntity)\n    │   ├── ModerationRecord.java                  (@Entity — extends BaseEntity)\n    │   └── enums/\n    │       ├── ReviewStatus.java\n    │       ├── ReviewEligibilityStatus.java\n    │       ├── AttendanceVerificationStatus.java\n    │       ├── ModerationMethod.java\n    │       └── ModerationDecision.java\n    ├── service/\n    │   ├── ReviewService.java                     (submission orchestration, eligibility guard, publish transition)\n    │   ├── AttendanceVerificationService.java     (internal booking REST check + EbAttendeeService cross-check)\n    │   ├── ModerationService.java                 (auto-mod dispatch, retry scheduler, manual override)\n    │   └── ReviewRatingSummaryService.java        (compute aggregate + Redis cache-aside)\n    ├── repository/\n    │   ├── ReviewRepository.java                  (JpaRepository<Review, Long>)\n    │   ├── ReviewEligibilityRepository.java       (JpaRepository<ReviewEligibility, Long>)\n    │   └── ModerationRecordRepository.java        (JpaRepository<ModerationRecord, Long>)\n    ├── mapper/\n    │   └── ReviewMapper.java                      (MapStruct — Review ↔ ReviewResponse; ModerationRecord ↔ AdminReviewResponse)\n    ├── event/\n    │   ├── listener/\n    │   │   ├── BookingConfirmedEventListener.java (@TransactionalEventListener AFTER_COMMIT → unlock eligibility)\n    │   │   └── BookingCancelledEventListener.java (@TransactionalEventListener AFTER_COMMIT → revoke eligibility)\n    │   └── published/\n    │       └── ReviewPublishedEvent.java          (record: Long reviewId, Long eventId, int rating)\n    └── exception/\n        ├── ReviewNotEligibleException.java        (extends BusinessRuleException — 403)\n        ├── ReviewAlreadySubmittedException.java   (extends BusinessRuleException — 409)\n        └── ReviewAlreadyModeratedException.java   (extends BusinessRuleException — 409)\n```\n\n---\n\n### `shared/` Additions Required\n\n| File | Location | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `OpenAiModerationService.java` | `shared/openai/service/` | ACL wrapper for `POST https://api.openai.com/v1/moderations`; returns `ModerationResult(flagged, categories, categoryScores)` |\n| `ReviewPublishedEvent.java` | `shared/common/event/published/` | Published by `engagement`; consumed by admin analytics in future |\n\n> **Note:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` and `BookingCancelledEvent` already exist in `shared/common/event/published/` from FR5/FR6. No changes needed.\n\n---\n\n### Inter-Module REST Calls (Outbound from `engagement`)\n\n| Target Module | Endpoint | Caller Service | Timeout | Failure Handling |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `payments-ticketing` | `GET /api/v1/internal/bookings/by-user-event?userId={}&eventId={}` | `AttendanceVerificationService` | 2s | Block submission on timeout — booking check is mandatory |\n| `identity` | `GET /api/v1/internal/users/{userId}/display-name` | `ReviewService` (read path) | 500ms | Return `\"Anonymous\"` on timeout — never fail the read request |\n| `discovery-catalog` | `GET /api/v1/catalog/events/{eventId}/eb-metadata` | `AttendanceVerificationService` | 1s | Skip EB check if timeout → proceed with `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` |\n\n---\n\n### Spring Events Consumed\n\n| Event (in `shared/`) | Phase | Handler | Action |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` | `AFTER_COMMIT` | `BookingConfirmedEventListener` | Upsert `ReviewEligibility(UNLOCKED)` for `(userId, eventId)` |\n| `BookingCancelledEvent` | `AFTER_COMMIT` | `BookingCancelledEventListener` | Transition `ReviewEligibility → REVOKED` for cancelled booking |\n\n### Spring Events Published\n\n| Event | Trigger | Consumers |\n|---|---|---|\n| `ReviewPublishedEvent(reviewId, eventId, rating)` | Review transitions to `PUBLISHED` | (prepared for extension — no active consumer in FR8) |\n\n---\n\n### Redis Usage\n\n| Key Pattern | TTL | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}` | 5 minutes | Cached `ReviewRatingSummary` (avg rating, distribution) |\n\nInvalidated on `ReviewPublishedEvent` by `ReviewRatingSummaryService`.\n\n---\n\n### External ACL Facades Used\n\n| Facade | Location | Purpose in FR8 |\n|---|---|---|\n| `EbAttendeeService` | `shared/eventbrite/service/` | `getAttendeesByEvent(orgToken, ebEventId)` — cross-check attendance |\n| `OpenAiModerationService` | `shared/openai/service/` | `moderate(text)` — auto-moderation of review content |\n\n---\n\n### Hard Rules Applied\n\n| Rule | Application |\n|---|---|\n| No cross-module `@Service` / `@Repository` import | All cross-module reads via REST in `AttendanceVerificationService` and `ReviewService` |\n| All external calls via `shared/` ACL only | `EbAttendeeService` and `OpenAiModerationService` — never direct HTTP |\n| One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/` | `engagement` exceptions extend `BusinessRuleException` from `shared/`; no `@ControllerAdvice` in module |\n| MapStruct in `mapper/` only | `ReviewMapper` handles all Review ↔ DTO conversions |\n| `admin/` owns no domain | `AdminModerationController` lives in `engagement/api/controller/` — admin calls this endpoint, but does NOT import the controller class |\n| Spring Events AFTER_COMMIT | Both event listeners use `@TransactionalEventListener(phase = AFTER_COMMIT)` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 4 — DB Schema\n\n> **Migration baseline:** Last applied = V37. FR8 uses V38–V40.  \n> **Convention:** All FKs to other modules' tables are intentionally omitted — cross-module constraints are enforced at the service layer only.\n\n---\n\n### V38 — `create_review_eligibility_table.sql`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE review_eligibility (\n    id                  BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n    user_id             BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    event_id            BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    slot_id             BIGINT,\n    booking_id          BIGINT,\n    status              VARCHAR(30)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'UNLOCKED',\n    eligible_until      TIMESTAMP,\n    created_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n\n    CONSTRAINT uq_review_eligibility_user_event UNIQUE (user_id, event_id),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_review_eligibility_status\n        CHECK (status IN ('UNLOCKED', 'REVOKED', 'EXPIRED'))\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_review_eligibility_status  ON review_eligibility (status);\nCREATE INDEX idx_review_eligibility_booking ON review_eligibility (booking_id);\nCREATE INDEX idx_review_eligibility_user    ON review_eligibility (user_id);\n```\n\n---\n\n### V39 — `create_reviews_table.sql`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE reviews (\n    id                              BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n    user_id                         BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    event_id                        BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    rating                          SMALLINT        NOT NULL,\n    title                           VARCHAR(100)    NOT NULL,\n    body                            VARCHAR(2000)   NOT NULL,\n    status                          VARCHAR(30)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED',\n    attendance_verification_status  VARCHAR(40)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED',\n    rejection_reason                VARCHAR(255),\n    published_at                    TIMESTAMP,\n    submitted_at                    TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    created_at                      TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at                      TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n\n    CONSTRAINT uq_reviews_user_event UNIQUE (user_id, event_id),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_rating\n        CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_status\n        CHECK (status IN ('SUBMITTED', 'PENDING_MODERATION', 'APPROVED', 'PUBLISHED', 'REJECTED')),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_attendance_status\n        CHECK (attendance_verification_status IN (\n            'EB_VERIFIED', 'ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED', 'ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE'\n        ))\n);\n\n-- Listing reviews for an event (public read path)\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_event_status   ON reviews (event_id, status);\n-- User's own reviews\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_user_id        ON reviews (user_id);\n-- Admin moderation queue\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_status         ON reviews (status);\n-- Rating aggregation\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_event_rating   ON reviews (event_id, rating) WHERE status = 'PUBLISHED';\n```\n\n---\n\n### V40 — `create_moderation_records_table.sql`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE moderation_records (\n    id                  BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n    review_id           BIGINT          NOT NULL\n                            REFERENCES reviews(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    method              VARCHAR(20)     NOT NULL,\n    input_text          TEXT            NOT NULL,\n    decision            VARCHAR(20)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',\n    flags               VARCHAR(500),\n    scores_json         TEXT,\n    moderator_id        BIGINT,\n    reason              VARCHAR(500),\n    auto_retry_count    INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    retry_after         TIMESTAMP,\n    decided_at          TIMESTAMP,\n    created_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n\n    CONSTRAINT chk_moderation_method\n        CHECK (method IN ('AUTO', 'MANUAL')),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_moderation_decision\n        CHECK (decision IN ('APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'PENDING'))\n);\n\n-- Join from review to its moderation records\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_review_id       ON moderation_records (review_id);\n-- Retry scheduler: find records needing retry\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_retry_after     ON moderation_records (retry_after)\n    WHERE decision = 'PENDING' AND retry_after IS NOT NULL;\n-- Admin: filter by method + decision\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_method_decision ON moderation_records (method, decision);\n```\n\n---\n\n### Index Rationale Summary\n\n| Index | Query served |\n|---|---|\n| `uq_review_eligibility_user_event` | Idempotent eligibility upsert; submission guard |\n| `uq_reviews_user_event` | Duplicate review prevention |\n| `idx_reviews_event_status` | `GET /reviews/events/{eventId}` — PUBLISHED filter |\n| `idx_reviews_event_rating WHERE status='PUBLISHED'` | Rating summary aggregate |\n| `idx_reviews_user_id` | `GET /reviews/me` |\n| `idx_moderation_retry_after WHERE PENDING` | Retry scheduler job — partial index keeps scan small |\n| `idx_moderation_review_id` | `JOIN reviews ↔ moderation_records` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 5 — API Design\n\n> **Base path:** `/api/v1`  \n> **Auth:** JWT Bearer token via `JwtAuthenticationFilter`.  \n> **Error format:** Global `ApiError { errorCode, message, timestamp, path }` from `shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler`.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Submit a Review\n\n```\nPOST /api/v1/engagement/reviews\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_USER)\nContent-Type: application/json\n```\n\n**Request Body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"eventId\": 1001,\n  \"rating\": 4,\n  \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n  \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal. Production quality was top-notch.\"\n}\n```\n\n**Validations:**\n- `eventId`: not null\n- `rating`: not null, min 1, max 5\n- `title`: not blank, max 100 chars\n- `body`: not blank, max 2000 chars\n\n**Response — 201 Created:**\n```json\n{\n  \"reviewId\": 55,\n  \"status\": \"PENDING_MODERATION\",\n  \"message\": \"Your review has been submitted and is under moderation.\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Responses:**\n\n| HTTP | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 400 | `INVALID_RATING` | `rating` outside 1–5 |\n| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Blank title/body or missing fields |\n| 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | No UNLOCKED eligibility for (userId, eventId) |\n| 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | Booking not CONFIRMED (booking REST check fails) |\n| 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` | Review already exists for (userId, eventId) — any status |\n\n---\n\n### 2. List Published Reviews for an Event\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/{eventId}?page=0&size=20&sort=publishedAt,desc\nAuthorization: (none — public)\n```\n\n**Path Params:** `eventId` (Long)  \n**Query Params:** `page` (default 0), `size` (default 20, max 100), `sort` (default `publishedAt,desc`)\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 55,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"rating\": 4,\n      \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n      \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal...\",\n      \"publishedAt\": \"2026-03-05T18:00:00Z\",\n      \"reviewerDisplayName\": \"Jane D.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 142,\n  \"totalPages\": 8\n}\n```\n\n"
  }
}
```

                  BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    event_id                        BIGINT          NOT NULL,\n    rating                          SMALLINT        NOT NULL,\n    title                           VARCHAR(100)    NOT NULL,\n    body                            VARCHAR(2000)   NOT NULL,\n    status                          VARCHAR(30)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'SUBMITTED',\n    attendance_verification_status  VARCHAR(40)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED',\n    rejection_reason                VARCHAR(255),\n    published_at                    TIMESTAMP,\n    submitted_at                    TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    created_at                      TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at                      TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n\n    CONSTRAINT uq_reviews_user_event UNIQUE (user_id, event_id),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_rating\n        CHECK (rating >= 1 AND rating <= 5),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_status\n        CHECK (status IN ('SUBMITTED', 'PENDING_MODERATION', 'APPROVED', 'PUBLISHED', 'REJECTED')),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_reviews_attendance_status\n        CHECK (attendance_verification_status IN (\n            'EB_VERIFIED', 'ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED', 'ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE'\n        ))\n);\n\n-- Listing reviews for an event (public read path)\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_event_status   ON reviews (event_id, status);\n-- User's own reviews\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_user_id        ON reviews (user_id);\n-- Admin moderation queue\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_status         ON reviews (status);\n-- Rating aggregation\nCREATE INDEX idx_reviews_event_rating   ON reviews (event_id, rating) WHERE status = 'PUBLISHED';\n```\n\n---\n\n### V40 — `create_moderation_records_table.sql`\n\n```sql\nCREATE TABLE moderation_records (\n    id                  BIGSERIAL       PRIMARY KEY,\n    review_id           BIGINT          NOT NULL\n                            REFERENCES reviews(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n    method              VARCHAR(20)     NOT NULL,\n    input_text          TEXT            NOT NULL,\n    decision            VARCHAR(20)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',\n    flags               VARCHAR(500),\n    scores_json         TEXT,\n    moderator_id        BIGINT,\n    reason              VARCHAR(500),\n    auto_retry_count    INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n    retry_after         TIMESTAMP,\n    decided_at          TIMESTAMP,\n    created_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n    updated_at          TIMESTAMP       NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),\n\n    CONSTRAINT chk_moderation_method\n        CHECK (method IN ('AUTO', 'MANUAL')),\n    CONSTRAINT chk_moderation_decision\n        CHECK (decision IN ('APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'PENDING'))\n);\n\n-- Join from review to its moderation records\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_review_id       ON moderation_records (review_id);\n-- Retry scheduler: find records needing retry\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_retry_after     ON moderation_records (retry_after)\n    WHERE decision = 'PENDING' AND retry_after IS NOT NULL;\n-- Admin: filter by method + decision\nCREATE INDEX idx_moderation_method_decision ON moderation_records (method, decision);\n```\n\n---\n\n### Index Rationale Summary\n\n| Index | Query served |\n|---|---|\n| `uq_review_eligibility_user_event` | Idempotent eligibility upsert; submission guard |\n| `uq_reviews_user_event` | Duplicate review prevention |\n| `idx_reviews_event_status` | `GET /reviews/events/{eventId}` — PUBLISHED filter |\n| `idx_reviews_event_rating WHERE status='PUBLISHED'` | Rating summary aggregate |\n| `idx_reviews_user_id` | `GET /reviews/me` |\n| `idx_moderation_retry_after WHERE PENDING` | Retry scheduler job — partial index keeps scan small |\n| `idx_moderation_review_id` | `JOIN reviews ↔ moderation_records` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 5 — API Design\n\n> **Base path:** `/api/v1`  \n> **Auth:** JWT Bearer token via `JwtAuthenticationFilter`.  \n> **Error format:** Global `ApiError { errorCode, message, timestamp, path }` from `shared/common/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler`.\n\n---\n\n### 1. Submit a Review\n\n```\nPOST /api/v1/engagement/reviews\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_USER)\nContent-Type: application/json\n```\n\n**Request Body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"eventId\": 1001,\n  \"rating\": 4,\n  \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n  \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal. Production quality was top-notch.\"\n}\n```\n\n**Validations:**\n- `eventId`: not null\n- `rating`: not null, min 1, max 5\n- `title`: not blank, max 100 chars\n- `body`: not blank, max 2000 chars\n\n**Response — 201 Created:**\n```json\n{\n  \"reviewId\": 55,\n  \"status\": \"PENDING_MODERATION\",\n  \"message\": \"Your review has been submitted and is under moderation.\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Responses:**\n\n| HTTP | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 400 | `INVALID_RATING` | `rating` outside 1–5 |\n| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Blank title/body or missing fields |\n| 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | No UNLOCKED eligibility for (userId, eventId) |\n| 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | Booking not CONFIRMED (booking REST check fails) |\n| 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` | Review already exists for (userId, eventId) — any status |\n\n---\n\n### 2. List Published Reviews for an Event\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/{eventId}?page=0&size=20&sort=publishedAt,desc\nAuthorization: (none — public)\n```\n\n**Path Params:** `eventId` (Long)  \n**Query Params:** `page` (default 0), `size` (default 20, max 100), `sort` (default `publishedAt,desc`)\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 55,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"rating\": 4,\n      \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n      \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal...\",\n      \"publishedAt\": \"2026-03-05T18:00:00Z\",\n      \"reviewerDisplayName\": \"Jane D.\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 142,\n  \"totalPages\": 8\n}\n```\n\n"
  }
}
```

FR5 (Stripe payments) and FR6 (Stripe refunds) operate independently of `eventbrite_event_id`.\n"

## Entry 39
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T22:26:35.780Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:26:35.780Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_aUPCQNEyHrYGYxM2f0PEMw1F",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 26c01f\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2971\nOutput:\n# Eventbrite Integration Strategy\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026 — FR1–FR7 implemented: Stripe owns all payments/refunds; EB Checkout Widget removed; promotions scaffolded; pre-FR7 gaps fixed  \n**Status:** FR1–FR7 Implemented — FR8+ Pending\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEventbrite serves as a **passive event listing sync layer** for the multi-city event booking platform. The architecture follows an **internal-first approach**: your database is the source of truth. **Stripe owns all payments and refunds**. Eventbrite is pushed to after internal validation for listing purposes only.\n\n**Core Principle:** Eventbrite does not create orders or users. It only provides event listing infrastructure, ticket class capacity tracking, and organizer-side webhooks. All payments are processed via Stripe. The EB Checkout Widget is removed and must not be referenced.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Eventbrite ACL Facades (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n### Available Facades\n\n| Facade | Responsibilities | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| **EbEventSyncService** | Create, update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbVenueService** | Create and update venues on Eventbrite; list venues by org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbScheduleService** | Create recurring event schedules and occurrences | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbTicketService** | Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory tiers; check availability | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbCapacityService** | Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; manage seat maps | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbOrderService** | Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbAttendeeService** | Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event or org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbDiscountSyncService** | Create, update, delete discount codes on Eventbrite; sync with internal promotions | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbRefundService** | Read refund request status on orders; filter orders by refund status | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbWebhookService** | Register and manage org-scoped webhooks for event/order state changes | ✅ Designed |\n\n**Hard Rule:** No module ever calls Eventbrite directly. All API calls go through these facades in `shared/`.\n\n---\n\n## 2. The Spine: `eventbrite_event_id`\n\nThe `eventbrite_event_id` is the bridge between your internal DB and Eventbrite. Every downstream flow depends on it:\n\n- **FR2 (Scheduling)** — Create show slot → Store `eventbrite_event_id`\n- **FR4 (Seats)** — Validate availability against Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n- **FR5 (Payment)** — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs valid `event_id` to render\n- **FR6 (Cancellation)** — Refund APIs reference `event_id` + `order_id`\n- **FR7 (Discounts)** — Discount codes scoped to `event_id`\n- **FR8 (Reviews)** — Attendance verification uses `event_id` + attendee email\n\n**Without FR2 sync:** If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Integration Flows — Planned vs. Constraints\n\n### FR1: City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser selects city \n  ↓\nApp queries internal venues by city_id\n  ↓\nGET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nMerge internal event_catalog with Eventbrite org events\n  ↓\nUser sees unified event list\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list org venues for cross-reference\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — pull org's published events\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — fetch individual event details for catalog refresh\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ Public event search (`GET /events/search/`) is **deprecated** on Eventbrite. Event discovery is limited to your org's events only. No city-wide public search available.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Limited to org events\n\n---\n\n### FR2: Show Scheduling → Time Slot Configuration → Conflict Validation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Phase 1: Venue Registration\n```\nAdmin registers venue in your app\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_venue_id in internal DB\n```\n\n#### Phase 2: Show Slot Creation (Core Sync)\n```\nAdmin creates show slot with venue + date + time\n  ↓\nInternal validation (duplicate check, turnaround gaps)\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/events/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nEventbrite returns event_id\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_event_id in show_slots table\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/ [Eventbrite] — create ticket tiers\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/publish/ [Eventbrite] — make event live\n```\n\n#### Phase 3: Recurring Shows\n```\nAdmin creates recurring series (e.g., every Saturday)\n  ↓\nInternal DB generates individual show_slot records\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/schedules/ [Eventbrite] — create recurring occurrences\n```\n\n#### Phase 4: Status Sync\n```\nAdmin updates/deletes slot\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — sync changes\n  OR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/cancel/ [Eventbrite] — cancel event\n```\n\n#### Phase 5: Conflict Validation (Background Job)\n```\nPeriodic job queries internal show_slots\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — verify eventbrite_event_id still exists\n  ↓\nFlags orphaned records for admin review\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create venue\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list venues for conflict check\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tiers\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — publish event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/schedules/` — create recurring occurrences\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update event details\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — verify event exists\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **Turnaround policy enforcement** — Eventbrite has no conflict validation API. Your app must enforce time gaps between shows internally. Conflict validation is 100% internal DB logic.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Turnaround gaps are internal logic only\n\n---\n\n### FR3: User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser registers (email + password + name)\n  ↓\nInternal users table created\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite call — no user creation API exists]\n  ↓\nUser fills profile (phone, city, avatar)\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite sync at this stage]\n  ↓\nUser selects preferences (genres, cities, price ranges)\n  ↓\nPreferences stored in users_preferences table\n  ↓\n[Post-purchase: user linked to Eventbrite via order email]\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- No Eventbrite call for this flow we will use the atendee sync that happens in later flows\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No user creation API on Eventbrite.** User identity is 100% internal. The link between your app user and Eventbrite is established post-purchase via the order email. No user profile sync is possible.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Zero Eventbrite integration at registration time\n\n---\n\n### FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser picks seats from internal SeatMap\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nValidate: ticket count still > 0?\n  ↓\nApply internal pricing tiers (VIP, Premium, General)\n  ↓\nPOST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/ [Eventbrite] — validate pricing\n  ↓\nSoft lock seat via Redis (TTL = cart session)\n  ↓\nREST call to promotions module — validate coupon eligibility\n  ↓\nCartAssembledEvent published\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining ticket count\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — verify tier capacity\n- `POST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/` — validate price against Eventbrite fees\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/ticket_groups/` — check ticket bundles\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal using Redis + Spring State Machine. Eventbrite is consulted only once — for availability count.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Seat locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n### FR5: Payment via Stripe → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n\n**Implementation: Stripe replaces Eventbrite Checkout Widget entirely.**  \nAll payment processing, order creation, and refunds are handled by Stripe. Eventbrite retains only a passive event listing role.\n\n#### Step 1: Stripe PaymentIntent Creation\n```\nCartAssembledEvent received by payments-ticketing\n  ↓\nBackend calculates discounted total (after coupon, after group discount) in paise\n  ↓\nStripePaymentService.createPaymentIntent() → POST /v1/payment_intents\n  ↓\nclient_secret returned to frontend ONLY (never stored)\n  ↓\nstripe_payment_intent_id stored in payments table (status=PENDING)\n```\n\n#### Step 2: User Pays via Stripe Payment Element (Frontend)\n```\nFrontend renders Stripe Payment Element with client_secret\n  ↓\nUser enters card details → stripe.confirmPayment()\n  ↓\nStripe redirects to return_url with payment_intent as query param\n  ↓\nFrontend sends payment_intent_id to POST /api/payments/confirm-order\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Backend Confirms Payment\n```\nStripePaymentService.retrievePaymentIntent(id) → GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}\n  ↓\nVerify status=succeeded AND amount_received==amount\n  ↓\nIdempotency check: skip if booking already CONFIRMED for this payment_intent_id\n  ↓\nInternal booking record created (bookings table, status=CONFIRMED)\n  ↓\nbooking_items created from cart snapshot\n  ↓\nSeat locks: HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED\n  ↓\nstripe_payment_intent_id + stripe_charge_id (latest_charge) saved\n  ↓\nBookingConfirmedEvent published (bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)\n  ↓\nengagement → review eligibility unlocked\n  → identity → order history updated\n  → promotions → CouponRedemption recorded (via bookingId)\n```\n\n#### Step 4: E-Ticket Generation\n```\nETicket record created in e_tickets table\n  ↓\nQR code generated internally from booking_reference + booking_item_id (NO EB barcode)\n  ↓\nPDF e-ticket stored in object storage\n  ↓\nDownload link returned; confirmation email sent\n```\n\n#### Step 5: Stripe Webhooks (Safety Net)\n```\npayment_intent.succeeded   → idempotent booking confirm (skip if already CONFIRMED)\npayment_intent.payment_failed → PaymentFailedEvent → seat locks released\npayment_intent.canceled    → same as payment_intent.payment_failed\nrefund.updated             → update refunds.status\nrefund.failed              → alert ops + notify user\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:** None for payment, order, or e-ticket generation.  \nEB is used passively: organizer creates event on EB for listing; `event.updated` / `event.unpublished` webhooks trigger bulk cancellation.\n\n**Constraints Resolved:** ✅ EB Checkout Widget removed. Orders created internally in `bookings` table on Stripe confirmation. No EB order creation API needed.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Implemented (Stripe sandbox `sk_test_...`)\n\n---\n\n### FR6: Cancellation Request → Refund Policy Engine → Wallet Credit\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: User Requests Cancellation\n```\nUser submits cancellation request in your app\n  ↓\nInternal refund_policy calculates refund % (time-based)\n  ↓\nCheck if cancellation permitted\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Stripe Refund Issued\n```\nBackend retrieves stripe_payment_intent_id from bookings table\n  ↓\nStripeRefundService.createRefund(paymentIntentId, amount, reason) → POST /v1/refunds\n  → Full refund: payment_intent=pi_..., reason=requested_by_customer\n  → Partial refund: payment_intent=pi_..., amount=<paise>, reason=requested_by_customer\n  ↓\n"
  }
}
```
