    "output": "Chunk ID: 26c01f\nWall time: 0.0505 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2971\nOutput:\n# Eventbrite Integration Strategy\n\n**Owner:** Lalit  \n**Domain:** EntertainmentTech  \n**Last Updated:** March 2026 — FR1–FR7 implemented: Stripe owns all payments/refunds; EB Checkout Widget removed; promotions scaffolded; pre-FR7 gaps fixed  \n**Status:** FR1–FR7 Implemented — FR8+ Pending\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nEventbrite serves as a **passive event listing sync layer** for the multi-city event booking platform. The architecture follows an **internal-first approach**: your database is the source of truth. **Stripe owns all payments and refunds**. Eventbrite is pushed to after internal validation for listing purposes only.\n\n**Core Principle:** Eventbrite does not create orders or users. It only provides event listing infrastructure, ticket class capacity tracking, and organizer-side webhooks. All payments are processed via Stripe. The EB Checkout Widget is removed and must not be referenced.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Eventbrite ACL Facades (`shared/eventbrite/service/`)\n\n### Available Facades\n\n| Facade | Responsibilities | Status |\n|---|---|---|\n| **EbEventSyncService** | Create, update, publish, unpublish, cancel, delete events; pull org event catalog; verify sync integrity | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbVenueService** | Create and update venues on Eventbrite; list venues by org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbScheduleService** | Create recurring event schedules and occurrences | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbTicketService** | Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory tiers; check availability | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbCapacityService** | Retrieve and update event capacity tiers; manage seat maps | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbOrderService** | Retrieve order details post-checkout; list orders by event/org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbAttendeeService** | Retrieve attendee records; list attendees by event or org | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbDiscountSyncService** | Create, update, delete discount codes on Eventbrite; sync with internal promotions | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbRefundService** | Read refund request status on orders; filter orders by refund status | ✅ Designed |\n| **EbWebhookService** | Register and manage org-scoped webhooks for event/order state changes | ✅ Designed |\n\n**Hard Rule:** No module ever calls Eventbrite directly. All API calls go through these facades in `shared/`.\n\n---\n\n## 2. The Spine: `eventbrite_event_id`\n\nThe `eventbrite_event_id` is the bridge between your internal DB and Eventbrite. Every downstream flow depends on it:\n\n- **FR2 (Scheduling)** — Create show slot → Store `eventbrite_event_id`\n- **FR4 (Seats)** — Validate availability against Eventbrite `ticket_classes` for that `event_id`\n- **FR5 (Payment)** — Eventbrite Checkout Widget needs valid `event_id` to render\n- **FR6 (Cancellation)** — Refund APIs reference `event_id` + `order_id`\n- **FR7 (Discounts)** — Discount codes scoped to `event_id`\n- **FR8 (Reviews)** — Attendance verification uses `event_id` + attendee email\n\n**Without FR2 sync:** If `eventbrite_event_id` is null or orphaned, the entire chain breaks.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Integration Flows — Planned vs. Constraints\n\n### FR1: City Selection → Venue Discovery → Event Listing\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser selects city \n  ↓\nApp queries internal venues by city_id\n  ↓\nGET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nMerge internal event_catalog with Eventbrite org events\n  ↓\nUser sees unified event list\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list org venues for cross-reference\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — pull org's published events\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — fetch individual event details for catalog refresh\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ Public event search (`GET /events/search/`) is **deprecated** on Eventbrite. Event discovery is limited to your org's events only. No city-wide public search available.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Limited to org events\n\n---\n\n### FR2: Show Scheduling → Time Slot Configuration → Conflict Validation\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Phase 1: Venue Registration\n```\nAdmin registers venue in your app\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_venue_id in internal DB\n```\n\n#### Phase 2: Show Slot Creation (Core Sync)\n```\nAdmin creates show slot with venue + date + time\n  ↓\nInternal validation (duplicate check, turnaround gaps)\n  ↓\nPOST /organizations/{org_id}/events/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nEventbrite returns event_id\n  ↓\nStore eventbrite_event_id in show_slots table\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/ [Eventbrite] — create ticket tiers\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/publish/ [Eventbrite] — make event live\n```\n\n#### Phase 3: Recurring Shows\n```\nAdmin creates recurring series (e.g., every Saturday)\n  ↓\nInternal DB generates individual show_slot records\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/schedules/ [Eventbrite] — create recurring occurrences\n```\n\n#### Phase 4: Status Sync\n```\nAdmin updates/deletes slot\n  ↓\nPOST /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — sync changes\n  OR\nPOST /events/{event_id}/cancel/ [Eventbrite] — cancel event\n```\n\n#### Phase 5: Conflict Validation (Background Job)\n```\nPeriodic job queries internal show_slots\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ [Eventbrite] — verify eventbrite_event_id still exists\n  ↓\nFlags orphaned records for admin review\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create venue\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — list venues for conflict check\n- `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tiers\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — publish event\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/schedules/` — create recurring occurrences\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update event details\n- `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/` — verify event exists\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **Turnaround policy enforcement** — Eventbrite has no conflict validation API. Your app must enforce time gaps between shows internally. Conflict validation is 100% internal DB logic.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Turnaround gaps are internal logic only\n\n---\n\n### FR3: User Registration → Profile Setup → Preference Onboarding\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser registers (email + password + name)\n  ↓\nInternal users table created\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite call — no user creation API exists]\n  ↓\nUser fills profile (phone, city, avatar)\n  ↓\n[No Eventbrite sync at this stage]\n  ↓\nUser selects preferences (genres, cities, price ranges)\n  ↓\nPreferences stored in users_preferences table\n  ↓\n[Post-purchase: user linked to Eventbrite via order email]\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- No Eventbrite call for this flow we will use the atendee sync that happens in later flows\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No user creation API on Eventbrite.** User identity is 100% internal. The link between your app user and Eventbrite is established post-purchase via the order email. No user profile sync is possible.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Zero Eventbrite integration at registration time\n\n---\n\n### FR4: Seat Selection → Pricing Tier Validation → Cart Assembly\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n```\nUser picks seats from internal SeatMap\n  ↓\nGET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/ [Eventbrite]\n  ↓\nValidate: ticket count still > 0?\n  ↓\nApply internal pricing tiers (VIP, Premium, General)\n  ↓\nPOST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/ [Eventbrite] — validate pricing\n  ↓\nSoft lock seat via Redis (TTL = cart session)\n  ↓\nREST call to promotions module — validate coupon eligibility\n  ↓\nCartAssembledEvent published\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:**\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/for_sale/` — remaining ticket count\n- `GET /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — verify tier capacity\n- `POST /pricing/calculate_price_for_item/` — validate price against Eventbrite fees\n- `GET /organizations/{org_id}/ticket_groups/` — check ticket bundles\n\n**Constraints:** ⚠️ **No seat lock API on Eventbrite.** The entire state machine (AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED → HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED) is internal using Redis + Spring State Machine. Eventbrite is consulted only once — for availability count.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Designed; ⚠️ Seat locking is 100% internal\n\n---\n\n### FR5: Payment via Stripe → Booking Confirmation → E-Ticket Generation\n\n**Implementation: Stripe replaces Eventbrite Checkout Widget entirely.**  \nAll payment processing, order creation, and refunds are handled by Stripe. Eventbrite retains only a passive event listing role.\n\n#### Step 1: Stripe PaymentIntent Creation\n```\nCartAssembledEvent received by payments-ticketing\n  ↓\nBackend calculates discounted total (after coupon, after group discount) in paise\n  ↓\nStripePaymentService.createPaymentIntent() → POST /v1/payment_intents\n  ↓\nclient_secret returned to frontend ONLY (never stored)\n  ↓\nstripe_payment_intent_id stored in payments table (status=PENDING)\n```\n\n#### Step 2: User Pays via Stripe Payment Element (Frontend)\n```\nFrontend renders Stripe Payment Element with client_secret\n  ↓\nUser enters card details → stripe.confirmPayment()\n  ↓\nStripe redirects to return_url with payment_intent as query param\n  ↓\nFrontend sends payment_intent_id to POST /api/payments/confirm-order\n```\n\n#### Step 3: Backend Confirms Payment\n```\nStripePaymentService.retrievePaymentIntent(id) → GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}\n  ↓\nVerify status=succeeded AND amount_received==amount\n  ↓\nIdempotency check: skip if booking already CONFIRMED for this payment_intent_id\n  ↓\nInternal booking record created (bookings table, status=CONFIRMED)\n  ↓\nbooking_items created from cart snapshot\n  ↓\nSeat locks: HARD_LOCKED → CONFIRMED\n  ↓\nstripe_payment_intent_id + stripe_charge_id (latest_charge) saved\n  ↓\nBookingConfirmedEvent published (bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)\n  ↓\nengagement → review eligibility unlocked\n  → identity → order history updated\n  → promotions → CouponRedemption recorded (via bookingId)\n```\n\n#### Step 4: E-Ticket Generation\n```\nETicket record created in e_tickets table\n  ↓\nQR code generated internally from booking_reference + booking_item_id (NO EB barcode)\n  ↓\nPDF e-ticket stored in object storage\n  ↓\nDownload link returned; confirmation email sent\n```\n\n#### Step 5: Stripe Webhooks (Safety Net)\n```\npayment_intent.succeeded   → idempotent booking confirm (skip if already CONFIRMED)\npayment_intent.payment_failed → PaymentFailedEvent → seat locks released\npayment_intent.canceled    → same as payment_intent.payment_failed\nrefund.updated             → update refunds.status\nrefund.failed              → alert ops + notify user\n```\n\n**Eventbrite Calls:** None for payment, order, or e-ticket generation.  \nEB is used passively: organizer creates event on EB for listing; `event.updated` / `event.unpublished` webhooks trigger bulk cancellation.\n\n**Constraints Resolved:** ✅ EB Checkout Widget removed. Orders created internally in `bookings` table on Stripe confirmation. No EB order creation API needed.\n\n**Status:** ✅ Implemented (Stripe sandbox `sk_test_...`)\n\n---\n\n### FR6: Cancellation Request → Refund Policy Engine → Wallet Credit\n\n**What Was Supposed to Happen:**\n\n#### Step 1: User Requests Cancellation\n```\nUser submits cancellation request in your app\n  ↓\nInternal refund_policy calculates refund % (time-based)\n  ↓\nCheck if cancellation permitted\n```\n\n#### Step 2: Stripe Refund Issued\n```\nBackend retrieves stripe_payment_intent_id from bookings table\n  ↓\nStripeRefundService.createRefund(paymentIntentId, amount, reason) → POST /v1/refunds\n  → Full refund: payment_intent=pi_..., reason=requested_by_customer\n  → Partial refund: payment_intent=pi_..., amount=<paise>, reason=requested_by_customer\n  ↓\n"
  }
}
```

Falls back to `\"Anonymous\"` on failure.\n\n**Error Responses:**\n\n| HTTP | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 404 | `EVENT_NOT_FOUND` | `eventId` does not exist in `event_catalog` |\n\n---\n\n### 3. Get Event Rating Summary\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/{eventId}/summary\nAuthorization: (none — public)\n```\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"eventId\": 1001,\n  \"averageRating\": 4.2,\n  \"totalReviews\": 142,\n  \"distribution\": {\n    \"1\": 3,\n    \"2\": 7,\n    \"3\": 18,\n    \"4\": 55,\n    \"5\": 59\n  },\n  \"cachedAt\": \"2026-03-05T18:04:00Z\"\n}\n```\n\n> Result served from Redis cache (key: `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}`, TTL 5 min). Computed fresh on cache miss.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Get Current User's Reviews\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/me?page=0&size=20\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_USER)\n```\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 55,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"rating\": 4,\n      \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n      \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal...\",\n      \"status\": \"PUBLISHED\",\n      \"rejectionReason\": null,\n      \"submittedAt\": \"2026-03-05T15:30:00Z\",\n      \"publishedAt\": \"2026-03-05T15:32:00Z\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 3,\n  \"totalPages\": 1\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. List Reviews — Admin Moderation Queue\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews?status=PENDING_MODERATION&eventId=1001&submittedAfter=2026-03-01T00:00:00Z&page=0&size=20\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_ADMIN)\n```\n\n**Query Params:** `status` (optional, default `PENDING_MODERATION`), `eventId` (optional), `submittedAfter` (optional ISO-8601), `page`, `size`\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 56,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"userId\": 200,\n      \"rating\": 2,\n      \"title\": \"Disappointing\",\n      \"body\": \"Full body text...\",\n      \"status\": \"PENDING_MODERATION\",\n      \"attendanceVerificationStatus\": \"EB_VERIFIED\",\n      \"submittedAt\": \"2026-03-05T16:00:00Z\",\n      \"autoModerationAttempts\": 1,\n      \"lastAutoDecision\": \"PENDING\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 7,\n  \"totalPages\": 1\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. Moderate a Review (Admin)\n\n```\nPUT /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews/{reviewId}/moderate\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_ADMIN)\nContent-Type: application/json\n```\n\n**Request Body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"decision\": \"APPROVE\",\n  \"reason\": null\n}\n```\nor\n```json\n{\n  \"decision\": \"REJECT\",\n  \"reason\": \"Content violates community guidelines — hate speech detected\"\n}\n```\n\n**Validations:**\n- `decision`: required, must be `APPROVE` or `REJECT`\n- `reason`: required when `decision = REJECT`; max 500 chars\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"reviewId\": 56,\n  \"newStatus\": \"PUBLISHED\",\n  \"decidedAt\": \"2026-03-05T18:10:00Z\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Responses:**\n\n| HTTP | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | `reason` missing on REJECT |\n| 404 | `REVIEW_NOT_FOUND` | `reviewId` does not exist |\n| 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_MODERATED` | Review is already `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED` |\n\n---\n\n### SecurityConfig Route Summary\n\n| Pattern | Access |\n|---|---|\n| `POST /api/v1/engagement/reviews` | `ROLE_USER` |\n| `GET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/me` | `ROLE_USER` |\n| `GET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/**` | `permitAll()` |\n| `GET /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews` | `ROLE_ADMIN` |\n| `PUT /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews/*/moderate` | `ROLE_ADMIN` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 6 — Business Logic\n\n---\n\n### 6.1 — `BookingConfirmedEventListener`\n\n```\ntrigger: BookingConfirmedEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)\n```\n\n1. Resolve `eventId` from `slotId` sourced from `CartSummaryDto` — **NOTE:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` does not carry `eventId` directly. `engagement` must derive it by reading slot→event linkage. If this is unavailable from the event payload, `engagement` must call `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` to resolve `eventId` before upserting eligibility.  \n   > **Action item:** Add `slotId` to `BookingConfirmedEvent` if not already present, or add a derived `eventId` field.\n2. Upsert `ReviewEligibility` — `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, event_id) DO NOTHING` to ensure idempotency.\n3. If `engagement.review.eligibility-window-days` is configured: set `eligible_until = eventDate + window`.\n4. Method is `@Transactional` — rollback on any persistence failure; event is re-deliverable.\n\n---\n\n### 6.2 — `BookingCancelledEventListener`\n\n```\ntrigger: BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)\n```\n\n1. Lookup `ReviewEligibility` by `bookingId` column.\n2. If found and `status = UNLOCKED`: transition to `REVOKED`, set `updatedAt`.\n3. If review is already `PUBLISHED` for this user+event: do NOT revoke or unpublish — cancellation after review is published does not retroactively remove the review.\n4. If review is in `PENDING_MODERATION`: transition `Review.status → REJECTED` with `rejectionReason = \"Booking cancelled\"` and transition `ReviewEligibility → REVOKED`.\n5. Idempotent: no-op if eligibility already `REVOKED`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.3 — `ReviewService.submitReview(Long userId, ReviewSubmitRequest request)`\n\n```java\n@Transactional\npublic ReviewSubmitResponse submitReview(Long userId, ReviewSubmitRequest request)\n```\n\n**Step 1 — Eligibility guard:**\n- Load `ReviewEligibility` by `(userId, eventId)`.\n- If absent or `status != UNLOCKED` → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\")`.\n- If `eligible_until != null && eligible_until < now()` → update `status = EXPIRED`, throw `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED\")`.\n\n**Step 2 — Duplicate guard:**\n- `reviewRepository.existsByUserIdAndEventId(userId, eventId)` → if true → throw `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException`.\n\n**Step 3 — Attendance verification:**\n- Delegate to `AttendanceVerificationService.verify(userId, eventId)` → returns `AttendanceVerificationStatus`.\n- If `ReviewNotEligibleException` thrown inside (booking not CONFIRMED): propagate.\n\n**Step 4 — Persist review:**\n- Create `Review` with `status = SUBMITTED`, `attendanceVerificationStatus` from Step 3.\n- Flush to assign `id`.\n- Immediately set `status = PENDING_MODERATION` within same transaction.\n- Save and return `review`.\n\n**Step 5 — Trigger async moderation:**\n- Publish an internal Spring Event `ModerationRequiredEvent(reviewId)` (internal to `engagement` only — not in `shared/`).\n- `ModerationService` listens to this event with `@Async @EventListener`.\n- HTTP response is returned before moderation completes.\n\n**Step 6 — Return:**\n- Return `ReviewSubmitResponse(reviewId, status=\"PENDING_MODERATION\", message)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4 — `AttendanceVerificationService.verify(Long userId, Long eventId)`\n\n**Step 1 — Internal booking check (mandatory gate):**\n- REST `GET /api/v1/internal/bookings/by-user-event?userId={}&eventId={}` to `payments-ticketing`.\n- Timeout: 2 seconds.\n- If response is not 200 or `status != CONFIRMED` → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException`.\n- On timeout or 5xx → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` (booking check is mandatory, not advisory).\n\n**Step 2 — Fetch `ebEventId` (non-blocking):**\n- REST `GET /api/v1/catalog/events/{eventId}/eb-metadata` to `discovery-catalog`.\n- Timeout: 1 second.\n- On timeout/error → `ebEventId = null` → skip EB check.\n\n**Step 3 — Eventbrite attendee cross-check (advisory):**\n- If `ebEventId` is null → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED`.\n- Retrieve `orgToken` from `EbTokenStore.getValidToken(orgId)`.\n- Call `EbAttendeeService.getAttendeesByEvent(orgToken, ebEventId)` → filter by user email.\n- If attendee found and `!cancelled && !refunded` → return `EB_VERIFIED`.\n- If attendee found but `cancelled = true || refunded = true` → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` (internal booking already confirmed; EB state is advisory).\n- If attendee NOT found → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` (log `WARN`).\n- On any EB API exception (timeout, 5xx, `EbAuthException`) → return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` (log `WARN`; never throw to caller).\n\n---\n\n### 6.5 — `ModerationService.triggerAutoModeration(Long reviewId)` `@Async`\n\n**Step 1 — Load and prepare:**\n- Load `Review` by `reviewId`.\n- Assert `review.status = PENDING_MODERATION` — if already `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED`, no-op (idempotent guard).\n- Build `inputText = review.title + \" \" + review.body`.\n\n**Step 2 — Create ModerationRecord:**\n- Persist `ModerationRecord(reviewId, method=AUTO, inputText, decision=PENDING)`.\n\n**Step 3 — Call OpenAI Moderation API:**\n- `OpenAiModerationService.moderate(inputText)` → returns `ModerationResult(flagged, flags, scores)`.\n\n**Step 4a — Not flagged (`flagged = false`):**\n- Update `ModerationRecord.decision = APPROVED`, `decidedAt = now`.\n- Transition `Review.status → PUBLISHED`, set `published_at = now`.\n- Publish `ReviewPublishedEvent(reviewId, eventId, rating)`.\n- Invalidate Redis summary cache `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}`.\n\n**Step 4b — Flagged (`flagged = true`):**\n- Update `ModerationRecord.decision = REJECTED`, `flags = [top flagged categories]`, `scores_json`, `decidedAt = now`.\n- Transition `Review.status → REJECTED`, set `rejection_reason = topCategory`.\n\n**Step 4c — API failure (exception):**\n- Increment `ModerationRecord.auto_retry_count`.\n- Compute `retry_after` using exponential backoff: attempt 1 → +30s, attempt 2 → +2min, attempt 3 → +10min.\n- If `auto_retry_count >= 3`: do not set `retry_after`; leave `PENDING_MODERATION` for manual queue. Log `ERROR`.\n- Update `ModerationRecord` — do NOT throw; error is swallowed.\n\n---\n\n### 6.6 — `ModerationService.retryPendingModerations()` `@Scheduled`\n\n```java\n@Scheduled(fixedDelay = 30_000)  // every 30 seconds\n```\n\n1. Query: `SELECT * FROM moderation_records WHERE decision = 'PENDING' AND retry_after <= NOW() AND auto_retry_count < 3`.\n2. For each record: call `triggerAutoModeration(record.reviewId)` synchronously (within the scheduler thread).\n3. Catch all exceptions per-record — continue processing remaining records (isolated failure).\n4. Log processed count and any per-record failures at INFO level.\n\n---\n\n### 6.7 — `ModerationService.applyManualDecision(Long adminId, Long reviewId, ModerationDecision decision, String reason)`\n\n```java\n@Transactional\npublic ModerationResponse applyManualDecision(...)\n```\n\n1. Load `Review` by `reviewId` → else throw `ReviewNotFoundException`.\n2. Assert `review.status = PENDING_MODERATION` → else throw `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException`.\n3. Persist `ModerationRecord(method=MANUAL, moderatorId=adminId, decision, reason, decidedAt=now)`.\n4. If `decision = APPROVE`:\n   - Transition `Review.status → PUBLISHED`, set `published_at = now`.\n   - Publish `ReviewPublishedEvent`.\n   - Invalidate Redis summary.\n5. If `decision = REJECT`:\n   - Transition `Review.status → REJECTED`, set `rejection_reason = reason`.\n6. Return `ModerationResponse(reviewId, newStatus, decidedAt)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.8 — `ReviewRatingSummaryService.getSummary(Long eventId)`\n\n1. Try Redis GET key `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}`.\n2. If hit: deserialize and return `ReviewRatingSummary`.\n3. If miss: execute DB aggregate:\n   ```sql\n   SELECT\n     AVG(rating)::NUMERIC(3,1)     AS average_rating,\n     COUNT(*)                       AS total_reviews,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=1 THEN 1 END) AS one,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=2 THEN 1 END) AS two,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=3 THEN 1 END) AS three,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=4 THEN 1 END) AS four,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=5 THEN 1 END) AS five\n   FROM reviews\n   WHERE event_id = ? AND status = 'PUBLISHED'\n   ```\n4. Build and serialize `ReviewRatingSummary`.\n5. Redis SET with TTL 5 minutes.\n6. Return summary.\n\n**Cache invalidation:** `ReviewPublishedEvent` listener in `ReviewRatingSummaryService` calls `redisTemplate.delete(\"engagement:review:summary:\" + eventId)`.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 7 — Error Handling\n\n---\n\n### 7.1 — Exception Catalog\n\nAll exceptions extend `BusinessRuleException` from `shared/common/exception/`. The single `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/` maps them to `ApiError { errorCode, message, timestamp, path }`.\n\n| Exception Class | HTTP Code | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\")` | 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | `ReviewEligibility` absent, `status != UNLOCKED`, or booking check returns non-CONFIRMED |\n| `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED\")` | 403 | `REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED` | `eligible_until != null && eligible_until < now()` — review submission window expired |\n| `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException` | 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` | A `Review` record already exists for `(userId, eventId)` in any status |\n| `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException` | 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_MODERATED` | Admin attempts to moderate a review already in `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED` state |\n| `ReviewNotFoundException` | 404 | `REVIEW_NOT_FOUND` | `reviewId` not found in `reviews` table |\n| `MethodArgumentNotValidException` (Spring) | 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Bean validation failure: `rating` out of 1–5 range, blank `title`/`body`, missing `eventId` |\n\n**Error response shape (from `GlobalExceptionHandler`):**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\",\n  \"message\": \"You are not eligible to review this event.\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-05T18:00:00Z\",\n  \"path\": \"/api/v1/engagement/reviews\"\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7.2 — External Dependency Failure Handling\n\n#### `payments-ticketing` Booking Check (Mandatory Gate)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on User |\n|---|---|---|\n| HTTP 404 — no booking found | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE |\n| HTTP 200 but `status != CONFIRMED` | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE |\n| Timeout (> 2s) | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE — booking check is mandatory |\n| HTTP 5xx | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE — fail safe |\n\n> Rationale: Allowing a review submission when booking status is unknown risks fraudulent reviews. Fail closed.\n\n---\n\n#### `discovery-catalog` EB-Metadata Fetch (Advisory)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Submission |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout (> 1s) | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| HTTP 404 | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| HTTP 5xx | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n\n---\n\n#### Eventbrite `EbAttendeeService` Call (Advisory)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Submission |\n|---|---|---|\n| `EbAuthException` — org token expired/invalid | Log WARN, return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` | Proceed (non-blocking) |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx | Log WARN, return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` | Proceed (non-blocking) |\n| Attendee not found in results | Log WARN (reconciliation), return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` | Proceed |\n| Attendee found but `cancelled=true` | Return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` | Proceed — internal booking is authoritative |\n\n> **Never** throw an exception from the EB cross-check path. EB is advisory; internal booking is the gate.\n\n---\n\n#### OpenAI Moderation API (Async — No User Impact)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 1) | Set `retry_after = now + 30s`, `auto_retry_count = 1` | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 2) | Set `retry_after = now + 2min`, `auto_retry_count = 2` | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 3) | Log ERROR, no `retry_after` set | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` — enters manual queue |\n| All 3 retries exhausted | Log ERROR with `reviewId` and last error; alert via metrics counter `engagement.review.moderation.auto.exhausted` | Review visible in admin moderation queue |\n| Exception inside `@Async` handler | Caught and swallowed — does NOT propagate to caller | Review record already committed safely |\n\n---\n\n#### `identity` Display Name Fetch (Read Path — Non-Blocking)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Response |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout (> 500ms) | Return `\"Anonymous\"` for that reviewer | 200 response returned normally |\n| HTTP 404 — user not found | Return `\"Anonymous\"` | 200 response returned normally |\n| HTTP 5xx | Return `\"Anonymous\"` | 200 response returned normally |\n\n---\n\n#### Redis Cache Failure (Summary Endpoint)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Response |\n|---|---|---|\n| Redis `GET` throws exception | Log WARN, fall through to DB query | Summary computed live — slight latency, no error |\n| Redis `SET` throws exception after DB query | Log WARN, return computed result without caching | Correct result returned; cache miss on next call |\n| Redis `DEL` (invalidation) throws | Log WARN | Next read will return stale cache (max 5-min stale) |\n\n---\n\n### 7.3 — Concurrent Submission Guard\n\nIf two concurrent requests hit `POST /reviews` with the same `(userId, eventId)` simultaneously:\n- The duplicate guard at service layer (`existsByUserIdAndEventId`) races against the `UNIQUE` constraint on `(user_id, event_id)` in the DB.\n- The second request will receive a `DataIntegrityViolationException` from JPA on `UNIQUE` violation.\n- `GlobalExceptionHandler` maps `DataIntegrityViolationException` → 409 `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` when the constraint name contains `uq_reviews_user_event`.\n- This is safe and correct — no data corruption possible.\n\n---\n\n### 7.4 — Spring Event Listener Failure Policy\n\n| Event | Listener | Failure Handling |\n|---|---|---|\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` | `BookingConfirmedEventListener` | `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)` — if listener throws, eligibility write fails silently. Log ERROR. The sponsoring transaction is already committed. Re-processing only possible via replay mechanism (future). |\n| `BookingCancelledEvent` | `BookingCancelledEventListener` | Same — log ERROR, no retry. Manual reconciliation job can repair. |\n\n> **Operational note:** Both listeners are idempotent — re-running them with the same event produces a no-op (upsert / conditional update). A reconciliation endpoint `POST /api/v1/internal/engagement/reconcile-eligibility?bookingId={}` can be added for ops use.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 8 — Tests\n\n> **Test structure follows `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` 4-layer model.**  \n> **Location convention:** `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/`\n\n---\n\n### Layer 1 — Domain Unit Tests (`domain/`)\n\n**`ReviewTest.java`**\n\n| Test | Given | When | Then |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `cannotPublishFromSubmitted` | `review.status = SUBMITTED` | Domain method `publish()` called | `IllegalStateException` or guard throws |\n| `canPublishFromApproved` | `review.status = APPROVED` | `publish()` called | `status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt` set |\n| `rejectedIsTerminal` | `review.status = REJECTED` | Any status setter called | State unchanged or guarded |\n"
  }
}
```

Get Current User's Reviews\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/me?page=0&size=20\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_USER)\n```\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 55,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"rating\": 4,\n      \"title\": \"Incredible performance\",\n      \"body\": \"The band was phenomenal...\",\n      \"status\": \"PUBLISHED\",\n      \"rejectionReason\": null,\n      \"submittedAt\": \"2026-03-05T15:30:00Z\",\n      \"publishedAt\": \"2026-03-05T15:32:00Z\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 3,\n  \"totalPages\": 1\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. List Reviews — Admin Moderation Queue\n\n```\nGET /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews?status=PENDING_MODERATION&eventId=1001&submittedAfter=2026-03-01T00:00:00Z&page=0&size=20\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_ADMIN)\n```\n\n**Query Params:** `status` (optional, default `PENDING_MODERATION`), `eventId` (optional), `submittedAfter` (optional ISO-8601), `page`, `size`\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"content\": [\n    {\n      \"reviewId\": 56,\n      \"eventId\": 1001,\n      \"userId\": 200,\n      \"rating\": 2,\n      \"title\": \"Disappointing\",\n      \"body\": \"Full body text...\",\n      \"status\": \"PENDING_MODERATION\",\n      \"attendanceVerificationStatus\": \"EB_VERIFIED\",\n      \"submittedAt\": \"2026-03-05T16:00:00Z\",\n      \"autoModerationAttempts\": 1,\n      \"lastAutoDecision\": \"PENDING\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"page\": 0,\n  \"size\": 20,\n  \"totalElements\": 7,\n  \"totalPages\": 1\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. Moderate a Review (Admin)\n\n```\nPUT /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews/{reviewId}/moderate\nAuthorization: Bearer <JWT>   (ROLE_ADMIN)\nContent-Type: application/json\n```\n\n**Request Body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"decision\": \"APPROVE\",\n  \"reason\": null\n}\n```\nor\n```json\n{\n  \"decision\": \"REJECT\",\n  \"reason\": \"Content violates community guidelines — hate speech detected\"\n}\n```\n\n**Validations:**\n- `decision`: required, must be `APPROVE` or `REJECT`\n- `reason`: required when `decision = REJECT`; max 500 chars\n\n**Response — 200 OK:**\n```json\n{\n  \"reviewId\": 56,\n  \"newStatus\": \"PUBLISHED\",\n  \"decidedAt\": \"2026-03-05T18:10:00Z\"\n}\n```\n\n**Error Responses:**\n\n| HTTP | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|\n| 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | `reason` missing on REJECT |\n| 404 | `REVIEW_NOT_FOUND` | `reviewId` does not exist |\n| 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_MODERATED` | Review is already `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED` |\n\n---\n\n### SecurityConfig Route Summary\n\n| Pattern | Access |\n|---|---|\n| `POST /api/v1/engagement/reviews` | `ROLE_USER` |\n| `GET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/me` | `ROLE_USER` |\n| `GET /api/v1/engagement/reviews/events/**` | `permitAll()` |\n| `GET /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews` | `ROLE_ADMIN` |\n| `PUT /api/v1/admin/engagement/reviews/*/moderate` | `ROLE_ADMIN` |\n\n---\n\n## Stage 6 — Business Logic\n\n---\n\n### 6.1 — `BookingConfirmedEventListener`\n\n```\ntrigger: BookingConfirmedEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId)\n```\n\n1. Resolve `eventId` from `slotId` sourced from `CartSummaryDto` — **NOTE:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` does not carry `eventId` directly. `engagement` must derive it by reading slot→event linkage. If this is unavailable from the event payload, `engagement` must call `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` to resolve `eventId` before upserting eligibility.  \n   > **Action item:** Add `slotId` to `BookingConfirmedEvent` if not already present, or add a derived `eventId` field.\n2. Upsert `ReviewEligibility` — `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (user_id, event_id) DO NOTHING` to ensure idempotency.\n3. If `engagement.review.eligibility-window-days` is configured: set `eligible_until = eventDate + window`.\n4. Method is `@Transactional` — rollback on any persistence failure; event is re-deliverable.\n\n---\n\n### 6.2 — `BookingCancelledEventListener`\n\n```\ntrigger: BookingCancelledEvent(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, userId, reason)\n```\n\n1. Lookup `ReviewEligibility` by `bookingId` column.\n2. If found and `status = UNLOCKED`: transition to `REVOKED`, set `updatedAt`.\n3. If review is already `PUBLISHED` for this user+event: do NOT revoke or unpublish — cancellation after review is published does not retroactively remove the review.\n4. If review is in `PENDING_MODERATION`: transition `Review.status → REJECTED` with `rejectionReason = \"Booking cancelled\"` and transition `ReviewEligibility → REVOKED`.\n5. Idempotent: no-op if eligibility already `REVOKED`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.3 — `ReviewService.submitReview(Long userId, ReviewSubmitRequest request)`\n\n```java\n@Transactional\npublic ReviewSubmitResponse submitReview(Long userId, ReviewSubmitRequest request)\n```\n\n**Step 1 — Eligibility guard:**\n- Load `ReviewEligibility` by `(userId, eventId)`.\n- If absent or `status != UNLOCKED` → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\")`.\n- If `eligible_until != null && eligible_until < now()` → update `status = EXPIRED`, throw `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED\")`.\n\n**Step 2 — Duplicate guard:**\n- `reviewRepository.existsByUserIdAndEventId(userId, eventId)` → if true → throw `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException`.\n\n**Step 3 — Attendance verification:**\n- Delegate to `AttendanceVerificationService.verify(userId, eventId)` → returns `AttendanceVerificationStatus`.\n- If `ReviewNotEligibleException` thrown inside (booking not CONFIRMED): propagate.\n\n**Step 4 — Persist review:**\n- Create `Review` with `status = SUBMITTED`, `attendanceVerificationStatus` from Step 3.\n- Flush to assign `id`.\n- Immediately set `status = PENDING_MODERATION` within same transaction.\n- Save and return `review`.\n\n**Step 5 — Trigger async moderation:**\n- Publish an internal Spring Event `ModerationRequiredEvent(reviewId)` (internal to `engagement` only — not in `shared/`).\n- `ModerationService` listens to this event with `@Async @EventListener`.\n- HTTP response is returned before moderation completes.\n\n**Step 6 — Return:**\n- Return `ReviewSubmitResponse(reviewId, status=\"PENDING_MODERATION\", message)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.4 — `AttendanceVerificationService.verify(Long userId, Long eventId)`\n\n**Step 1 — Internal booking check (mandatory gate):**\n- REST `GET /api/v1/internal/bookings/by-user-event?userId={}&eventId={}` to `payments-ticketing`.\n- Timeout: 2 seconds.\n- If response is not 200 or `status != CONFIRMED` → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException`.\n- On timeout or 5xx → throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` (booking check is mandatory, not advisory).\n\n**Step 2 — Fetch `ebEventId` (non-blocking):**\n- REST `GET /api/v1/catalog/events/{eventId}/eb-metadata` to `discovery-catalog`.\n- Timeout: 1 second.\n- On timeout/error → `ebEventId = null` → skip EB check.\n\n**Step 3 — Eventbrite attendee cross-check (advisory):**\n- If `ebEventId` is null → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED`.\n- Retrieve `orgToken` from `EbTokenStore.getValidToken(orgId)`.\n- Call `EbAttendeeService.getAttendeesByEvent(orgToken, ebEventId)` → filter by user email.\n- If attendee found and `!cancelled && !refunded` → return `EB_VERIFIED`.\n- If attendee found but `cancelled = true || refunded = true` → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` (internal booking already confirmed; EB state is advisory).\n- If attendee NOT found → return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` (log `WARN`).\n- On any EB API exception (timeout, 5xx, `EbAuthException`) → return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` (log `WARN`; never throw to caller).\n\n---\n\n### 6.5 — `ModerationService.triggerAutoModeration(Long reviewId)` `@Async`\n\n**Step 1 — Load and prepare:**\n- Load `Review` by `reviewId`.\n- Assert `review.status = PENDING_MODERATION` — if already `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED`, no-op (idempotent guard).\n- Build `inputText = review.title + \" \" + review.body`.\n\n**Step 2 — Create ModerationRecord:**\n- Persist `ModerationRecord(reviewId, method=AUTO, inputText, decision=PENDING)`.\n\n**Step 3 — Call OpenAI Moderation API:**\n- `OpenAiModerationService.moderate(inputText)` → returns `ModerationResult(flagged, flags, scores)`.\n\n**Step 4a — Not flagged (`flagged = false`):**\n- Update `ModerationRecord.decision = APPROVED`, `decidedAt = now`.\n- Transition `Review.status → PUBLISHED`, set `published_at = now`.\n- Publish `ReviewPublishedEvent(reviewId, eventId, rating)`.\n- Invalidate Redis summary cache `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}`.\n\n**Step 4b — Flagged (`flagged = true`):**\n- Update `ModerationRecord.decision = REJECTED`, `flags = [top flagged categories]`, `scores_json`, `decidedAt = now`.\n- Transition `Review.status → REJECTED`, set `rejection_reason = topCategory`.\n\n**Step 4c — API failure (exception):**\n- Increment `ModerationRecord.auto_retry_count`.\n- Compute `retry_after` using exponential backoff: attempt 1 → +30s, attempt 2 → +2min, attempt 3 → +10min.\n- If `auto_retry_count >= 3`: do not set `retry_after`; leave `PENDING_MODERATION` for manual queue. Log `ERROR`.\n- Update `ModerationRecord` — do NOT throw; error is swallowed.\n\n---\n\n### 6.6 — `ModerationService.retryPendingModerations()` `@Scheduled`\n\n```java\n@Scheduled(fixedDelay = 30_000)  // every 30 seconds\n```\n\n1. Query: `SELECT * FROM moderation_records WHERE decision = 'PENDING' AND retry_after <= NOW() AND auto_retry_count < 3`.\n2. For each record: call `triggerAutoModeration(record.reviewId)` synchronously (within the scheduler thread).\n3. Catch all exceptions per-record — continue processing remaining records (isolated failure).\n4. Log processed count and any per-record failures at INFO level.\n\n---\n\n### 6.7 — `ModerationService.applyManualDecision(Long adminId, Long reviewId, ModerationDecision decision, String reason)`\n\n```java\n@Transactional\npublic ModerationResponse applyManualDecision(...)\n```\n\n1. Load `Review` by `reviewId` → else throw `ReviewNotFoundException`.\n2. Assert `review.status = PENDING_MODERATION` → else throw `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException`.\n3. Persist `ModerationRecord(method=MANUAL, moderatorId=adminId, decision, reason, decidedAt=now)`.\n4. If `decision = APPROVE`:\n   - Transition `Review.status → PUBLISHED`, set `published_at = now`.\n   - Publish `ReviewPublishedEvent`.\n   - Invalidate Redis summary.\n5. If `decision = REJECT`:\n   - Transition `Review.status → REJECTED`, set `rejection_reason = reason`.\n6. Return `ModerationResponse(reviewId, newStatus, decidedAt)`.\n\n---\n\n### 6.8 — `ReviewRatingSummaryService.getSummary(Long eventId)`\n\n1. Try Redis GET key `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}`.\n2. If hit: deserialize and return `ReviewRatingSummary`.\n3. If miss: execute DB aggregate:\n   ```sql\n   SELECT\n     AVG(rating)::NUMERIC(3,1)     AS average_rating,\n     COUNT(*)                       AS total_reviews,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=1 THEN 1 END) AS one,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=2 THEN 1 END) AS two,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=3 THEN 1 END) AS three,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=4 THEN 1 END) AS four,\n     COUNT(CASE WHEN rating=5 THEN 1 END) AS five\n   FROM reviews\n   WHERE event_id = ? AND status = 'PUBLISHED'\n   ```\n4. Build and serialize `ReviewRatingSummary`.\n5. Redis SET with TTL 5 minutes.\n6. Return summary.\n\n**Cache invalidation:** `ReviewPublishedEvent` listener in `ReviewRatingSummaryService` calls `redisTemplate.delete(\"engagement:review:summary:\" + eventId)`.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 7 — Error Handling\n\n---\n\n### 7.1 — Exception Catalog\n\nAll exceptions extend `BusinessRuleException` from `shared/common/exception/`. The single `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/` maps them to `ApiError { errorCode, message, timestamp, path }`.\n\n| Exception Class | HTTP Code | `errorCode` | Condition |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\")` | 403 | `REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` | `ReviewEligibility` absent, `status != UNLOCKED`, or booking check returns non-CONFIRMED |\n| `ReviewNotEligibleException(\"REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED\")` | 403 | `REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED` | `eligible_until != null && eligible_until < now()` — review submission window expired |\n| `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException` | 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` | A `Review` record already exists for `(userId, eventId)` in any status |\n| `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException` | 409 | `REVIEW_ALREADY_MODERATED` | Admin attempts to moderate a review already in `PUBLISHED` or `REJECTED` state |\n| `ReviewNotFoundException` | 404 | `REVIEW_NOT_FOUND` | `reviewId` not found in `reviews` table |\n| `MethodArgumentNotValidException` (Spring) | 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Bean validation failure: `rating` out of 1–5 range, blank `title`/`body`, missing `eventId` |\n\n**Error response shape (from `GlobalExceptionHandler`):**\n```json\n{\n  \"errorCode\": \"REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE\",\n  \"message\": \"You are not eligible to review this event.\",\n  \"timestamp\": \"2026-03-05T18:00:00Z\",\n  \"path\": \"/api/v1/engagement/reviews\"\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7.2 — External Dependency Failure Handling\n\n#### `payments-ticketing` Booking Check (Mandatory Gate)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on User |\n|---|---|---|\n| HTTP 404 — no booking found | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE |\n| HTTP 200 but `status != CONFIRMED` | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE |\n| Timeout (> 2s) | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE — booking check is mandatory |\n| HTTP 5xx | Throw `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403 REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE — fail safe |\n\n> Rationale: Allowing a review submission when booking status is unknown risks fraudulent reviews. Fail closed.\n\n---\n\n#### `discovery-catalog` EB-Metadata Fetch (Advisory)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Submission |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout (> 1s) | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| HTTP 404 | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| HTTP 5xx | `ebEventId = null`, skip EB check | Proceed with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n\n---\n\n#### Eventbrite `EbAttendeeService` Call (Advisory)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Submission |\n|---|---|---|\n| `EbAuthException` — org token expired/invalid | Log WARN, return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` | Proceed (non-blocking) |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx | Log WARN, return `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE` | Proceed (non-blocking) |\n| Attendee not found in results | Log WARN (reconciliation), return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` | Proceed |\n| Attendee found but `cancelled=true` | Return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` | Proceed — internal booking is authoritative |\n\n> **Never** throw an exception from the EB cross-check path. EB is advisory; internal booking is the gate.\n\n---\n\n#### OpenAI Moderation API (Async — No User Impact)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 1) | Set `retry_after = now + 30s`, `auto_retry_count = 1` | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 2) | Set `retry_after = now + 2min`, `auto_retry_count = 2` | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| Timeout / HTTP 5xx (attempt 3) | Log ERROR, no `retry_after` set | Review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` — enters manual queue |\n| All 3 retries exhausted | Log ERROR with `reviewId` and last error; alert via metrics counter `engagement.review.moderation.auto.exhausted` | Review visible in admin moderation queue |\n| Exception inside `@Async` handler | Caught and swallowed — does NOT propagate to caller | Review record already committed safely |\n\n---\n\n#### `identity` Display Name Fetch (Read Path — Non-Blocking)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Response |\n|---|---|---|\n| Timeout (> 500ms) | Return `\"Anonymous\"` for that reviewer | 200 response returned normally |\n| HTTP 404 — user not found | Return `\"Anonymous\"` | 200 response returned normally |\n| HTTP 5xx | Return `\"Anonymous\"` | 200 response returned normally |\n\n---\n\n#### Redis Cache Failure (Summary Endpoint)\n\n| Failure Mode | Handling | Effect on Response |\n|---|---|---|\n| Redis `GET` throws exception | Log WARN, fall through to DB query | Summary computed live — slight latency, no error |\n| Redis `SET` throws exception after DB query | Log WARN, return computed result without caching | Correct result returned; cache miss on next call |\n| Redis `DEL` (invalidation) throws | Log WARN | Next read will return stale cache (max 5-min stale) |\n\n---\n\n### 7.3 — Concurrent Submission Guard\n\nIf two concurrent requests hit `POST /reviews` with the same `(userId, eventId)` simultaneously:\n- The duplicate guard at service layer (`existsByUserIdAndEventId`) races against the `UNIQUE` constraint on `(user_id, event_id)` in the DB.\n- The second request will receive a `DataIntegrityViolationException` from JPA on `UNIQUE` violation.\n- `GlobalExceptionHandler` maps `DataIntegrityViolationException` → 409 `REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` when the constraint name contains `uq_reviews_user_event`.\n- This is safe and correct — no data corruption possible.\n\n---\n\n### 7.4 — Spring Event Listener Failure Policy\n\n| Event | Listener | Failure Handling |\n|---|---|---|\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` | `BookingConfirmedEventListener` | `@TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)` — if listener throws, eligibility write fails silently. Log ERROR. The sponsoring transaction is already committed. Re-processing only possible via replay mechanism (future). |\n| `BookingCancelledEvent` | `BookingCancelledEventListener` | Same — log ERROR, no retry. Manual reconciliation job can repair. |\n\n> **Operational note:** Both listeners are idempotent — re-running them with the same event produces a no-op (upsert / conditional update). A reconciliation endpoint `POST /api/v1/internal/engagement/reconcile-eligibility?bookingId={}` can be added for ops use.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 8 — Tests\n\n> **Test structure follows `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` 4-layer model.**  \n> **Location convention:** `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/`\n\n---\n\n### Layer 1 — Domain Unit Tests (`domain/`)\n\n**`ReviewTest.java`**\n\n| Test | Given | When | Then |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `cannotPublishFromSubmitted` | `review.status = SUBMITTED` | Domain method `publish()` called | `IllegalStateException` or guard throws |\n| `canPublishFromApproved` | `review.status = APPROVED` | `publish()` called | `status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt` set |\n| `rejectedIsTerminal` | `review.status = REJECTED` | Any status setter called | State unchanged or guarded |\n"
  }
}
```
---

{

    "output": "Chunk ID: 5cc6c0\nWall time: 0.0560 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1707\nOutput:\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` — list org discounts for validation\n  - `GET /discounts/{discount_id}/` — read discount rules and current usage count\n\n**Step 3: Discount Applied at Checkout (Stripe)**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - `CouponAppliedEvent` published → booking-inventory recomputes cart total with discounted price\n  - Discounted total used as `amount` in `POST /v1/payment_intents` during FR5 Step 1\n  - No Eventbrite call — discount is entirely internal at this stage\n- Stripe API Call: (via FR5 path) `POST /v1/payment_intents` with discounted `amount`\n- Eventbrite API Call: (none)\n\n**Step 4: Post-Redemption Sync**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - After successful order, coupon redemption recorded in `coupon_redemptions` table\n  - Usage count incremented internally\n  - If usage limit reached, discount deactivated on Eventbrite\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /discounts/{discount_id}/` — update usage limit or deactivate\n  - `DELETE /discounts/{discount_id}/` — remove if fully exhausted\n\n**ACL Facades:** `EbDiscountSyncService`  \n**DB Tables:** `promotions`, `coupons`, `coupon_redemptions`  \n**Publishes:** `CouponAppliedEvent` → booking-inventory\n\n### FR8 — Review & Rating Submission → Moderation Queue → Public Publish\n**Module:** engagement\n\nKey Point: There is NO reviews API on Eventbrite. The entire review system is internal. Eventbrite is used only to verify that the user actually attended the event (via attendee record) before allowing review submission.\n\n**Step 1: Verify Attendance**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - User attempts to submit a review\n  - Backend checks whether user has a CONFIRMED booking for this `event_id`\n  - REST call to payments-ticketing to verify booking status\n  - Cross-check Eventbrite attendee record using user email\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — find attendee by email\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/{attendee_id}/` — check `cancelled=false`, `refunded=false`\n\n**Step 2: Review Submission**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - If attendance verified: Review record created in `reviews` table with status `SUBMITTED`\n  - Review transitions to `PENDING_MODERATION`\n  - Moderation queue updated\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - (none — review submission is 100% internal)\n\n**Step 3: Moderation**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Moderation engine runs: profanity filter, spam detection (OpenAI moderation API)\n  - `ModerationRecord` created with decision\n  - If APPROVED: review transitions to `PUBLISHED`\n  - If REJECTED: review is terminal — user notified\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - (none — moderation is 100% internal)\n\n**Review States:** `SUBMITTED → PENDING_MODERATION → APPROVED → PUBLISHED / REJECTED (terminal)`  \n**ACL Facades:** `EbAttendeeService`  \n**DB Tables:** `reviews`, `moderation_records`  \n**Listens to:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` → unlocks review eligibility for that booking\n\n### FR9 — Admin Dashboard → Event CRUD → Seat Inventory Management\n**Module:** `admin/` (orchestrator) — delegates to scheduling, discovery-catalog, booking-inventory\n\nThe admin dashboard is the most fully-supported Eventbrite flow. Almost every action has a corresponding Eventbrite API. Eventbrite and your internal DB are kept in sync through every CRUD operation.\n\n**Event CRUD**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin creates/edits/deletes events in app dashboard\n  - Internal DB updated first (`event_catalog`, `show_slots`)\n  - Then Eventbrite is called to mirror the action\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update details\n  - `DELETE /events/{event_id}/` — delete draft event\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — go live\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/unpublish/` — pull from public view\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/copy/` — duplicate template\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event (triggers FR6 bulk refund)\n\n**Seat Inventory Management**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin manages ticket tiers, capacities, seat maps\n  - Internal `pricing_tiers`, `seat_maps`, `inventory` updated first\n  - Then pushed to Eventbrite\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tier\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/{id}/` — update ticket tier\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/` — set inventory capacity\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — update inventory tier\n  - `DELETE /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — remove inventory tier\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/` — read capacity\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/` — update capacity ceiling\n\n**Venue & Media Management**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin uploads event images, banners\n  - Admin updates venue details\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /media/upload/` — upload event banner/image\n  - `GET /media/{media_id}/` — retrieve uploaded media\n  - `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create/update venue\n  - `PUT /venues/{venue_id}/` — update venue address/capacity\n\n**Event Description & Content**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin writes rich HTML event description\n  - Structured content (modules, widgets) configured\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/structured_content/{version}/` — set structured content\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/structured_content/` — retrieve published content\n  - `GET /event-descriptions/{event_id}/` — retrieve full HTML description\n\n**Reporting & Analytics**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin views sales and attendance reports\n  - Reports pulled from Eventbrite and merged with internal analytics\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /reports/sales/` — pull sales report\n  - `GET /reports/attendees/` — pull attendee report\n  - `GET /organizations/{org_id}/orders/` — list all orders\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — list attendees for event\n\n**ACL Facades:** `EbEventSyncService`, `EbVenueService`, `EbTicketService`, `EbCapacityService`\n\n### FR10 — Seat Lock State Machine with Conflict Resolution\n**Module:** booking-inventory — `booking-inventory/statemachine/`\n\nKey Point: Eventbrite has no seat lock API. The entire state machine runs on Spring State Machine + Redis. Eventbrite is consulted only once — at the `AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED` transition — to check if tickets are still available. All locking, conflict resolution, and rollback is internal.\n\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_qPpqZ9RBLIFD6ujLqime17DK",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 5cc6c0\nWall time: 0.0560 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1707\nOutput:\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /organizations/{org_id}/discounts/` — list org discounts for validation\n  - `GET /discounts/{discount_id}/` — read discount rules and current usage count\n\n**Step 3: Discount Applied at Checkout (Stripe)**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - `CouponAppliedEvent` published → booking-inventory recomputes cart total with discounted price\n  - Discounted total used as `amount` in `POST /v1/payment_intents` during FR5 Step 1\n  - No Eventbrite call — discount is entirely internal at this stage\n- Stripe API Call: (via FR5 path) `POST /v1/payment_intents` with discounted `amount`\n- Eventbrite API Call: (none)\n\n**Step 4: Post-Redemption Sync**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - After successful order, coupon redemption recorded in `coupon_redemptions` table\n  - Usage count incremented internally\n  - If usage limit reached, discount deactivated on Eventbrite\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /discounts/{discount_id}/` — update usage limit or deactivate\n  - `DELETE /discounts/{discount_id}/` — remove if fully exhausted\n\n**ACL Facades:** `EbDiscountSyncService`  \n**DB Tables:** `promotions`, `coupons`, `coupon_redemptions`  \n**Publishes:** `CouponAppliedEvent` → booking-inventory\n\n### FR8 — Review & Rating Submission → Moderation Queue → Public Publish\n**Module:** engagement\n\nKey Point: There is NO reviews API on Eventbrite. The entire review system is internal. Eventbrite is used only to verify that the user actually attended the event (via attendee record) before allowing review submission.\n\n**Step 1: Verify Attendance**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - User attempts to submit a review\n  - Backend checks whether user has a CONFIRMED booking for this `event_id`\n  - REST call to payments-ticketing to verify booking status\n  - Cross-check Eventbrite attendee record using user email\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — find attendee by email\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/{attendee_id}/` — check `cancelled=false`, `refunded=false`\n\n**Step 2: Review Submission**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - If attendance verified: Review record created in `reviews` table with status `SUBMITTED`\n  - Review transitions to `PENDING_MODERATION`\n  - Moderation queue updated\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - (none — review submission is 100% internal)\n\n**Step 3: Moderation**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Moderation engine runs: profanity filter, spam detection (OpenAI moderation API)\n  - `ModerationRecord` created with decision\n  - If APPROVED: review transitions to `PUBLISHED`\n  - If REJECTED: review is terminal — user notified\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - (none — moderation is 100% internal)\n\n**Review States:** `SUBMITTED → PENDING_MODERATION → APPROVED → PUBLISHED / REJECTED (terminal)`  \n**ACL Facades:** `EbAttendeeService`  \n**DB Tables:** `reviews`, `moderation_records`  \n**Listens to:** `BookingConfirmedEvent` → unlocks review eligibility for that booking\n\n### FR9 — Admin Dashboard → Event CRUD → Seat Inventory Management\n**Module:** `admin/` (orchestrator) — delegates to scheduling, discovery-catalog, booking-inventory\n\nThe admin dashboard is the most fully-supported Eventbrite flow. Almost every action has a corresponding Eventbrite API. Eventbrite and your internal DB are kept in sync through every CRUD operation.\n\n**Event CRUD**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin creates/edits/deletes events in app dashboard\n  - Internal DB updated first (`event_catalog`, `show_slots`)\n  - Then Eventbrite is called to mirror the action\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /organizations/{org_id}/events/` — create event\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/` — update details\n  - `DELETE /events/{event_id}/` — delete draft event\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/publish/` — go live\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/unpublish/` — pull from public view\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/copy/` — duplicate template\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/cancel/` — cancel event (triggers FR6 bulk refund)\n\n**Seat Inventory Management**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin manages ticket tiers, capacities, seat maps\n  - Internal `pricing_tiers`, `seat_maps`, `inventory` updated first\n  - Then pushed to Eventbrite\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/` — create ticket tier\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/ticket_classes/{id}/` — update ticket tier\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/` — set inventory capacity\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — update inventory tier\n  - `DELETE /events/{event_id}/inventory_tiers/{id}/` — remove inventory tier\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/` — read capacity\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/capacity_tier/` — update capacity ceiling\n\n**Venue & Media Management**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin uploads event images, banners\n  - Admin updates venue details\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /media/upload/` — upload event banner/image\n  - `GET /media/{media_id}/` — retrieve uploaded media\n  - `POST /organizations/{org_id}/venues/` — create/update venue\n  - `PUT /venues/{venue_id}/` — update venue address/capacity\n\n**Event Description & Content**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin writes rich HTML event description\n  - Structured content (modules, widgets) configured\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `POST /events/{event_id}/structured_content/{version}/` — set structured content\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/structured_content/` — retrieve published content\n  - `GET /event-descriptions/{event_id}/` — retrieve full HTML description\n\n**Reporting & Analytics**\n- Internal App (Your DB / Spring Boot)\n  - Admin views sales and attendance reports\n  - Reports pulled from Eventbrite and merged with internal analytics\n- Eventbrite API Call (via `shared/` ACL)\n  - `GET /reports/sales/` — pull sales report\n  - `GET /reports/attendees/` — pull attendee report\n  - `GET /organizations/{org_id}/orders/` — list all orders\n  - `GET /events/{event_id}/attendees/` — list attendees for event\n\n**ACL Facades:** `EbEventSyncService`, `EbVenueService`, `EbTicketService`, `EbCapacityService`\n\n### FR10 — Seat Lock State Machine with Conflict Resolution\n**Module:** booking-inventory — `booking-inventory/statemachine/`\n\nKey Point: Eventbrite has no seat lock API. The entire state machine runs on Spring State Machine + Redis. Eventbrite is consulted only once — at the `AVAILABLE → SOFT_LOCKED` transition — to check if tickets are still available. All locking, conflict resolution, and rollback is internal.\n\n"

**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:26:41.410Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_hdqhb2RYUadTMeEue7eeDyld",
    "output": "Chunk ID: a8be44\nWall time: 0.0503 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1527\nOutput:\n| `JwtAuthenticationFilter` | `shared/security/` | Spring Security filter — registered once |\n| `RedisConfig` | `shared/config/` | Redisson bean for distributed seat locking |\n| `ElasticsearchConfig` | `shared/config/` | ES client for RAG vector store |\n| `SlotSummaryReader` | `shared/common/service/` | Read interface: `getSlotSummary(Long slotId) → SlotSummaryDto`; implemented by `scheduling/SlotSummaryReaderImpl` |\n| `SlotPricingReader` | `shared/common/service/` | Read interface: `getSlotPricing(Long slotId) → List<PricingTierDto>`; implemented by `scheduling/SlotPricingReaderImpl` |\n| `CartSnapshotReader` | `shared/common/service/` | Read interface with two methods: `getCartItems(Long cartId) → List<CartItemSnapshotDto>` (line items) and `getCartSummary(Long cartId) → CartSummaryDto` (cart header); implemented by `booking-inventory/CartSnapshotReaderImpl`; consumed by `payments-ticketing` (payment hot path) and `promotions` (coupon eligibility) |\n| `PaymentConfirmationReader` | `shared/common/service/` | Read interface: `isPaymentConfirmed(Long cartId) → boolean`; implemented by `payments-ticketing/PaymentConfirmationReaderImpl`; consumed by `booking-inventory` `PaymentTimeoutWatchdog` |\n| `SlotSummaryDto` | `shared/common/dto/` | Record: `slotId, status, ebEventId, seatingMode, orgId, venueId, cityId, sourceSeatMapId` |\n| `PricingTierDto` | `shared/common/dto/` | Record: `tierId, tierName, price(Money), quota, tierType, ebTicketClassId, ebInventoryTierId, groupDiscountThreshold, groupDiscountPercent` |\n| `CartItemSnapshotDto` | `shared/common/dto/` | Record: `itemId, cartId, seatId, gaClaimId, ticketClassId, unitPrice, currency, quantity`; used by `CartSnapshotReader` |\n| `CartSummaryDto` | `shared/common/dto/` | Record: `cartId, orgId, slotId, couponCode, expiresAt, currency`; cart-level header for coupon eligibility checks in `promotions` |\n| `CouponAppliedEvent` | `shared/common/event/published/` | Record: `cartId, couponCode, discountAmountInSmallestUnit, currency, userId`; published by `promotions`, consumed by `booking-inventory.CouponAppliedListener` to set `cart.couponDiscountAmount` |\n| `CouponValidatedEvent` | `shared/common/event/published/` | Record: `cartId, couponCode, discountAmountInSmallestUnit, currency, userId`; published by `promotions` after passing eligibility checks — audit/analytics signal before cart mutation |\n\n## 9. Hard Rules — Never Violate These\n\n1. `app/` has zero business logic. No `@Service`, no `@Entity`, no `@Repository`.\n2. `shared/` has zero dependency on any module. It knows nothing about bookings, users, or events.\n3. No module imports another module's `@Entity` class, `@Repository` interface, or concrete `@Service` bean. The sole approved exception is shared reader interfaces defined in `shared/common/service/` — see Rule 1 above.\n4. No module calls Eventbrite HTTP directly — only through `shared/eventbrite/service/`. No module calls Stripe HTTP directly — only through `shared/stripe/service/`.\n5. Spring Events carry only primitive values, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n6. `admin/` defines no `@Entity` and owns no database table.\n7. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n8. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never add `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n9. Internal DB is always updated FIRST before any Eventbrite or Stripe API call is made.\n10. `eventbrite_event_id` is never null in production for events that require EB listing sync — if null, block FR7, FR8, FR10 (EB availability check). FR5 (Stripe payments) and FR6 (Stripe refunds) operate independently of `eventbrite_event_id`.\n11. All payments are accepted and processed via **Stripe** (`StripePaymentService`). No payment processing through Eventbrite. The Eventbrite Checkout Widget is removed — do NOT use or reference it.\n12. All refunds are submitted programmatically via **Stripe** (`StripeRefundService` — `POST /v1/refunds`). Do NOT use `EbRefundService` for any refund submission. Do NOT mimic admin actions on Eventbrite for refunds.\n13. Stripe `client_secret` is sent to the frontend ONLY — never log, store, or return it from the backend. `stripe_payment_intent_id` (`pi_...`) and `stripe_charge_id` (`ch_...`) are stored in the `payments` and `bookings` tables.\n14. Stripe webhook endpoint (`POST /api/webhooks/stripe`) MUST verify the `Stripe-Signature` header via `Webhook.constructEvent()` before any processing. Reject any request where verification fails with HTTP 400.\n\n## 10. Where to Put New Code\n\n**Java package naming:** module names with hyphens removed. Example: `booking-inventory` → `com.[org].bookinginventory`\n\n### Code Placement Guide\n\n| Need | Location |\n|---|---|\n| New REST endpoint | `[module]/api/controller/` |\n| New request/response shape | `[module]/api/dto/request/` or `response/` |\n| New business rule or domain behavior | `[module]/domain/` (no Spring annotations) |\n| New use case orchestration | `[module]/service/` |\n| New database table access | `[module]/repository/` |\n| Module needs to notify others | `[module]/event/published/` |\n| Module reacts to another | `[module]/event/listener/` |\n| Shared across 2+ modules | `shared/common/` |\n| New external system integration | `shared/[system-acl]/` (never inside a module) |\n| Admin read/aggregate view | `admin/service/` + `admin/api/controller/` |\n| New Eventbrite API call | `shared/eventbrite/service/` — add to appropriate facade only |\n| New Stripe API call | `shared/stripe/service/` — add to `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, or `StripeWebhookHandler` only |\n\n## 11. Flyway Migration Conventions\n\n- All Flyway scripts live in `app/src/main/resources/db/migration/`\n- Naming: `V[n]__[verb]_[table].sql` — e.g. `V5__create_bookings.sql`\n- Version numbers are global across all modules — check the current highest before adding (latest as of March 2026: `V37__create_promotions_tables.sql`)\n- Never modify an existing migration file — always add a new one\n- Table names use `snake_case`\n"
  }
}
```

}

Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Reference Files\\n\\n| File | Governs |\\n|---|---|\\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Quick Reference\\n\\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\\n\\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\\n\\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\\n\\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\\n\\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\\n\\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\\n\\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\\n\\n```\\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\\n  |\\n  v\\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\n  |\\n  v\\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\\n  |\\n  v\\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\\n```\\n\\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\\n\\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\\n\\n\\n## Skills\\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\\n### Available skills\\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \\\"how do I do X\\\", \\\"find a skill for X\\\", \\\"is there a skill that can...\\\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills.
