(file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\\n### How to use skills\\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\\n- Coordination and sequencing:\\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\\n- Context hygiene:\\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\\n</INSTRUCTIONS>\"\n./POQ/Payments/Flow5_Planning_refractor.md:124:        \"text\": \"# AGENTS.md instructions for /home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking\\n\\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\\n# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\\n\\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\\n> any output. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Quick Reference\\n\\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\\n\\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\\n\\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\\n\\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\\n\\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\\n\\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\\n\\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\\n\\n```\\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\\n  |\\n  v\\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\n  |\\n  v\\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\\n  |\\n  v\\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\\n```\\n\\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\\n\\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\\n\\n\\n## Skills\\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\\n### Available skills\\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \\\"how do I do X\\\", \\\"find a skill for X\\\", \\\"is there a skill that can...\\\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill shou…252144 tokens truncated…les\\n\\n| File | Governs |\\n|---|---|\\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Reference Files\\n\\n| File | Governs |\\n|---|---|\\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Quick Reference\\n\\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\\n\\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\\n\\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\\n\\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\\n\\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\\n\\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\\n\\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\\n\\n```\\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\\n  |\\n  v\\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\n  |\\n  v\\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\\n  |\\n  v\\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\\n```\\n\\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\\n\\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\\n\\n\\n## Skills\\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\\n### Available skills\\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \\\"how do I do X\\\", \\\"find a skill for X\\\", \\\"is there a skill that can...\\\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\\n### How to use skills\\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\\n- Coordination and sequencing:\\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\\n- Context hygiene:\\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\\n</INSTRUCTIONS>\"\n./POQ/Payments/Flow5_Planning_refractor.md:124:        \"text\": \"# AGENTS.md instructions for /home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking\\n\\n<INSTRUCTIONS>\\n# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\\n\\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\\n> any output. Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\\n- Coordination and sequencing:\\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\\n- Context hygiene:\\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\",\n./POQ/Search-Catalog/Search-catalog-implementation-RanTests.md:63408:    \"user_instructions\": \"# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\\n\\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Reference Files\\n\\n| File | Governs |\\n|---|---|\\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (planned vs. implemented), critical constraints (no user/order creation, no single-order cancel, no seat locking), workarounds. Read before designing any Eventbrite feature. |\\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Quick Reference\\n\\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\\n\\n| Module | Owns | Eventbrite ACL Facades Used |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | `EbAttendeeService` |\\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `EbOrderService`, `EbAttendeeService`, `EbRefundService` |\\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService` |\\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\\n\\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\\n\\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Razorpay, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\\n\\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\\n\\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (NOT creation — JS SDK widget only)\\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews\\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\\n9. **EbRefundService** — Read refund status only (NO submission API — workaround required)\\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\\n\\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\\n\\n```\\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\\n  |\\n  v\\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\\n  |\\n  v\\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\\n  |\\n  v\\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\\n  |\\n  v\\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\\n  |\\n  v\\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\\n```\\n\\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\\n\\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\\n\\n\\n## Skills\\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\\n### Available skills\\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \\\"how do I do X\\\", \\\"find a skill for X\\\", \\\"is there a skill that can...\\\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\\n### How to use skills\\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\\n- Coordination and sequencing:\\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\\n- Context hygiene:\\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.\",\n"

response = new ErrorResponse(\n            \"INVALID_WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE\",\n            \"Invalid webhook signature\",\n            400,\n            Instant.now(),\n            null\n        );\n     "output": "Chunk ID: 61306e\nWall time: 0.0513 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 608\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.controller;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.request.PaymentConfirmRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.request.PaymentFailedRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.BookingResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.api.dto.response.CheckoutInitResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.service.PaymentService;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.AuthenticatedUser;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.Roles;\nimport jakarta.validation.Valid;\nimport java.util.Map;\nimport org.springframework.security.access.prepost.PreAuthorize;\nimport org.springframework.security.core.Authentication;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.GetMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PathVariable;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.PostMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestBody;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RequestMapping;\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\n\n@RestController\n@RequestMapping(\"/api/v1/payments\")\npublic class PaymentController {\n\n    private final PaymentService paymentService;\n\n    public PaymentController(PaymentService paymentService) {\n        this.paymentService = paymentService;\n    }\n\n    @GetMapping(\"/checkout/{cartId}\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.USER + \"')\")\n    public CheckoutInitResponse getCheckout(Authentication authentication, @PathVariable Long cartId) {\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\n        return paymentService.getCheckout(cartId, user.userId());\n    }\n\n    @PostMapping(\"/confirm\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.USER + \"')\")\n    public BookingResponse confirm(Authentication authentication, @Valid @RequestBody PaymentConfirmRequest request) {\n        AuthenticatedUser user = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\n        return paymentService.confirmPayment(user.userId(), request.paymentIntentId());\n    }\n\n    @PostMapping(\"/failed\")\n    @PreAuthorize(\"hasRole('\" + Roles.USER + \"')\")\n    public Map<String, String> failed(@Valid @RequestBody PaymentFailedRequest request) {\n        paymentService.handleFailure(request.paymentIntentId(), null, \"frontend_reported_failure\");\n        return Map.of(\"message\", \"Seat locks released. Cart cleared.\");\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:27:38.264Z",

    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_HrMypCEergzbfB3Hl4PHxs0I",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 7ace23\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 192\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published;\n\nimport java.util.List;\n\n/**\n * Published by payments-ticketing after Stripe confirms the payment.\n * Consumed by:\n *   - booking-inventory  → transitions seat states to CONFIRMED\n *   - promotions         → creates CouponRedemption keyed by bookingId\n *\n * Rule 4 compliance: contains only primitives, IDs, strings, and value collections.\n *\n * CHANGE (FR7 pre-fix): added {@code bookingId} so promotions can FK-reference\n * the confirmed booking when recording coupon redemptions.\n */\npublic record BookingConfirmedEvent(\n    Long bookingId,\n    Long cartId,\n    List<Long> seatIds,\n    String stripePaymentIntentId,   // was: ebOrderId — now carries the Stripe PaymentIntent ID\n    Long userId\n) {\n}\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 174
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-04T22:27:52.782Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:27:52.782Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_Bo9WphxgwzLwjKuVmfX80lpz",
    "output": "Chunk ID: a2ab1f\nWall time: 0.0523 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 322\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.dto.response.EbOrderResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.exception.EbIntegrationException;\nimport org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Qualifier;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatusCode;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.web.client.RestClient;\n\n@Service\npublic class DefaultEbOrderService implements EbOrderService {\n\n    private final RestClient client;\n\n    public DefaultEbOrderService(@Qualifier(\"ebRestClient\") RestClient client) {\n        this.client = client;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public EbOrderResponse getOrder(String orderId) {\n        try {\n            return client.get()\n                .uri(\"/v3/orders/{orderId}/\", orderId)\n                .retrieve()\n                .onStatus(HttpStatusCode::isError, (req, resp2) -> {\n                    throw new EbIntegrationException(\"EB getOrder failed: \" + resp2.getStatusCode());\n                })\n                .body(EbOrderResponse.class);\n        } catch (EbIntegrationException ex) {\n            throw ex;\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            throw new EbIntegrationException(\"EB getOrder error: \" + ex.getMessage(), ex);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

{

  "type": "response_item",

    "output": "Chunk ID: db1b8f\nWall time: 0.0520 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 810\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.shared.security;\n\nimport jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;\nimport org.springframework.context.annotation.Bean;\nimport org.springframework.context.annotation.Configuration;\nimport org.springframework.security.authentication.AuthenticationManager;\nimport org.springframework.security.config.annotation.authentication.configuration.AuthenticationConfiguration;\nimport org.springframework.security.config.annotation.method.configuration.EnableMethodSecurity;\nimport org.springframework.security.config.annotation.web.builders.HttpSecurity;\nimport org.springframework.security.config.http.SessionCreationPolicy;\nimport org.springframework.security.crypto.bcrypt.BCryptPasswordEncoder;\nimport org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;\nimport org.springframework.security.web.SecurityFilterChain;\nimport org.springframework.security.web.authentication.UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter;\n\n@Configuration\n@EnableMethodSecurity\npublic class SecurityConfig {\n\n    private final JwtAuthenticationFilter jwtAuthenticationFilter;\n\n    public SecurityConfig(JwtAuthenticationFilter jwtAuthenticationFilter) {\n        this.jwtAuthenticationFilter = jwtAuthenticationFilter;\n    }\n\n    @Bean\n    public SecurityFilterChain securityFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {\n        http\n            .csrf(csrf -> csrf.disable())\n            .sessionManagement(session -> session.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))\n            .exceptionHandling(ex -> ex\n                .authenticationEntryPoint((request, response, authException) ->\n                    response.sendError(HttpServletResponse.SC_UNAUTHORIZED, \"Unauthorized\"))\n                .accessDeniedHandler((request, response, accessDeniedException) ->\n                    response.sendError(HttpServletResponse.SC_FORBIDDEN, \"Forbidden\"))\n            )\n            .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth\n                .requestMatchers(\n                    \"/api/v1/catalog/**\",\n                    \"/api/v1/booking/slots/*/seats\",          // public seat map view (no auth needed)\n                    \"/api/v1/booking/slots/*/availability\",   // public GA availability check\n                    \"/api/v1/admin/orgs/*/eventbrite/callback\",\n                    \"/admin/v1/webhooks/eventbrite\",\n                    \"/api/v1/webhooks/stripe\",\n                    \"/api/v1/auth/register\",\n                    \"/api/v1/auth/login\",\n                    \"/api/v1/auth/token/refresh\",\n                    \"/api/v1/auth/password/reset\",\n                    \"/api/v1/auth/password/reset/confirm\",\n                    \"/internal/**\"                            // server-to-server internal module calls\n                ).permitAll()\n                .anyRequest().authenticated()\n            )\n            .addFilterBefore(jwtAuthenticationFilter, UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.class);\n        return http.build();\n    }\n\n    @Bean\n    public PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder() {\n        return new BCryptPasswordEncoder();\n    }\n\n    @Bean\n    public AuthenticationManager authenticationManager(AuthenticationConfiguration configuration) throws Exception {\n        return configuration.getAuthenticationManager();\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:28:16.655Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_eaWkPm5U861JUkfbLb73dje1",
    "output": "Chunk ID: fc38df\nWall time: 0.0511 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1332\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.paymentsticketing.domain.enums.BookingStatus;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Column;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Entity;\nimport jakarta.persistence.EnumType;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Enumerated;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Table;\nimport java.time.Instant;\n\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"bookings\")\npublic class Booking extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @Column(name = \"booking_ref\", nullable = false, unique = true)\n    private String bookingRef;\n\n    @Column(name = \"cart_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long cartId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"user_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long userId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"event_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long eventId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"slot_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long slotId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"org_id\")\n    private Long orgId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"slot_start_time\")\n    private Instant slotStartTime;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"status\", nullable = false)\n    private BookingStatus status;\n\n    @Column(name = \"stripe_payment_intent_id\")\n    private String stripePaymentIntentId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"stripe_charge_id\")\n    private String stripeChargeId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"total_amount\", nullable = false)\n    private Long totalAmount;\n\n    @Column(name = \"currency\", nullable = false)\n    private String currency;\n\n    protected Booking() {\n    }\n\n    public Booking(\n        String bookingRef,\n        Long cartId,\n        Long userId,\n        Long eventId,\n        Long slotId,\n        Long orgId,\n        Instant slotStartTime,\n        Long totalAmount,\n        String currency\n    ) {\n        this.bookingRef = bookingRef;\n        this.cartId = cartId;\n        this.userId = userId;\n        this.eventId = eventId;\n        this.slotId = slotId;\n        this.orgId = orgId;\n        this.slotStartTime = slotStartTime;\n        this.totalAmount = totalAmount;\n        this.currency = currency;\n        this.status = BookingStatus.PENDING;\n    }\n\n    public Booking(String bookingRef, Long cartId, Long userId, Long eventId, Long slotId, Long totalAmount, String currency) {\n        this(bookingRef, cartId, userId, eventId, slotId, null, null, totalAmount, currency);\n    }\n\n    public void confirm(String stripePaymentIntentId, String stripeChargeId) {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be confirmed from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.stripePaymentIntentId = stripePaymentIntentId;\n        this.stripeChargeId = stripeChargeId;\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n    }\n\n    public void markCancellationPending() {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CONFIRMED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot enter cancellation pending from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING;\n    }\n\n    public void revertCancellationPending() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void cancelFromConfirmed() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            return;\n        }\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CONFIRMED && status != BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void updateTotalAmount(Long totalAmount) {\n        this.totalAmount = totalAmount;\n    }\n\n    public void cancelDueToPaymentFailure() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            return;\n        }\n        if (status == BookingStatus.PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CONFIRMED) {\n            this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n            return;\n        }\n        throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n    }\n\n    public String getBookingRef() {\n        return bookingRef;\n    }\n\n    public Long getCartId() {\n        return cartId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getUserId() {\n        return userId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getEventId() {\n        return eventId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getSlotId() {\n        return slotId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getOrgId() {\n        return orgId;\n    }\n\n    public Instant getSlotStartTime() {\n        return slotStartTime;\n    }\n\n    public BookingStatus getStatus() {\n        return status;\n    }\n\n    public String getStripePaymentIntentId() {\n        return stripePaymentIntentId;\n    }\n\n    public String getStripeChargeId() {\n        return stripeChargeId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getTotalAmount() {\n        return totalAmount;\n    }\n\n    public String getCurrency() {\n        return currency;\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

jakarta.persistence.Enumerated;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Table;\nimport java.time.Instant;\n\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"bookings\")\npublic class Booking extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @Column(name = \"booking_ref\", nullable = false, unique = true)\n    private String bookingRef;\n\n    @Column(name = \"cart_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long cartId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"user_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long userId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"event_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long eventId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"slot_id\", nullable = false)\n    private Long slotId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"org_id\")\n    private Long orgId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"slot_start_time\")\n    private Instant slotStartTime;\n\n    @Enumerated(EnumType.STRING)\n    @Column(name = \"status\", nullable = false)\n    private BookingStatus status;\n\n    @Column(name = \"stripe_payment_intent_id\")\n    private String stripePaymentIntentId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"stripe_charge_id\")\n    private String stripeChargeId;\n\n    @Column(name = \"total_amount\", nullable = false)\n    private Long totalAmount;\n\n    @Column(name = \"currency\", nullable = false)\n    private String currency;\n\n    protected Booking() {\n    }\n\n    public Booking(\n        String bookingRef,\n        Long cartId,\n        Long userId,\n        Long eventId,\n        Long slotId,\n        Long orgId,\n        Instant slotStartTime,\n        Long totalAmount,\n        String currency\n    ) {\n        this.bookingRef = bookingRef;\n        this.cartId = cartId;\n        this.userId = userId;\n        this.eventId = eventId;\n        this.slotId = slotId;\n        this.orgId = orgId;\n        this.slotStartTime = slotStartTime;\n        this.totalAmount = totalAmount;\n        this.currency = currency;\n        this.status = BookingStatus.PENDING;\n    }\n\n    public Booking(String bookingRef, Long cartId, Long userId, Long eventId, Long slotId, Long totalAmount, String currency) {\n        this(bookingRef, cartId, userId, eventId, slotId, null, null, totalAmount, currency);\n    }\n\n    public void confirm(String stripePaymentIntentId, String stripeChargeId) {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be confirmed from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.stripePaymentIntentId = stripePaymentIntentId;\n        this.stripeChargeId = stripeChargeId;\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n    }\n\n    public void markCancellationPending() {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CONFIRMED) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot enter cancellation pending from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING;\n    }\n\n    public void revertCancellationPending() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            this.status = BookingStatus.CONFIRMED;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void cancel() {\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void cancelFromConfirmed() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            return;\n        }\n        if (status != BookingStatus.CONFIRMED && status != BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n        }\n        this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n    }\n\n    public void updateTotalAmount(Long totalAmount) {\n        this.totalAmount = totalAmount;\n    }\n\n    public void cancelDueToPaymentFailure() {\n        if (status == BookingStatus.CANCELLED) {\n            return;\n        }\n        if (status == BookingStatus.PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CANCELLATION_PENDING || status == BookingStatus.CONFIRMED) {\n            this.status = BookingStatus.CANCELLED;\n            return;\n        }\n        throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Booking cannot be cancelled from state \" + status, \"INVALID_BOOKING_STATE\");\n    }\n\n    public String getBookingRef() {\n        return bookingRef;\n    }\n\n    public Long getCartId() {\n        return cartId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getUserId() {\n        return userId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getEventId() {\n        return eventId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getSlotId() {\n        return slotId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getOrgId() {\n        return orgId;\n    }\n\n    public Instant getSlotStartTime() {\n        return slotStartTime;\n    }\n\n    public BookingStatus getStatus() {\n        return status;\n    }\n\n    public String getStripePaymentIntentId() {\n        return stripePaymentIntentId;\n    }\n\n    public String getStripeChargeId() {\n        return stripeChargeId;\n    }\n\n    public Long getTotalAmount() {\n        return totalAmount;\n    }\n\n    public String getCurrency() {\n        return currency;\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
```

org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.RestController;\n\n@RestController\n@RequestMapping(\"/api/v1/catalog\")\npublic class EventCatalogController {\n\n    private final EventCatalogService {
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:28:38.640Z",

    "call_id": "call_nF0jgMz0ADWJzaeiiuGUEVJO",
    "output": "Chunk ID: b7a154\nWall time: 0.0508 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2564\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.request.EventCatalogSearchRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.response.EventCatalogItemResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.response.EventCatalogSearchResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.api.dto.response.PaginationInfo;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.EventCatalogItem;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.enums.CatalogSource;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.domain.value.SnapshotPayload;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.exception.CatalogNotFoundException;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.exception.InvalidCatalogSearchException;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.mapper.EventCatalogMapper;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.CityRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.EventCatalogRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.VenueRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.repository.WebhookConfigRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.cache.EventCatalogSnapshotCache;\nimport com.eventplatform.discoverycatalog.service.metrics.EventCatalogMetrics;\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.function.Predicate;\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\nimport org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.Page;\nimport org.springframework.data.domain.PageRequest;\nimport org.springframework.data.jpa.domain.Specification;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\n\n@Service\npublic class EventCatalogService {\n\n    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(EventCatalogService.class);\n    private static final Duration WEBHOOK_STALE_THRESHOLD = Duration.ofHours(1);\n\n    private final EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository;\n    private final CityRepository cityRepository;\n    private final VenueRepository venueRepository;\n    private final WebhookConfigRepository webhookConfigRepository;\n    private final EventCatalogSnapshotCache snapshotCache;\n    private final EventCatalogMapper eventCatalogMapper;\n    private final EventCatalogRefreshService refreshService;\n    private final EventCatalogMetrics metrics;\n\n    public EventCatalogService(\n        EventCatalogRepository eventCatalogRepository,\n        CityRepository cityRepository,\n        VenueRepository venueRepository,\n        WebhookConfigRepository webhookConfigRepository,\n        EventCatalogSnapshotCache snapshotCache,\n        EventCatalogMapper eventCatalogMapper,\n        EventCatalogRefreshService refreshService,\n        EventCatalogMetrics metrics\n    ) {\n        this.eventCatalogRepository = eventCatalogRepository;\n        this.cityRepository = cityRepository;\n        this.venueRepository = venueRepository;\n        this.webhookConfigRepository = webhookConfigRepository;\n        this.snapshotCache = snapshotCache;\n        this.eventCatalogMapper = eventCatalogMapper;\n        this.refreshService = refreshService;\n        this.metrics = metrics;\n    }\n\n    public EventCatalogSearchResponse search(Long organizationId, EventCatalogSearchRequest request) {\n        validateSearch(request);\n        ensureCityExists(request.cityId());\n        if (request.venueId() != null && !venueRepository.existsById(request.venueId())) {\n            throw new CatalogNotFoundException(\"Venue not found: \" + request.venueId());\n        }\n\n        boolean triggeredRefresh = triggerRefreshIfStale(organizationId, request.cityId());\n        CatalogSource source;\n        List<EventCatalogItemResponse> events;\n        Instant snapshotTimestamp = null;\n\n        Optional<SnapshotPayload> snapshotOpt = snapshotCache.getSnapshot(organizationId, request.cityId());\n        if (snapshotOpt.isPresent()) {\n            metrics.incrementCacheHit(organizationId);\n            source = CatalogSource.CACHE;\n            snapshotTimestamp = snapshotOpt.get().snapshotTimestamp();\n            events = applyFilters(snapshotOpt.get().events(), request);\n        } else {\n            metrics.incrementCacheMiss(organizationId);\n            source = CatalogSource.DB;\n            Page<EventCatalogItem> page = eventCatalogRepository.findAll(buildSpecification(organizationId, request),\n                PageRequest.of(request.page(), request.size()));\n            events = page.map(eventCatalogMapper::toResponse).getContent();\n            refreshSnapshotCacheFromDb(organizationId, request.cityId());\n            PaginationInfo pagination = new PaginationInfo(request.page(), request.size(), page.getTotalElements(), page.getTotalPages());\n            return new EventCatalogSearchResponse(events, triggeredRefresh, snapshotTimestamp, source, pagination);\n        }\n\n        PaginationInfo pagination = paginateInfo(events.size(), request.page(), request.size());\n        List<EventCatalogItemResponse> pagedEvents = paginate(events, request.page(), request.size());\n        return new EventCatalogSearchResponse(pagedEvents, triggeredRefresh, snapshotTimestamp, source, pagination);\n    }\n\n    private void validateSearch(EventCatalogSearchRequest request) {\n        if (request.cityId() == null) {\n            throw new InvalidCatalogSearchException(\"cityId is required\");\n        }\n        if (request.size() > 100) {\n            throw new InvalidCatalogSearchException(\"size must be <= 100\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void ensureCityExists(Long cityId) {\n        if (!cityRepository.existsById(cityId)) {\n            throw new CatalogNotFoundException(\"City not found: \" + cityId);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private boolean triggerRefreshIfStale(Long organizationId, Long cityId) {\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        return webhookConfigRepository.findByOrganizationId(organizationId)\n            .map(config -> {\n                Instant lastWebhookAt = config.getLastWebhookAt();\n                if (lastWebhookAt == null || lastWebhookAt.isBefore(now.minus(WEBHOOK_STALE_THRESHOLD))) {\n                    metrics.incrementReadPathRefresh(organizationId);\n                    log.info(\"Read-path refresh triggered. orgId={} cityId={}\", organizationId, cityId);\n                    refreshService.refreshAsync(organizationId, cityId, null);\n                    return true;\n                }\n                return false;\n            })\n            .orElse(false);\n    }\n\n true;\n        if (request.venueId() != null) {\n            predicate = predicate.and(item -> request.venueId().equals(item.venueId()));\n        }\n        if (request.state() != null) {\n predicate.and(item -> item.startTime() != null && item.startTime().isAfter(request.startAfter()));\n        }\n        if (request.startBefore() != null) {\n            predicate = predicate.and(item -> item.startTime() != null && item.startTime().isBefore(request.startBefore()));\n        }\n        return events.stream().filter(predicate).toList();\n    }\n\n    private Specification<EventCatalogItem> buildSpecification(Long organizationId, EventCatalogSearchRequest request) {\n        return (root, query, cb) -> {\n            var predicates = cb.conjunction();\n            predicates = cb.and(predicates, cb.equal(root.get(\"organizationId\"), organizationId));\n            predicates = cb.and(predicates, cb.equal(root.get(\"cityId\"), request.cityId()));\n      if (request.q() != null && !request.q().isBlank()) {\n                predicates = cb.and(predicates, cb.like(cb.lower(root.get(\"name\")), \"%\" + request.q().toLowerCase() + \"%\"));\n            }\n            if (request.startAfter() != null) {\n                predicates = cb.and(predicates, cb.greaterThan(root.get(\"startTime\"), request.startAfter()));\n            }\n            if (request.startBefore() != null) {\n                predicates = cb.and(predicates, cb.lessThan(root.get(\"startTime\"), request.startBefore()));\n            }\n            return predicates;\n        };\n    }\n\n    private PaginationInfo paginateInfo(long totalElements, int page, int size) {\n        int totalPages = size == 0 ? 1 : (int) Math.ceil((double) totalElements / (double) size);\n        return new PaginationInfo(page, size, totalElements, totalPages);\n    }\n\n    private List<EventCatalogItemResponse> paginate(List<EventCatalogItemResponse> events, int page, int size) {\n        if (size == 0) {\n            return events;\n        }\n        int fromIndex = Math.min(page * size, events.size());\n        int toIndex = Math.min(fromIndex + size, events.size());\n        return events.subList(fromIndex, toIndex);\n    }\n\n    private void refreshSnapshotCacheFromDb(Long organizationId, Long cityId) {\n        try {\n            List<EventCatalogItemResponse> allEvents = eventCatalogRepository\n                .findByOrganizationIdAndCityIdAndDeletedAtIsNull(organizationId, cityId)\n                .stream()\n                .map(eventCatalogMapper::toResponse)\n                .toList();\n            snapshotCache.putSnapshot(organizationId, cityId, new SnapshotPayload(allEvents, Instant.now()));\n        } catch (Exception ex) {\n            log.warn(\"Failed to rebuild snapshot cache from DB. orgId={} cityId={}\", organizationId, cityId, ex);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"

{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-04T22:28:49.167Z",

    "output": "Chunk ID: dea76f\nWall time: 0.0506 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2549\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.identity.service;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.LoginRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.PasswordChangeRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.PasswordResetConfirmRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.request.RegisterRequest;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.AccessTokenResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.AuthTokensResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.MessageResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.RegisterResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.api.dto.response.UserProfileResponse;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.PasswordResetToken;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.RefreshToken;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.User;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.domain.UserWallet;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.exception.IdentityException;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.exception.UserNotFoundException;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.mapper.UserMapper;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.PasswordResetTokenRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.RefreshTokenRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.identity.repository.UserWalletRepository;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.ValidationException;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.JwtTokenProvider;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.security.SecurityConstants;\nimport java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;\nimport java.security.MessageDigest;\nimport java.security.NoSuchAlgorithmException;\nimport java.security.SecureRandom;\nimport java.time.Instant;\nimport java.util.Base64;\nimport java.util.Locale;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport org.springframework.http.HttpStatus;\nimport org.springframework.security.crypto.password.PasswordEncoder;\nimport org.springframework.stereotype.Service;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.annotation.Transactional;\n\n@Service\npublic class AuthService {\n\n    private static final SecureRandom SECURE_RANDOM = new SecureRandom();\n\n    private final UserRepository userRepository;\n    private final UserWalletRepository userWalletRepository;\n    private final RefreshTokenRepository refreshTokenRepository;\n    private final PasswordResetTokenRepository passwordResetTokenRepository;\n    private final PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder;\n    private final JwtTokenProvider jwtTokenProvider;\n    private final UserMapper userMapper;\n\n    public AuthService(\n        UserRepository userRepository,\n        UserWalletRepository userWalletRepository,\n        RefreshTokenRepository refreshTokenRepository,\n        PasswordResetTokenRepository passwordResetTokenRepository,\n        PasswordEncoder passwordEncoder,\n        JwtTokenProvider jwtTokenProvider,\n        UserMapper userMapper\n    ) {\n        this.userRepository = userRepository;\n        this.userWalletRepository = userWalletRepository;\n        this.refreshTokenRepository = refreshTokenRepository;\n        this.passwordResetTokenRepository = passwordResetTokenRepository;\n        this.passwordEncoder = passwordEncoder;\n        this.jwtTokenProvider = jwtTokenProvider;\n        this.userMapper = userMapper;\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public RegisterResponse register(RegisterRequest request) {\n        request.validateNoUnknownFields();\n\n        String normalizedEmail = normalizeEmail(request.getEmail());\n        if (userRepository.existsByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizedEmail)) {\n            throw new IdentityException(\"Email already in use\", \"DUPLICATE_EMAIL\", HttpStatus.CONFLICT);\n        }\n\n        User user = new User(normalizedEmail, passwordEncoder.encode(request.getPassword()));\n        User savedUser = userRepository.save(user);\n        userWalletRepository.save(new UserWallet(savedUser));\n        return userMapper.toRegisterResponse(savedUser);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public AuthTokensResponse login(LoginRequest request) {\n        User user = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizeEmail(request.email()))\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new IdentityException(\"Invalid credentials\", \"INVALID_CREDENTIALS\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED));\n\n        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.password(), user.getPasswordHash())) {\n            throw new IdentityException(\"Invalid credentials\", \"INVALID_CREDENTIALS\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED);\n        }\n\n        String accessToken = jwtTokenProvider.generateToken(user.getId(), user.getRole(), null, user.getEmail());\n        String refreshTokenRaw = generateOpaqueToken();\n        String refreshTokenHash = hashToken(refreshTokenRaw);\n\n        RefreshToken refreshToken = new RefreshToken(\n            user,\n            refreshTokenHash,\n            Instant.now().plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_S)\n        );\n        refreshTokenRepository.save(refreshToken);\n\n        return new AuthTokensResponse(accessToken, refreshTokenRaw);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public AccessTokenResponse refreshToken(String refreshTokenRaw) {\n        RefreshToken refreshToken = refreshTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(refreshTokenRaw))\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new IdentityException(\"Refresh token invalid\", \"REFRESH_TOKEN_INVALID\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED));\n\n        if (refreshToken.isRevoked() || refreshToken.isExpired(Instant.now())) {\n            throw new IdentityException(\"Refresh token invalid\", \"REFRESH_TOKEN_INVALID\", HttpStatus.UNAUTHORIZED);\n        }\n\n        String accessToken = jwtTokenProvider.generateToken(refreshToken.getUser().getId(), refreshToken.getUser().getRole(), null, refreshToken.getUser().getEmail());\n        return new AccessTokenResponse(accessToken);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public MessageResponse logout(String refreshTokenRaw) {\n        Optional<RefreshToken> refreshTokenOpt = refreshTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(refreshTokenRaw));\n        refreshTokenOpt.ifPresent(token -> {\n            token.revoke(Instant.now());\n            refreshTokenRepository.save(token);\n        });\n        return new MessageResponse(\"Logged out successfully\");\n    }\n\n    @Transactional(readOnly = true)\n    public UserProfileResponse me(Long userId) {\n        User user = userRepository.findById(userId).orElseThrow(() -> new UserNotFoundException(userId));\n        return userMapper.toProfileResponse(user);\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public MessageResponse changePassword(Long userId, PasswordChangeRequest request) {\n        User user = userRepository.findById(userId).orElseThrow(() -> new UserNotFoundException(userId));\n\n        if (!passwordEncoder.matches(request.currentPassword(), user.getPasswordHash())) {\n            throw new ValidationException(\"Current password incorrect\", \"CURRENT_PASSWORD_INCORRECT\");\n        }\n\n        if (!request.newPassword().equals(request.confirmPassword())) {\n            throw new ValidationException(\"Password mismatch\", \"PASSWORD_MISMATCH\");\n        }\n\n        user.changePassword(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));\n        userRepository.save(user);\n        return new MessageResponse(\"Password changed successfully\");\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public MessageResponse requestPasswordReset(String email) {\n        Optional<User> userOptional = userRepository.findByEmailIgnoreCase(normalizeEmail(email));\n        if (userOptional.isPresent()) {\n            User user = userOptional.get();\n            passwordResetTokenRepository.invalidateActiveByUserId(user.getId(), Instant.now());\n            PasswordResetToken token = new PasswordResetToken(\n                user,\n                hashToken(generateOpaqueToken()),\n                Instant.now().plusSeconds(SecurityConstants.RESET_TOKEN_TTL_S)\n            );\n            passwordResetTokenRepository.save(token);\n        }\n\n        return new MessageResponse(\"Password reset token generated\");\n    }\n\n    @Transactional\n    public MessageResponse confirmPasswordReset(PasswordResetConfirmRequest request) {\n        if (!request.newPassword().equals(request.confirmPassword())) {\n            throw new ValidationException(\"Password mismatch\", \"PASSWORD_MISMATCH\");\n        }\n\n        PasswordResetToken token = passwordResetTokenRepository.findByTokenHash(hashToken(request.token()))\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new ValidationException(\"Reset token invalid\", \"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\"));\n\n        Instant now = Instant.now();\n        if (token.isConsumed() || token.isExpired(now)) {\n            throw new ValidationException(\"Reset token invalid\", \"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\");\n        }\n\n        PasswordResetToken latestToken = passwordResetTokenRepository.findTopByUserIdOrderByCreatedAtDesc(token.getUser().getId())\n            .orElseThrow(() -> new ValidationException(\"Reset token invalid\", \"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\"));\n\n        if (!latestToken.getId().equals(token.getId())) {\n            throw new ValidationException(\"Reset token invalid\", \"RESET_TOKEN_INVALID\");\n        }\n\n        User user = token.getUser();\n        user.changePassword(passwordEncoder.encode(request.newPassword()));\n        userRepository.save(user);\n\n        token.consume(now);\n        passwordResetTokenRepository.save(token);\n\n        return new MessageResponse(\"Password reset successfully\");\n    }\n\n    private String normalizeEmail(String email) {\n        return email.trim().toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT);\n    }\n\n    private String generateOpaqueToken() {\n        byte[] bytes = new byte[32];\n        SECURE_RANDOM.nextBytes(bytes);\n        return Base64.getUrlEncoder().withoutPadding().encodeToString(bytes);\n    }\n\n    private String hashToken(String token) {\n        try {\n            MessageDigest digest = MessageDigest.getInstance(\"SHA-256\");\n            byte[] encoded = digest.digest(token.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));\n            StringBuilder builder = new StringBuilder(encoded.length * 2);\n            for (byte b : encoded) {\n                builder.append(String.format(\"%02x\", b));\n            }\n            return builder.toString();\n        } catch (NoSuchAlgorithmException ex) {\n            throw new IllegalStateException(\"SHA-256 algorithm unavailable\", ex);\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.BusinessRuleException;\nimport jakarta.persistence.CascadeType;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Column;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Entity;\nimport jakarta.persistence.FetchType;\nimport jakarta.persistence.JoinColumn;\nimport jakarta.persistence.ManyToOne;\nimport jakarta.persistence.OneToMany;\nimport jakarta.persistence.OneToOne;\nimport jakarta.persistence.Table;\nimport java.time.LocalDate;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.HashSet;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Set;\n\n@Entity\n@Table(name = \"user_settings\")\npublic class UserSettings extends BaseEntity {\n\n    @OneToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY, optional = false)\n    @JoinColumn(name = \"user_id\", nullable = false)\n    private User user;\n\n    @Column(name = \"full_name\")\n    private String fullName;\n\n    @Column(name = \"phone\")\n    private String phone;\n\n    @Column(name = \"dob\")\n    private LocalDate dob;\n\n    @Column(name = \"address\")\n    private String address;\n\n    @ManyToOne(fetch = FetchType.LAZY)\n    @JoinColumn(name = \"preferred_city_option_id\")\n    private PreferenceOption preferredCityOption;\n\n    @Column(name = \"notification_opt_in\", nullable = false)\n    private boolean notificationOptIn;\n\n    @OneToMany(mappedBy = \"userSettings\", cascade = CascadeType.ALL, orphanRemoval = true)\n    private final List<UserGenrePreference> genrePreferences = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    protected UserSettings() {\n    }\n\n    public UserSettings(User user) {\n        this.user = user;\n        this.notificationOptIn = true;\n    }\n\n    public User getUser() {\n        return user;\n    }\n\n    public String getFullName() {\n        return fullName;\n    }\n\n    public String getPhone() {\n        return phone;\n    }\n\n    public LocalDate getDob() {\n        return dob;\n    }\n\n    public String getAddress() {\n        return address;\n    }\n\n    public PreferenceOption getPreferredCityOption() {\n        return preferredCityOption;\n    }\n\n    public boolean isNotificationOptIn() {\n        return notificationOptIn;\n    }\n\n    public List<UserGenrePreference> getGenrePreferences() {\n        return genrePreferences;\n    }\n\n    public void updateProfile(\n        String fullName,\n        String phone,\n        LocalDate dob,\n        String address,\n        PreferenceOption preferredCityOption,\n        boolean notificationOptIn\n    ) {\n        if (preferredCityOption == null) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n        }\n        this.fullName = fullName;\n        this.phone = phone;\n        this.dob = dob;\n        this.address = address;\n        this.preferredCityOption = preferredCityOption;\n        this.notificationOptIn = notificationOptIn;\n    }\n\n    public void replaceGenrePreferences(List<PreferenceOption> genreOptions) {\n        if (genreOptions.size() > 3) {\n            throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n        }\n        Set<Long> uniqueIds = new HashSet<>();\n        for (PreferenceOption option : genreOptions) {\n            Long optionId = option.getId();\n            if (optionId != null && !uniqueIds.add(optionId)) {\n                throw new BusinessRuleException(\"Invalid preference\", \"INVALID_PREFERENCE\");\n            }\n        }\n        genrePreferences.clear();\n        for (PreferenceOption genreOption : genreOptions) {\n            genrePreferences.add(new UserGenrePreference(this, genreOption));\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}
