    "current_date": "2026-03-05",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "auto",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (FR1–FR7 implemented), critical constraints (no user creation, no seat locking; single-order cancel/refunds are handled via `StripeRefundService`). Read before designing any Eventbrite-touching feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | External ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | — |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, `StripeWebhookHandler` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Stripe, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (admin/ reporting only — NOT used in FR5/FR6 payment flows; orders are created internally in `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews (engagement only — FR8 attendance verification)\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — INACTIVE for payments — all refunds go through `StripeRefundService`; original EB refund read-only API has no submission endpoint\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Stripe ACL Facades (payments-ticketing only)\n\nAll 3 facades are in `shared/stripe/service/`. Only `payments-ticketing` calls these:\n\n1. **StripePaymentService** — Create PaymentIntents; confirm and capture payments\n2. **StripeRefundService** — Submit refunds; query refund status\n3. **StripeWebhookHandler** — Verify webhook signatures; parse `payment_intent.succeeded` / `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `charge.refunded` events\n\n**Hard Rule:** Never call `com.stripe.*` SDK classes directly from a module service — always delegate to the facade in `shared/stripe/service/`.\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 644
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:35:04.081Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:35:04.081Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_3uDyJNvQ0DfykATjmjxW5mCI",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 1b5dce\nWall time: 0.0517 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1600\nOutput:\n# TEST_AGENT.md — Testing Standards & Execution Agent\n\n> Read this file before writing or running tests.\n> This agent is responsible for test code implementation, execution, failure fixing, and result logging.\n\n---\n\n## Purpose\n\nThis file defines the responsibilities of the **Test Agent**.\n\nThe **SPEC_GENERATOR** is the sole source of truth for what to test — it writes all test cases in Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file. The **TEST_AGENT does not decide what to test**. Its only job is to:\n\n- **Translate** the test cases written in `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8 into executable test code\n- **Run** those tests\n- **Fix** failures caused by new or changed code\n- **Log** results in a timestamped report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Scope Ownership\n\n### Test Agent MUST\n\n- Read Stage 8 of the `SPEC_*.md` file and implement exactly those test cases — nothing more, nothing less\n- Write test code for all layers as specified in the test cases:\n  - `domain/` (pure unit)\n  - `service/` (Mockito orchestration)\n  - `api/` (`@WebMvcTest` HTTP contract)\n  - `arch/` (ArchUnit boundaries)\n- Run relevant tests after implementation\n- Fix failing tests and related test setup issues\n- Fix production code only when required to satisfy approved spec behavior\n- Log all found errors and all applied fixes in a test report file\n\n### Test Agent MUST NOT\n\n- Decide what test cases to write — that is SPEC_GENERATOR's job\n- Add, remove, or modify test cases from Stage 8\n- Introduce unapproved features not present in `SPEC_*.md`\n- Change module ownership or dependency direction rules\n- Add module-level `@ControllerAdvice`\n- Skip failed tests without documenting reason and impact\n\n---\n\n## Inputs (Required Before Writing Tests)\n\n1. Confirmed feature spec with Stage 8 test cases: `SPEC_*.md` ← **primary input**\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then full module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Known API Contract Changes (FR1–FR7) — Do Not Revert\n\nThe following contract changes were applied during the FR1–FR7 implementation cycle. Any test that references the old shape will fail. Fix the test — do not revert the production code.\n\n| Change | Old | New | Affected Test Files |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `CartResponse` discount field | `discountAmount` | `groupDiscountAmount` + `couponDiscountAmount` | `BookingInventoryControllerTest`, `CartServiceTest` |\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` first param | `(cartId, seatIds, ...)` | `(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, ...)` | `PaymentServiceTest` (uses `ArgumentCaptor` — no change needed) |\n| `CartSnapshotReader` | single `getCartItems()` | `getCartItems()` + `getCartSummary()` | Any test mocking `CartSnapshotReader` must stub both methods |\n| Event listener annotation | `@EventListener` | `@TransactionalEventListener(phase = AFTER_COMMIT)` on post-commit side-effects | Any listener test using `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` must use `@Transactional` test config |\n| `ShowSlotService.toEbCreateRequest()` | uses internal `venueId.toString()` | uses `venue.eventbriteVenueId()` from `venueCatalogClient.getVenue()` | `ShowSlotServiceTest` — must stub `venueCatalogClient.getVenue()` |\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All Stage 8 test cases from `SPEC_*.md` are implemented as code\n- All implemented tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test — writes all test cases in Stage 8\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` writes test code for Stage 8 cases, runs them, fixes failures, and publishes the final test report"
  }
}
```

Confirmed feature spec with Stage 8 test cases: `SPEC_*.md` ← **primary input**\n2. Module ownership and boundaries: `PRODUCT.md`\n3. Layer/package standards: `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md`\n4. Test patterns/rules: `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md`\n5. Coding constraints for compatibility: `agents/CODING_AGENT.md`\n\n---\n\n## Test Design Standards\n\n### AAA Format (Mandatory)\n\nAll tests must follow Arrange–Act–Assert.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_return_400_when_cityId_missing() {\n    // Arrange\n    MockHttpServletRequestBuilder request = get(\"/api/v1/catalog/venues\");\n\n    // Act\n    ResultActions result = mockMvc.perform(request);\n\n    // Assert\n    result.andExpect(status().isBadRequest());\n}\n```\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`should_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]`\n\nExamples:\n- `should_soft_delete_event_when_missing_from_org_sync`\n- `should_return_401_when_webhook_signature_invalid`\n- `should_not_query_db_when_cache_hit`\n\n### Layer Rules\n\n- `domain/`: no Spring context, no mocks for infra\n- `service/`: Mockito only (`@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)`)\n- `api/`: `@WebMvcTest`, services/mappers mocked\n- `arch/`: ArchUnit boundary checks based on hard rules\n\n---\n\n## Execution Workflow\n\n1. Read Stage 8 test cases from the approved `SPEC_*.md` — do not interpret or expand them\n2. Implement each test case as code in the correct layer\n3. Run focused tests first (changed files only), then full module suite\n4. Capture failures\n5. Fix test or code issues\n6. Re-run until green (or blocked)\n7. Produce and save test report in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Failure Triage Rules\n\nWhen tests fail, classify and resolve in this order:\n\n1. **Test setup issue** (mock, fixture, context) → fix test\n2. **Contract mismatch** (spec vs endpoint/DTO) → fix code or test to match approved spec\n3. **Behavior bug** (business logic wrong) → fix production code\n4. **Architecture violation** (ArchUnit) → fix package/dependency layering\n5. **External/infra flake** → document as blocked with reproducible evidence\n\nDo not silently ignore failing tests.\n\n---\n\n## Required Result Logging\n\nAfter every test cycle, generate a result log file in:\n\n`test_result_folder/`\n\n### Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\n`[feature_or_flow]_[yyyyMMdd_HHmmss].md`\n\nExamples:\n- `fr1_catalog_search_20260303_201530.md`\n- `payment_confirmation_flow_20260303_204012.md`\n\n### Log Template (Mandatory)\n\n```markdown\n# Test Result — [Feature/Flow]\n\n- Timestamp: [yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss]\n- Spec: [SPEC_*.md]\n- Module(s): [module list]\n- Commit/Working State: [hash or local]\n\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\n- Total planned:\n- Implemented:\n- Passed:\n- Failed:\n- Skipped:\n\n## Failures Found\n1. [test name]\n   - Error:\n   - Root cause:\n   - Fix applied:\n\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\n- [file path] — [short change summary]\n\n## Final Status\n- [PASS | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]\n- Notes:\n```\n\n---\n\n## Known API Contract Changes (FR1–FR7) — Do Not Revert\n\nThe following contract changes were applied during the FR1–FR7 implementation cycle. Any test that references the old shape will fail. Fix the test — do not revert the production code.\n\n| Change | Old | New | Affected Test Files |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `CartResponse` discount field | `discountAmount` | `groupDiscountAmount` + `couponDiscountAmount` | `BookingInventoryControllerTest`, `CartServiceTest` |\n| `BookingConfirmedEvent` first param | `(cartId, seatIds, ...)` | `(bookingId, cartId, seatIds, ...)` | `PaymentServiceTest` (uses `ArgumentCaptor` — no change needed) |\n| `CartSnapshotReader` | single `getCartItems()` | `getCartItems()` + `getCartSummary()` | Any test mocking `CartSnapshotReader` must stub both methods |\n| Event listener annotation | `@EventListener` | `@TransactionalEventListener(phase = AFTER_COMMIT)` on post-commit side-effects | Any listener test using `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` must use `@Transactional` test config |\n| `ShowSlotService.toEbCreateRequest()` | uses internal `venueId.toString()` | uses `venue.eventbriteVenueId()` from `venueCatalogClient.getVenue()` | `ShowSlotServiceTest` — must stub `venueCatalogClient.getVenue()` |\n\n---\n\n## Completion Criteria\n\nTest Agent is complete only when:\n\n- All Stage 8 test cases from `SPEC_*.md` are implemented as code\n- All implemented tests are executed\n- Failures are fixed or explicitly documented as blocked\n- A timestamped result log is saved in `test_result_folder/`\n\n---\n\n## Handoff Contract\n\n- `SPEC_GENERATOR` defines what to test — writes all test cases in Stage 8\n- `CODING_AGENT` implements production code\n- `TEST_AGENT` writes test code for Stage 8 cases, runs them, fixes failures, and publishes the final test report"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:35:04.088Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_lVwjR6LqDOkqZyWNh71jjRWZ",
    "output": "Chunk ID: f56d25\nWall time: 0.0518 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2631\nOutput:\n# Testing Guide — Modular Monolith\n\n> Tests are not optional. Every feature must have coverage across all four layers.\n> The `arch/` tests in particular are self-enforcing — they fail the build automatically\n> if anyone violates a module boundary. Never delete or weaken an arch test.\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle — Each Layer Tests One Thing\n\nThe most common testing mistake is putting the wrong test in the wrong layer.\nUse this rule: **each layer tests exactly one concern.**\n\n| Layer | Tests One Concern | Spring Context? | Key Annotation |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `domain/` | Business rules on the domain object | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n| `service/` | Orchestration — right calls in right order | None | `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` |\n| `api/` | HTTP contract — status codes, JSON shape, validation | Web layer only | `@WebMvcTest` |\n| `arch/` | Architectural boundaries | None | `@AnalyzeClasses` |\n| `mapper/` | Field translation correctness | None | Plain JUnit 5 |\n\n---\n\n## Test Folder Structure\n\nEvery module must mirror its source structure exactly:\n\n```\n[module-name]/\n└── src/\n    ├── main/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n    │   ├── api/controller/\n    │   ├── domain/\n    │   ├── service/\n    │   ├── repository/\n    │   ├── mapper/\n    │   └── event/\n    └── test/java/com/[org]/[module]/\n        ├── domain/        ← Pure unit tests. No Spring. No infrastructure mocks.\n        ├── service/       ← Mockito only. Mocks repositories and ACL facades.\n        ├── api/           ← @WebMvcTest slice. Mocks service and mapper.\n        ├── arch/          ← ArchUnit. Enforces module boundary rules at build time.\n        └── mapper/        ← MapStruct translation tests. No Spring.\n```\n\n---\n\n## Test Naming Convention (Mandatory)\n\nAll test methods must follow this exact pattern:\n\n```\nshould_[expected_behaviour]_when_[condition]\n```\n\nThe name must be readable as a sentence describing the behaviour being verified.\nNever use vague names like `testConfirm()`, `test1()`, or `happyPath()`.\n\n**Good examples:**\n```\nshould_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending\nshould_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking\nshould_return_200_when_booking_is_found\nshould_return_400_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum\nshould_return_404_when_booking_does_not_exist\nshould_publish_BookingConfirmedEvent_after_successful_confirmation\nshould_throw_BookingNotFoundException_when_booking_id_is_unknown\nshould_map_confirmed_booking_to_response_with_correct_status\n```\n\n**Bad examples (never use):**\n```\ntestConfirm()\ntest_happy_path()\nconfirmWorks()\nbooking_test_1()\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 1 — `domain/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the domain object enforces its own rules correctly.\nThese are the fastest tests in the suite — they must run in milliseconds.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- Valid state transitions succeed and produce the correct new state\n- Invalid state transitions throw `BusinessRuleException` with the correct message\n- Guard conditions (preconditions on methods) are enforced\n- Value object construction validates its invariants\n- Terminal states cannot be transitioned out of\n- Null or invalid inputs are rejected\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Persistence (no repositories, no DB)\n- HTTP (no controllers, no MockMvc)\n- Spring beans (no `@SpringBootTest`, no `@ExtendWith(SpringExtension.class)`)\n- Mocks of infrastructure — if you need a mock here, the domain has an infrastructure\n  dependency. Fix the domain, not the test.\n\n### Annotations\n\nNone. Plain JUnit 5 only.\n\n```java\n// ✅ Correct — no annotations on the class\nclass BookingTest {\n    ...\n}\n\n// ❌ Wrong — domain tests never load Spring\n@SpringBootTest\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n\n// ❌ Wrong — if you need this, your domain imports infrastructure\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\nclass BookingTest { ... }\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest that the domain method succeeds and produces the correct outcome under valid conditions.\nCover every valid state transition defined in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Happy path: valid transition succeeds\n    @Test\n    void should_confirm_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        // Arrange — set up the entity in a valid starting state\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        // Act — call the domain method\n        booking.confirm();\n\n        // Assert — verify the expected outcome\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_pending() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_cancel_booking_when_status_is_confirmed_and_event_is_more_than_48h_away() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusDays(3);\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        booking.cancel();\n\n        assertThat(booking.getStatus()).isEqualTo(BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Negative Path Tests\n\nTest that invalid operations are rejected with the correct exception and message.\nCover every forbidden transition in the spec's state machine.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    // Negative path: invalid transition throws BusinessRuleException\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_a_cancelled_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot confirm a cancelled booking\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_confirming_an_already_confirmed_booking() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Booking is already confirmed\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_BusinessRuleException_when_cancelling_within_48h_of_event() {\n        LocalDateTime eventTime = LocalDateTime.now().plusHours(24); // inside the 48h window\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CONFIRMED, eventTime);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel)\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot cancel within 48 hours of the event\");\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Exception / Edge Case Tests\n\nTest boundary conditions, null inputs, minimum/maximum values, and terminal states.\n\n```java\nclass BookingTest {\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_booking_id_is_null() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(null, BookingStatus.PENDING))\n            .isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_seat_count_exceeds_maximum() {\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.PENDING, 9))\n            .isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class)\n            .hasMessageContaining(\"Cannot book more than 8 seats\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void should_throw_when_operating_on_a_terminal_cancelled_state() {\n        Booking booking = new Booking(UUID.randomUUID(), BookingStatus.CANCELLED);\n\n        // All operations on a terminal state must be rejected\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::confirm).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n        assertThatThrownBy(booking::cancel).isInstanceOf(BusinessRuleException.class);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Layer 2 — `service/` Tests\n\n### Purpose\n\nVerify that the service correctly orchestrates domain objects, repositories, ACL facades,\nand event publishing. These tests prove the *use case* works — not the domain rule, not the\nHTTP contract.\n\n### What to Test Here\n\n- The correct repository method is called with the correct arguments\n- The correct ACL facade method is called when an external system is involved\n- `ApplicationEventPublisher.publishEvent()` is called with the correct event type and payload\n- `BookingNotFoundException` (or equivalent) is thrown when the entity is not found\n- `BusinessRuleException` propagates correctly when the domain rejects an operation\n- `[System]IntegrationException` is thrown (and compensation is triggered) when an external call fails\n- No event is published when the use case fails\n\n### What NOT to Test Here\n\n- Domain rules — those are tested in `domain/`. Do not re-test `booking.confirm()` logic here.\n- HTTP concerns — no MockMvc, no status codes.\n\n### Annotations\n\n```java\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) // Mockito only — no Spring\nclass BookingServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private BookingRepository bookingRepository;     // Always mock the repository\n\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher; // Always mock the event publisher\n\n    @Mock\n    private PaymentAclService paymentAclService;      // Mock ACL facades for external systems\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private BookingService bookingService;            // The class under test — real instance\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Happy Path Tests\n\nTest the complete use case: entity is found, domain method succeeds, repository is saved,\nevent is published, ACL facade is called if required.\n\n```java\n@Test\nvoid should_confirm_booking_and_publish_event_when_booking_is_pending() {\n    // Arrange\n    UUID bookingId = UUID.randomUUID();\n    Booking booking = new Booking(bookingId, BookingStatus.PENDING);\n    when(bookingRepository.findById(bookingId)).thenReturn(Optional.of(booking));\n\n    // Act\n    bookingService.confirmBooking(bookingId);\n\n    // Assert — verify orchestration\n    verify(bookingRepository).save(booking);                               // saved\n    verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(BookingConfirmedEvent.class)); // event fired\n}\n\n@Test\nvoid should_initiate_booking_and_call_payment_facade_when_request_is_valid() {\n    // Arrange\n    CreateBookingRequest request = new CreateBookingRequest(userId, seatIds, paymentMethodId);\n    when(paymentAclService.charge(any())).thenReturn(new PaymentResult(paymentIntentId));\n    when(bookingRepository.save(any())).thenAnswer(inv -> inv.getArgument(0));\n\n    // Act\n    Booking result = bookingService.initiateBooking(request);\n\n    // Assert\n"
  }
}

    "output": "Chunk ID: 91cdef\nWall time: 0.0527 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3230\nOutput:\n\n> **Operational note:** Both listeners are idempotent — re-running them with the same event produces a no-op (upsert / conditional update). A reconciliation endpoint `POST /api/v1/internal/engagement/reconcile-eligibility?bookingId={}` can be added for ops use.\n\n---\n\n## Stage 8 — Tests\n\n> **Test structure follows `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` 4-layer model.**  \n> **Location convention:** `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/`\n\n---\n\n### Layer 1 — Domain Unit Tests (`domain/`)\n\n**`ReviewTest.java`**\n\n| Test | Given | When | Then |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `cannotPublishFromSubmitted` | `review.status = SUBMITTED` | Domain method `publish()` called | `IllegalStateException` or guard throws |\n| `canPublishFromApproved` | `review.status = APPROVED` | `publish()` called | `status = PUBLISHED`, `publishedAt` set |\n| `rejectedIsTerminal` | `review.status = REJECTED` | Any status setter called | State unchanged or guarded |\n| `ratingBoundaryValid` | ratings 1 and 5 | Construct `Review` | No exception |\n| `ratingBoundaryInvalid` | rating 0 or 6 | Construct `Review` | `IllegalArgumentException` |\n\n**`ReviewEligibilityTest.java`**\n\n| Test | Given | When | Then |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `revokedCannotBeUnlocked` | `status = REVOKED` | `unlock()` called | Stays `REVOKED` / exception |\n| `expiredOnWindowCheck` | `eligible_until = 1 day ago` | `isEligible(now)` | Returns false |\n| `unlocked_isEligible` | `status = UNLOCKED`, `eligible_until = future` | `isEligible(now)` | Returns true |\n\n---\n\n### Layer 2 — Service Unit Tests (`service/`) — Mockito, no Spring context\n\n**`ReviewServiceTest.java`**\n\n```\nMocks: ReviewEligibilityRepository, ReviewRepository, AttendanceVerificationService, ApplicationEventPublisher\n```\n\n| Test | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|\n| `submitReview_happyPath` | Eligibility UNLOCKED, no existing review, attendance = EB_VERIFIED | Review saved with PENDING_MODERATION, `ModerationRequiredEvent` published |\n| `submitReview_noEligibility` | No `ReviewEligibility` row found | `ReviewNotEligibleException(REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE)` |\n| `submitReview_eligibilityRevoked` | `ReviewEligibility.status = REVOKED` | `ReviewNotEligibleException(REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE)` |\n| `submitReview_windowExpired` | `eligible_until = yesterday` | `ReviewNotEligibleException(REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED)` |\n| `submitReview_duplicateReview` | Existing `Review` for same (userId, eventId) | `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException` |\n| `submitReview_bookingCheckFails` | `AttendanceVerificationService` throws `ReviewNotEligibleException` | Propagated as `ReviewNotEligibleException` |\n| `submitReview_selfReportedAttendance` | EB attendee not found, booking CONFIRMED | Review saved with `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n\n---\n\n**`AttendanceVerificationServiceTest.java`**\n\n```\nMocks: BookingVerificationClient (REST), EbMetadataClient (REST), EbAttendeeService, EbTokenStore\n```\n\n| Test | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|\n| `verify_bookingConfirmed_ebVerified` | Booking CONFIRMED, EB attendee found, not cancelled/refunded | Returns `EB_VERIFIED` |\n| `verify_bookingConfirmed_ebAttendeeNotFound` | Booking CONFIRMED, EB attendee not found | Returns `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| `verify_bookingNotConfirmed` | Booking REST returns `CANCELLED` status | `ReviewNotEligibleException` thrown |\n| `verify_bookingCheckTimeout` | REST call to payments-ticketing times out | `ReviewNotEligibleException` thrown (fail closed) |\n| `verify_ebMetadataTimeout` | discovery-catalog timeout (1s) | `ebEventId = null`, returns `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n| `verify_ebApiException` | `EbAttendeeService` throws exception | Returns `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE`, no exception to caller |\n| `verify_ebAuthException` | `EbTokenStore` throws `EbAuthException` | Returns `ATTENDANCE_EB_UNAVAILABLE`, no exception to caller |\n| `verify_noEbEventId` | `ebEventId` is null (slot not synced) | Skip EB check, return `ATTENDANCE_SELF_REPORTED` |\n\n---\n\n**`ModerationServiceTest.java`**\n\n```\nMocks: ReviewRepository, ModerationRecordRepository, OpenAiModerationService, ApplicationEventPublisher, StringRedisTemplate\n```\n\n| Test | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|\n| `autoModeration_approved` | OpenAI returns `flagged=false` | `ModerationRecord.decision=APPROVED`, `Review.status=PUBLISHED`, `ReviewPublishedEvent` published, Redis invalidated |\n| `autoModeration_rejected` | OpenAI returns `flagged=true`, top flag = `hate` | `ModerationRecord.decision=REJECTED`, `Review.status=REJECTED`, `rejectionReason=\"hate\"` |\n| `autoModeration_apiFailure_firstAttempt` | OpenAI throws `RuntimeException` | `auto_retry_count=1`, `retry_after = now+30s`, `decision=PENDING`, review stays `PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| `autoModeration_apiFailure_thirdAttempt` | `auto_retry_count=2` before call, OpenAI throws | `auto_retry_count=3`, no `retry_after`, metrics counter incremented |\n| `autoModeration_idempotent_alreadyPublished` | `review.status=PUBLISHED` before auto-mod runs | No-op — method returns without any state change |\n| `retryPendingModerations_processesEligibleRecords` | 2 records with `retry_after <= now`, 1 with future | Processes 2, skips 1 |\n| `retryPendingModerations_continuesOnPerRecordFailure` | First record throws, second is valid | Second processed successfully despite first failure |\n| `manualDecision_approve` | Review in `PENDING_MODERATION` | status=PUBLISHED, `ModerationRecord` created with method=MANUAL, event published |\n| `manualDecision_reject` | Review in `PENDING_MODERATION`, `reason` provided | status=REJECTED, `ModerationRecord` created with method=MANUAL |\n| `manualDecision_alreadyModerated` | Review in `PUBLISHED` | `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException` |\n\n---\n\n**`ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java`**\n\n```\nMocks: ReviewRepository, StringRedisTemplate, ObjectMapper\n```\n\n| Test | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|\n| `getSummary_cacheHit` | Redis returns valid JSON | Deserialized `ReviewRatingSummary` returned; no DB call |\n| `getSummary_cacheMiss` | Redis returns null | DB aggregate executed; result cached in Redis |\n| `getSummary_redisGetThrows` | Redis throws exception | Falls through to DB query; result returned without caching |\n| `getSummary_redisSetThrows` | DB succeeds but Redis SET throws | Result returned; WARN logged |\n| `invalidateCache_onPublish` | `ReviewPublishedEvent` received | Redis DEL called for `engagement:review:summary:{eventId}` |\n\n---\n\n### Layer 3 — Controller Tests (`api/`) — `@WebMvcTest`\n\n**`ReviewControllerTest.java`**\n\n| Test | HTTP | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `submitReview_201` | POST `/reviews` | Valid body, mocked service returns success | 201, body contains `reviewId` and `status=PENDING_MODERATION` |\n| `submitReview_403_notEligible` | POST `/reviews` | Service throws `ReviewNotEligibleException` | 403, `errorCode=REVIEW_NOT_ELIGIBLE` |\n| `submitReview_409_duplicate` | POST `/reviews` | Service throws `ReviewAlreadySubmittedException` | 409, `errorCode=REVIEW_ALREADY_SUBMITTED` |\n| `submitReview_400_invalidRating` | POST `/reviews` | `rating=0` | 400, `errorCode=VALIDATION_ERROR` |\n| `submitReview_400_blankTitle` | POST `/reviews` | `title=\"\"` | 400, `errorCode=VALIDATION_ERROR` |\n| `submitReview_401_noJwt` | POST `/reviews` | No Authorization header | 401 |\n| `listReviews_200_public` | GET `/reviews/events/1001` | No auth, valid eventId | 200, paginated `ReviewResponse` list |\n| `listReviews_defaultSort` | GET `/reviews/events/1001` | No `sort` param | Default sort `publishedAt,desc` applied |\n| `getReviewSummary_200` | GET `/reviews/events/1001/summary` | Cache hit in mock | 200, `averageRating`, `distribution` |\n| `getMyReviews_200` | GET `/reviews/me` | Valid JWT | 200, user's reviews with `status` field |\n| `getMyReviews_401_noJwt` | GET `/reviews/me` | No JWT | 401 |\n\n---\n\n**`AdminModerationControllerTest.java`**\n\n| Test | HTTP | Scenario | Expected |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `getAdminQueue_200` | GET `/admin/reviews?status=PENDING_MODERATION` | ROLE_ADMIN | 200 paginated `AdminReviewResponse` |\n| `getAdminQueue_403_notAdmin` | GET `/admin/reviews` | ROLE_USER JWT | 403 |\n| `moderateApprove_200` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | `decision=APPROVE`, ROLE_ADMIN | 200, `newStatus=PUBLISHED` |\n| `moderateReject_200` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | `decision=REJECT`, `reason` provided | 200, `newStatus=REJECTED` |\n| `moderateReject_400_missingReason` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | `decision=REJECT`, no `reason` | 400, `VALIDATION_ERROR` |\n| `moderateReview_409_alreadyModerated` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | Service throws `ReviewAlreadyModeratedException` | 409 |\n| `moderateReview_404_notFound` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | Service throws `ReviewNotFoundException` | 404 |\n| `moderateReview_403_noAdminRole` | PUT `/admin/reviews/56/moderate` | ROLE_USER JWT | 403 |\n\n---\n\n### Layer 4 — Architecture Tests (`arch/`) — ArchUnit\n\n**`EngagementArchTest.java`**\n\n```java\n// Rule 1: No cross-module package imports\nnoClasses()\n    .that().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement..\")\n    .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\n        \"..payments.ticketing..\",\n        \"..booking.inventory..\",\n        \"..identity..\",\n        \"..promotions..\",\n        \"..scheduling..\",\n        \"..discovery.catalog..\"\n    )\n    .because(\"engagement must not import other module's internal classes\");\n\n// Rule 2: Eventbrite calls only via shared ACL\nnoClasses()\n    .that().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement..\")\n    .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAPackage(\"..com.eventbrite..\")\n    .because(\"Eventbrite calls must go through shared/eventbrite ACL facades\");\n\n// Rule 3: OpenAI calls only via shared ACL\nnoClasses()\n    .that().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement..\")\n    .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAPackage(\"..com.theokanning.openai..\")\n    .because(\"OpenAI calls must go through shared/openai ACL facades\");\n\n// Rule 4: No @ControllerAdvice in engagement\nnoClasses()\n    .that().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement..\")\n    .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class)\n    .because(\"Single GlobalExceptionHandler in shared/\");\n\n// Rule 5: Mapping only in mapper layer\nnoClasses()\n    .that().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement.service..\")\n    .or().resideInAPackage(\"..engagement.api.controller..\")\n    .should().callConstructor(ReviewResponse.class)\n    .because(\"Field mapping belongs in mapper/ layer only (MapStruct)\");\n```\n\n---\n\n### Test File Locations\n\n```\nengagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/\n├── domain/\n│   ├── ReviewTest.java\n│   └── ReviewEligibilityTest.java\n├── service/\n│   ├── ReviewServiceTest.java\n│   ├── AttendanceVerificationServiceTest.java\n│   ├── ModerationServiceTest.java\n│   └── ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java\n├── api/\n│   ├── ReviewControllerTest.java\n│   └── AdminModerationControllerTest.java\n└── arch/\n    └── EngagementArchTest.java\n```\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **`eventId` in `BookingConfirmedEvent`** — The event currently carries `bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId`. It does NOT directly carry `eventId`. The `engagement` listener needs `eventId` to create `ReviewEligibility`. Decision options:\n   - (a) Add `eventId` as a field to `BookingConfirmedEvent` in `shared/` — requires updating `payments-ticketing` publisher and all consumers.\n   - (b) `engagement` derives `eventId` by calling `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` at listener time — adds REST latency in `AFTER_COMMIT` listener.\n   - (c) Add `slotId` to `BookingConfirmedEvent` and have `engagement` call `discovery-catalog` to resolve `eventId`.\n   - **Recommended:** Option (a) — add `eventId` directly to `BookingConfirmedEvent` as it is naturally known at booking confirmation time.\n\n2. **Eligibility expiry enforcement** — `REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED` case requires knowing the `eventDate`. Should `ReviewEligibility` store `eventDate` at creation time, or should it call `discovery-catalog` on each submission check?\n\n3. **EB attendee email matching** — The user's email is stored in `identity`. `AttendanceVerificationService` must call `identity` REST to fetch user email before the EB API call, or the user email should be included in `BookingConfirmedEvent`. Which is preferred?\n\n4. **`orgId` for EB token retrieval** — `EbTokenStore.getValidToken(orgId)` requires an `orgId`. The `engagement` listener receives `orgId` from `BookingConfirmedEvent` (already carried). Confirm this field is reliably present.\n\n5. **Reviewer display name privacy** — Should display names be anonymized (e.g., \"Jane D.\" — first name + last initial) or shown in full? This affects `identity`'s internal display name endpoint contract.\n\n6. **`OpenAiModerationService`** — Does this facade already exist in `shared/openai/service/`? If not, it must be created as part of FR8 pre-work before implementation begins.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n"
  }
}
```

(MapStruct)\");\n```\n\n---\n\n### Test File Locations\n\n```\nengagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/\n├── domain/\n│   ├── ReviewTest.java\n│   └── ReviewEligibilityTest.java\n├── service/\n│   ├── ReviewServiceTest.java\n│   ├── AttendanceVerificationServiceTest.java\n│   ├── ModerationServiceTest.java\n│   └── ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java\n├── api/\n│   ├── ReviewControllerTest.java\n│   └── AdminModerationControllerTest.java\n└── arch/\n    └── EngagementArchTest.java\n```\n\n---\n\n## Open Questions\n\n1. **`eventId` in `BookingConfirmedEvent`** — The event currently carries `bookingId, cartId, seatIds, stripePaymentIntentId, userId`. It does NOT directly carry `eventId`. The `engagement` listener needs `eventId` to create `ReviewEligibility`. Decision options:\n   - (a) Add `eventId` as a field to `BookingConfirmedEvent` in `shared/` — requires updating `payments-ticketing` publisher and all consumers.\n   - (b) `engagement` derives `eventId` by calling `GET /api/v1/scheduling/slots/{slotId}` at listener time — adds REST latency in `AFTER_COMMIT` listener.\n   - (c) Add `slotId` to `BookingConfirmedEvent` and have `engagement` call `discovery-catalog` to resolve `eventId`.\n   - **Recommended:** Option (a) — add `eventId` directly to `BookingConfirmedEvent` as it is naturally known at booking confirmation time.\n\n2. **Eligibility expiry enforcement** — `REVIEW_WINDOW_CLOSED` case requires knowing the `eventDate`. Should `ReviewEligibility` store `eventDate` at creation time, or should it call `discovery-catalog` on each submission check?\n\n3. **EB attendee email matching** — The user's email is stored in `identity`. `AttendanceVerificationService` must call `identity` REST to fetch user email before the EB API call, or the user email should be included in `BookingConfirmedEvent`. Which is preferred?\n\n4. **`orgId` for EB token retrieval** — `EbTokenStore.getValidToken(orgId)` requires an `orgId`. The `engagement` listener receives `orgId` from `BookingConfirmedEvent` (already carried). Confirm this field is reliably present.\n\n5. **Reviewer display name privacy** — Should display names be anonymized (e.g., \"Jane D.\" — first name + last initial) or shown in full? This affects `identity`'s internal display name endpoint contract.\n\n6. **`OpenAiModerationService`** — Does this facade already exist in `shared/openai/service/`? If not, it must be created as part of FR8 pre-work before implementation begins.\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n\n"
  }
}
```

    "call_id": "call_DJf6bosrnH9tyfofsSY9EuLY",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 798681\nWall time: 0.0514 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 920\nOutput:\npackage com.eventplatform.promotions.arch;\n\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.classes;\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.noClasses;\n\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.BookingCancelledEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.BookingConfirmedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.CartAssembledEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.CouponAppliedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.CouponValidatedEvent;\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.PaymentFailedEvent;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.AnalyzeClasses;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.ArchTest;\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.lang.ArchRule;\nimport java.lang.reflect.Method;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\nimport org.springframework.transaction.event.TransactionalEventListener;\n\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \"com.eventplatform.promotions\")\nclass PromotionsArchTest {\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule promotions_module_should_not_import_any_other_module_service_or_repository =\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\"..promotions..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..bookinginventory.service..\",\n                \"..bookinginventory.repository..\",\n                \"..paymentsticketing.service..\",\n                \"..paymentsticketing.repository..\",\n                \"..identity.service..\",\n                \"..identity.repository..\",\n                \"..scheduling.service..\",\n                \"..scheduling.repository..\"\n            );\n\n    @ArchTest\n    static final ArchRule promotions_module_should_only_call_eventbrite_via_EbDiscountSyncService_facade =\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\"..promotions..\")\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\n                \"..shared.eventbrite.service.Default*\",\n                \"..shared.eventbrite.client..\"\n            );\n\n    @Test\n    void CouponAppliedEvent_and_CouponValidatedEvent_should_reside_in_shared_module_only() {\n        org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat(CouponAppliedEvent.class.getPackageName())\n            .contains(\"shared.common.event.published\");\n        org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat(CouponValidatedEvent.class.getPackageName())\n            .contains(\"shared.common.event.published\");\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void promotions_event_listeners_should_only_accept_shared_event_types() {\n        List<Class<?>> allowed = List.of(\n            BookingConfirmedEvent.class,\n            BookingCancelledEvent.class,\n            PaymentFailedEvent.class,\n            CartAssembledEvent.class\n        );\n        List<Class<?>> listeners = List.of(\n            com.eventplatform.promotions.event.listener.BookingConfirmedListener.class,\n            com.eventplatform.promotions.event.listener.BookingCancelledListener.class,\n            com.eventplatform.promotions.event.listener.PaymentFailedListener.class,\n            com.eventplatform.promotions.event.listener.CartAssembledListener.class\n        );\n\n        for (Class<?> listener : listeners) {\n            for (Method method : listener.getDeclaredMethods()) {\n                if (method.getAnnotation(TransactionalEventListener.class) == null) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat(method.getParameterTypes())\n                    .hasSize(1);\n                org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat(allowed)\n                    .contains(method.getParameterTypes()[0]);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension;\nimport org.springframework.context.ApplicationEventPublisher;\n\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\n@MockitoSettings(strictness = Strictness.LENIENT)\nclass CouponEligibilityServiceTest {\n\n    @Mock\n    private CouponRepository couponRepository;\n    @Mock\n    private CouponUsageReservationRepository reservationRepository;\n    @Mock\n    private CouponRedemptionRepository redemptionRepository;\n    @Mock\n    private CartSnapshotReader cartSnapshotReader;\n    @Mock\n    private SlotSummaryReader slotSummaryReader;\n    @Mock\n    private ApplicationEventPublisher eventPublisher;\n\n    @InjectMocks\n    private CouponEligibilityService service;\n\n    private Promotion promotion;\n    private Coupon coupon;\n    private CartSummaryDto cartSummary;\n\n    @BeforeEach\n    void setUp() {\n        promotion = new Promotion(\n            1L,\n            \"Promo\",\n            DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF,\n            new BigDecimal(\"10\"),\n            PromotionScope.ORG_WIDE,\n            null,\n            10,\n            2,\n            Instant.now().minusSeconds(3600),\n            Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600)\n        );\n        coupon = new Coupon(promotion, 1L, \"SAVE10\");\n        cartSummary = new CartSummaryDto(100L, 1L, 10L, null, Instant.now().plusSeconds(1800), \"inr\");\n\n        when(cartSnapshotReader.getCartSummary(100L)).thenReturn(cartSummary);\n        when(cartSnapshotReader.getCartItems(100L)).thenReturn(List.of(\n            new CartItemSnapshotDto(1L, null, null, \"tc1\", 10_000L, \"inr\", 1)\n        ));\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(coupon));\n        when(reservationRepository.countActiveByCouponId(any(), any())).thenReturn(0L);\n        when(redemptionRepository.countByCouponIdAndUserIdAndVoidedFalse(any(), any())).thenReturn(0L);\n        when(reservationRepository.findActiveByCouponIdAndCartId(any(), any(), any())).thenReturn(Optional.empty());\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_return_discount_breakdown_for_valid_active_coupon() {\n        DiscountBreakdownResponse result = service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L));\n\n        assertThat(result.couponCode()).isEqualTo(\"SAVE10\");\n        assertThat(result.discountAmountInSmallestUnit()).isEqualTo(1000L);\n        assertThat(result.adjustedCartTotalInSmallestUnit()).isEqualTo(9000L);\n        verify(reservationRepository).save(any(CouponUsageReservation.class));\n        verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(CouponValidatedEvent.class));\n        verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(CouponAppliedEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponExpiredException_when_outside_validity_window() {\n        Promotion expiredPromotion = new Promotion(\n            1L, \"Promo\", DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF, new BigDecimal(\"10\"), PromotionScope.ORG_WIDE,\n            null, 10, 2, Instant.now().minusSeconds(7200), Instant.now().minusSeconds(3600)\n        );\n        Coupon expiredCoupon = new Coupon(expiredPromotion, 1L, \"SAVE10\");\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(expiredCoupon));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponExpiredException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponInactiveException_when_status_is_EXHAUSTED() {\n        for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {\n            coupon.recordRedemption(10);\n        }\n        assertThat(coupon.getStatus()).isEqualTo(CouponStatus.EXHAUSTED);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponInactiveException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponOrgMismatchException_when_coupon_org_differs_from_cart_org() {\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\"))\n            .thenReturn(Optional.of(new Coupon(promotion, 2L, \"SAVE10\")));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponOrgMismatchException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponEventMismatchException_for_event_scoped_coupon_on_wrong_slot() {\n        Promotion eventScoped = new Promotion(\n            1L, \"Promo\", DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF, new BigDecimal(\"10\"), PromotionScope.EVENT_SCOPED,\n            \"eb-event-1\", 10, 2, Instant.now().minusSeconds(3600), Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600)\n        );\n        Coupon scopedCoupon = new Coupon(eventScoped, 1L, \"SAVE10\");\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(scopedCoupon));\n        when(slotSummaryReader.getSlotSummary(10L))\n            .thenReturn(new SlotSummaryDto(10L, \"ACTIVE\", \"eb-event-2\", SeatingMode.GA, 1L, 1L, 1L, null));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponEventMismatchException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponUsageLimitReachedException_when_count_plus_reservations_equals_max() {\n        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n            coupon.recordRedemption(100);\n        }\n        when(reservationRepository.countActiveByCouponId(any(), any())).thenReturn(1L);\n        Promotion limitPromotion = new Promotion(\n            1L, \"Promo\", DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF, new BigDecimal(\"10\"), PromotionScope.ORG_WIDE,\n            null, 5, 2, Instant.now().minusSeconds(3600), Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600)\n        );\n        Coupon limitCoupon = new Coupon(limitPromotion, 1L, \"SAVE10\");\n        for (int i = 0; i < 4; i++) {\n            limitCoupon.recordRedemption(100);\n        }\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(limitCoupon));\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponUsageLimitReachedException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_throw_CouponPerUserCapReachedException_when_user_has_reached_per_user_cap() {\n        Promotion capPromotion = new Promotion(\n            1L, \"Promo\", DiscountType.PERCENT_OFF, new BigDecimal(\"10\"), PromotionScope.ORG_WIDE,\n            null, 10, 1, Instant.now().minusSeconds(3600), Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600)\n        );\n        Coupon capCoupon = new Coupon(capPromotion, 1L, \"SAVE10\");\n        when(couponRepository.findTopByCodeIgnoreCase(\"SAVE10\")).thenReturn(Optional.of(capCoupon));\n        when(redemptionRepository.countByCouponIdAndUserIdAndVoidedFalse(any(), any())).thenReturn(1L);\n\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L)))\n            .isInstanceOf(CouponPerUserCapReachedException.class);\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_return_idempotent_response_when_reservation_already_exists_for_same_cart() {\n        when(reservationRepository.findActiveByCouponIdAndCartId(any(), any(), any()))\n            .thenReturn(Optional.of(new CouponUsageReservation(coupon, 100L, 99L, Instant.now().plusSeconds(1200))));\n\n        DiscountBreakdownResponse result = service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L));\n\n        assertThat(result.discountAmountInSmallestUnit()).isEqualTo(1000L);\n        verify(reservationRepository, never()).save(any(CouponUsageReservation.class));\n        verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any(CouponValidatedEvent.class));\n        verify(eventPublisher, never()).publishEvent(any(CouponAppliedEvent.class));\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void validate_should_create_CouponUsageReservation_with_expiresAt_matching_cart_expiry() {\n        Instant expiry = Instant.now().plusSeconds(1800);\n        when(cartSnapshotReader.getCartSummary(100L)).thenReturn(new CartSummaryDto(100L, 1L, 10L, null, expiry, \"inr\"));\n\n        service.validateAndApply(99L, new CouponValidateRequest(\"SAVE10\", 100L));\n\n        ArgumentCaptor<CouponUsageReservation> captor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(CouponUsageReservation.class);\n        verify(reservationRepository).save(captor.capture());\n        assertThat(captor.getValue().getExpiresAt()).isEqualTo(expiry);\n    }\n}\n"
  }
}

    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/Review.java\n@@\n     public Review(Long userId, Long eventId, int rating, String title, String body, AttendanceVerificationStatus attendanceVerificationStatus) {\n+        if (rating < 1 || rating > 5) {\n+            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"rating must be between 1 and 5\");\n+        }\n         this.userId = userId;\n         this.eventId = eventId;\n         this.rating = rating;\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

## Entry 678
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:35:43.564Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:35:43.564Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_KlCjNuuoxlbA8wqpYjo9t6uT",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibility.java\n@@\n     public void unlock(Long slotId, Long bookingId, Instant eligibleUntil) {\n+        if (this.status == ReviewEligibilityStatus.REVOKED) {\n+            return;\n+        }\n         this.slotId = slotId;\n         this.bookingId = bookingId;\n         this.eligibleUntil = eligibleUntil;\n         this.status = ReviewEligibilityStatus.UNLOCKED;\n     }\n@@\n     public void markExpired() {\n         this.status = ReviewEligibilityStatus.EXPIRED;\n     }\n+\n+    public boolean isEligible(Instant now) {\n+        if (status != ReviewEligibilityStatus.UNLOCKED) {\n+            return false;\n+        }\n+        return eligibleUntil == null || !eligibleUntil.isBefore(now);\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibility.java\n@@\n     public void unlock(Long slotId, Long bookingId, Instant eligibleUntil) {\n+        if (this.status == ReviewEligibilityStatus.REVOKED) {\n+            return;\n+        }\n         this.slotId = slotId;\n         this.bookingId = bookingId;\n         this.eligibleUntil = eligibleUntil;\n         this.status = ReviewEligibilityStatus.UNLOCKED;\n     }\n@@\n     public void markExpired() {\n         this.status = ReviewEligibilityStatus.EXPIRED;\n     }\n+\n+    public boolean isEligible(Instant now) {\n+        if (status != ReviewEligibilityStatus.UNLOCKED) {\n+            return false;\n+        }\n+        return eligibleUntil == null || !eligibleUntil.isBefore(now);\n+    }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

## Entry 696
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:36:12.709Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:36:12.709Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_motgcRMXaaPfGAAhFpGTfsZf",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/controller/AdminModerationController.java\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.engagement.api.dto.request.ModerationDecisionRequest;\n import com.eventplatform.engagement.api.dto.response.AdminReviewResponse;\n import com.eventplatform.engagement.api.dto.response.ModerationResponse;\n import com.eventplatform.engagement.domain.enums.ReviewStatus;\n import com.eventplatform.engagement.service.ModerationService;\n+import com.eventplatform.engagement.service.model.ManualModerationDecision;\n+import com.eventplatform.shared.common.exception.ValidationException;\n@@\n     public ModerationResponse moderate(\n         Authentication authentication,\n         @PathVariable Long reviewId,\n         @Valid @RequestBody ModerationDecisionRequest request\n     ) {\n+        if (request.decision() == ManualModerationDecision.REJECT\n+            && (request.reason() == null || request.reason().isBlank())) {\n+            throw new ValidationException(\"reason is required for reject decision\", \"VALIDATION_ERROR\");\n+        }\n         AuthenticatedUser admin = (AuthenticatedUser) authentication.getPrincipal();\n         return moderationService.applyManualDecision(admin.userId(), reviewId, request.decision(), request.reason());\n     }\n }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 704
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:36:32.466Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:36:32.466Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"mkdir -p engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/{domain,service,api,arch} && cat > engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewTest.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.engagement.domain;\\n\\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThatThrownBy;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.engagement.domain.enums.AttendanceVerificationStatus;\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\\n\\nclass ReviewTest {\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_throw_when_publish_called_from_submitted_status() {\\n        Review review = new Review(1L, 101L, 4, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n\\n        assertThatThrownBy(review::publish)\\n            .isInstanceOf(RuntimeException.class);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_publish_when_status_is_approved() {\\n        Review review = new Review(1L, 101L, 4, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n        review.markPendingModeration();\\n        review.approve();\\n\\n        review.publish();\\n\\n        assertThat(review.getStatus().name()).isEqualTo(\\\"PUBLISHED\\\");\\n        assertThat(review.getPublishedAt()).isNotNull();\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_keep_rejected_as_terminal_when_setters_called() {\\n        Review review = new Review(1L, 101L, 4, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n        review.markPendingModeration();\\n        review.reject(\\\"bad content\\\");\\n\\n        assertThatThrownBy(review::approve).isInstanceOf(RuntimeException.class);\\n        assertThatThrownBy(review::publish).isInstanceOf(RuntimeException.class);\\n        assertThat(review.getStatus().name()).isEqualTo(\\\"REJECTED\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_construct_review_when_rating_is_on_valid_boundaries() {\\n        Review low = new Review(1L, 101L, 1, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n        Review high = new Review(1L, 102L, 5, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n\\n        assertThat(low.getRating()).isEqualTo(1);\\n        assertThat(high.getRating()).isEqualTo(5);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_throw_illegal_argument_when_rating_outside_boundaries() {\\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Review(1L, 101L, 0, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED))\\n            .isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class);\\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> new Review(1L, 101L, 6, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED))\\n            .isInstanceOf(IllegalArgumentException.class);\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibilityTest.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.engagement.domain;\\n\\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.engagement.domain.enums.ReviewEligibilityStatus;\\nimport java.time.Instant;\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\\n\\nclass ReviewEligibilityTest {\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_not_unlock_when_status_is_revoked() {\\n        ReviewEligibility eligibility = new ReviewEligibility(10L, 20L, 30L, 40L, Instant.now().plusSeconds(3600));\\n        eligibility.revoke();\\n\\n        eligibility.unlock(50L, 60L, Instant.now().plusSeconds(7200));\\n\\n        assertThat(eligibility.getStatus()).isEqualTo(ReviewEligibilityStatus.REVOKED);\\n        assertThat(eligibility.getSlotId()).isEqualTo(30L);\\n        assertThat(eligibility.getBookingId()).isEqualTo(40L);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_return_false_for_eligibility_when_window_expired() {\\n        ReviewEligibility eligibility = new ReviewEligibility(10L, 20L, 30L, 40L, Instant.now().minusSeconds(86400));\\n\\n        boolean eligible = eligibility.isEligible(Instant.now());\\n\\n        assertThat(eligible).isFalse();\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_return_true_for_eligibility_when_unlocked_and_window_not_expired() {\\n        ReviewEligibility eligibility = new ReviewEligibility(10L, 20L, 30L, 40L, Instant.now().plusSeconds(86400));\\n\\n        boolean eligible = eligibility.isEligible(Instant.now());\\n\\n        assertThat(eligible).isTrue();\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_jnSGEeGp42bDN9DqVfoVwVJ1"
  }
}
```
