when(moderationRecordRepository.findByDecisionAndRetryAfterLessThanEqualAndAutoRetryCountLessThan(\\n            ModerationDecision.PENDING,\\n            any(Instant.class),\\n            3\\n        )).thenReturn(List.of(m1, m2));\\n        when(openAiModerationService.moderate(any()))\\n            .thenReturn(new OpenAiModerationService.ModerationResult(false, List.of(), Map.of()));\\n\\n        moderationService.retryPendingModerations();\\n\\n        verify(openAiModerationService).moderate(\\\"a\\\");\\n        verify(openAiModerationService).moderate(\\\"b\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_continue_processing_when_first_retry_record_fails() {\\n        Review r1 = pendingReview(1L);\\n        Review r2 = pendingReview(2L);\\n        ModerationRecord m1 = new ModerationRecord(r1, ModerationMethod.AUTO, \\\"a\\\");\\n        ModerationRecord m2 = new ModerationRecord(r2, ModerationMethod.AUTO, \\\"b\\\");\\n\\n        when(moderationRecordRepository.findByDecisionAndRetryAfterLessThanEqualAndAutoRetryCountLessThan(\\n            ModerationDecision.PENDING,\\n            any(Instant.class),\\n            3\\n        )).thenReturn(List.of(m1, m2));\\n        when(openAiModerationService.moderate(\\\"a\\\")).thenThrow(new RuntimeException(\\\"fail first\\\"));\\n        when(openAiModerationService.moderate(\\\"b\\\"))\\n            .thenReturn(new OpenAiModerationService.ModerationResult(false, List.of(), Map.of()));\\n\\n        moderationService.retryPendingModerations();\\n\\n        assertThat(r2.getStatus()).isEqualTo(ReviewStatus.PUBLISHED);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_publish_review_when_manual_approve_decision_applied() throws Exception {\\n        Review review = pendingReview(1L);\\n        when(reviewRepository.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(review));\\n\\n        var response = moderationService.applyManualDecision(99L, 1L, ManualModerationDecision.APPROVE, null);\\n\\n        assertThat(response.newStatus()).isEqualTo(ReviewStatus.PUBLISHED);\\n        verify(eventPublisher).publishEvent(any(ReviewPublishedEvent.class));\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_reject_review_when_manual_reject_decision_applied() {\\n        Review review = pendingReview(1L);\\n        when(reviewRepository.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(review));\\n\\n        var response = moderationService.applyManualDecision(99L, 1L, ManualModerationDecision.REJECT, \\\"policy\\\");\\n\\n        assertThat(response.newStatus()).isEqualTo(ReviewStatus.REJECTED);\\n        assertThat(review.getRejectionReason()).isEqualTo(\\\"policy\\\");\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_throw_review_already_moderated_when_manual_decision_attempted_on_published_review() {\\n        Review review = new Review(11L, 1001L, 4, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n        review.markPendingModeration();\\n        review.approve();\\n        review.publish();\\n        when(reviewRepository.findById(1L)).thenReturn(Optional.of(review));\\n\\n        assertThatThrownBy(() -> moderationService.applyManualDecision(99L, 1L, ManualModerationDecision.REJECT, \\\"x\\\"))\\n            .isInstanceOf(ReviewAlreadyModeratedException.class);\\n    }\\n\\n    private Review pendingReview(Long id) {\\n        Review review = new Review(11L, 1001L, 4, \\\"title\\\", \\\"body\\\", AttendanceVerificationStatus.EB_VERIFIED);\\n        review.markPendingModeration();\\n        try {\\n            java.lang.reflect.Field idField = com.eventplatform.shared.common.domain.BaseEntity.class.getDeclaredField(\\\"id\\\");\\n            idField.setAccessible(true);\\n            idField.set(review, id);\\n        } catch (Exception ignored) {\\n        }\\n        return review;\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\\n\\ncat > engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.engagement.service;\\n\\nimport static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\\nimport static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq;\\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.doThrow;\\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.never;\\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.verify;\\nimport static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.engagement.service.model.ReviewRatingSummary;\\nimport com.eventplatform.engagement.repository.ReviewRepository;\\nimport com.eventplatform.shared.common.event.published.ReviewPublishedEvent;\\nimport java.math.BigDecimal;\\nimport java.time.Duration;\\nimport java.time.Instant;\\nimport java.util.Map;\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\\nimport org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;\\nimport org.mockito.InjectMocks;\\nimport org.mockito.Mock;\\nimport org.mockito.junit.jupiter.MockitoExtension;\\nimport org.springframework.data.redis.core.RedisTemplate;\\nimport org.springframework.data.redis.core.ValueOperations;\\n\\n@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)\\nclass ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest {\\n\\n    @Mock\\n    private RedisTemplate<String, Object> redisTemplate;\\n    @Mock\\n    private ValueOperations<String, Object> valueOperations;\\n    @Mock\\n    private ReviewRepository reviewRepository;\\n\\n    @InjectMocks\\n    private ReviewRatingSummaryService service;\\n\\n    @BeforeEach\\n    void setUp() {\\n        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_return_summary_from_cache_when_cache_hit() {\\n        ReviewRatingSummary cached = new ReviewRatingSummary(1001L, new BigDecimal(\\\"4.2\\\"), 10L, Map.of(1, 1L), Instant.now());\\n        when(valueOperations.get(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\")).thenReturn(cached);\\n\\n        var response = service.getSummary(1001L);\\n\\n        assertThat(response.averageRating()).isEqualTo(new BigDecimal(\\\"4.2\\\"));\\n        verify(reviewRepository, never()).summarizeByEventId(any());\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_compute_and_cache_summary_when_cache_miss() {\\n        when(valueOperations.get(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\")).thenReturn(null);\\n        when(reviewRepository.summarizeByEventId(1001L)).thenReturn(new Object[]{\\n            new BigDecimal(\\\"4.25\\\"), 20L, 1L, 2L, 3L, 4L, 10L\\n        });\\n\\n        var response = service.getSummary(1001L);\\n\\n        assertThat(response.averageRating()).isEqualTo(new BigDecimal(\\\"4.3\\\"));\\n        verify(valueOperations).set(eq(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\"), any(ReviewRatingSummary.class), eq(Duration.ofMinutes(5)));\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_fallback_to_db_when_redis_get_throws() {\\n        when(valueOperations.get(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\")).thenThrow(new RuntimeException(\\\"redis down\\\"));\\n        when(reviewRepository.summarizeByEventId(1001L)).thenReturn(new Object[]{\\n            new BigDecimal(\\\"4.0\\\"), 5L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L\\n        });\\n\\n        var response = service.getSummary(1001L);\\n\\n        assertThat(response.totalReviews()).isEqualTo(5L);\\n        verify(reviewRepository).summarizeByEventId(1001L);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_return_result_when_redis_set_throws() {\\n        when(valueOperations.get(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\")).thenReturn(null);\\n        when(reviewRepository.summarizeByEventId(1001L)).thenReturn(new Object[]{\\n            new BigDecimal(\\\"4.0\\\"), 5L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L, 1L\\n        });\\n        doThrow(new RuntimeException(\\\"set fail\\\")).when(valueOperations)\\n            .set(eq(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\"), any(ReviewRatingSummary.class), eq(Duration.ofMinutes(5)));\\n\\n        var response = service.getSummary(1001L);\\n\\n        assertThat(response.totalReviews()).isEqualTo(5L);\\n    }\\n\\n    @Test\\n    void should_invalidate_cache_when_review_published_event_received() {\\n        service.onReviewPublished(new ReviewPublishedEvent(1L, 1001L, 4));\\n\\n        verify(redisTemplate).delete(\\\"engagement:review:summary:1001\\\");\\n    }\\n}\\nEOF\"}",

## Entry 722
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:39:49.414Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:39:49.414Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/arch/EngagementArchTest.java <<'EOF'\\npackage com.eventplatform.engagement.arch;\\n\\nimport static com.tngtech.archunit.lang.syntax.ArchRuleDefinition.noClasses;\\n\\nimport com.eventplatform.engagement.api.dto.response.ReviewResponse;\\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.AnalyzeClasses;\\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.junit.ArchTest;\\nimport com.tngtech.archunit.lang.ArchRule;\\nimport org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.ControllerAdvice;\\n\\n@AnalyzeClasses(packages = \\\"com.eventplatform.engagement\\\")\\nclass EngagementArchTest {\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule should_not_import_other_module_internal_classes =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\\n                \\\"..paymentsticketing..\\\",\\n                \\\"..bookinginventory..\\\",\\n                \\\"..identity..\\\",\\n                \\\"..promotions..\\\",\\n                \\\"..scheduling..\\\",\\n                \\\"..discoverycatalog..\\\"\\n            );\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule should_not_call_eventbrite_directly =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\\\"..com.eventbrite..\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule should_not_call_openai_sdk_directly =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\\\"..com.theokanning.openai..\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule should_not_have_controller_advice_in_engagement =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class);\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule service_and_controller_should_not_construct_review_response_directly =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAnyPackage(\\\"..engagement.service..\\\", \\\"..engagement.api.controller..\\\")\\n            .should().callConstructor(ReviewResponse.class);\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_5BhiyzVhUj8rSdN0BmaL6ih2"
  }
}
```

.should().dependOnClassesThat().resideInAnyPackage(\\\"..com.theokanning.openai..\\\");\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule should_not_have_controller_advice_in_engagement =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAPackage(\\\"..engagement..\\\")\\n            .should().beAnnotatedWith(ControllerAdvice.class);\\n\\n    @ArchTest\\n    static final ArchRule service_and_controller_should_not_construct_review_response_directly =\\n        noClasses().that().resideInAnyPackage(\\\"..engagement.service..\\\", \\\"..engagement.api.controller..\\\")\\n            .should().callConstructor(ReviewResponse.class);\\n}\\nEOF\"}",
```json
    "call_id": "call_5BhiyzVhUj8rSdN0BmaL6ih2"
  }
}
```

## Entry 762
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:41:01.277Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:41:01.277Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_k2yQHSoHA9tDjnRFdXTRCwDT",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/AttendanceVerificationServiceTest.java\n@@\n import com.eventplatform.shared.eventbrite.service.EbTokenStore;\n import java.util.List;\n-import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\n import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n@@\n-    @BeforeEach\n-    void setUp() {\n-        when(identityLookupService.getEmail(11L)).thenReturn(\"user@test.com\");\n-    }\n-\n     @Test\n     void should_return_eb_verified_when_booking_confirmed_and_eb_attendee_matches() {\n@@\n         when(eventMetadataLookupService.getEventMetadataOrNull(1001L))\n             .thenReturn(new EventMetadataLookupService.EventMetadata(1001L, 501L, \"eb-1\"));\n+        when(identityLookupService.getEmail(11L)).thenReturn(\"user@test.com\");\n         when(ebTokenStore.getAccessToken(501L)).thenReturn(\"token\");\n@@\n         when(eventMetadataLookupService.getEventMetadataOrNull(1001L))\n             .thenReturn(new EventMetadataLookupService.EventMetadata(1001L, 501L, \"eb-1\"));\n+        when(identityLookupService.getEmail(11L)).thenReturn(\"user@test.com\");\n         when(ebTokenStore.getAccessToken(501L)).thenReturn(\"token\");\n@@\n         when(eventMetadataLookupService.getEventMetadataOrNull(1001L))\n             .thenReturn(new EventMetadataLookupService.EventMetadata(1001L, 501L, \"eb-1\"));\n+        when(identityLookupService.getEmail(11L)).thenReturn(\"user@test.com\");\n         when(ebTokenStore.getAccessToken(501L)).thenReturn(\"token\");\n@@\n         when(eventMetadataLookupService.getEventMetadataOrNull(1001L))\n             .thenReturn(new EventMetadataLookupService.EventMetadata(1001L, 501L, \"eb-1\"));\n+        when(identityLookupService.getEmail(11L)).thenReturn(\"user@test.com\");\n         when(ebTokenStore.getAccessToken(501L)).thenThrow(new EbAuthException(\"auth\"));\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

```json
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java\n@@\n import java.math.BigDecimal;\n import java.time.Duration;\n import java.time.Instant;\n import java.util.Map;\n-import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\n import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n@@\n-    @BeforeEach\n-    void setUp() {\n-        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\n-    }\n-\n     @Test\n     void should_return_summary_from_cache_when_cache_hit() {\n+        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\n         ReviewRatingSummary cached = new ReviewRatingSummary(1001L, new BigDecimal(\"4.2\"), 10L, Map.of(1, 1L), Instant.now());\n         when(valueOperations.get(\"engagement:review:summary:1001\")).thenReturn(cached);\n@@\n     @Test\n     void should_compute_and_cache_summary_when_cache_miss() {\n+        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\n         when(valueOperations.get(\"engagement:review:summary:1001\")).thenReturn(null);\n@@\n     @Test\n     void should_fallback_to_db_when_redis_get_throws() {\n+        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\n         when(valueOperations.get(\"engagement:review:summary:1001\")).thenThrow(new RuntimeException(\"redis down\"));\n@@\n     @Test\n     void should_return_result_when_redis_set_throws() {\n+        when(redisTemplate.opsForValue()).thenReturn(valueOperations);\n         when(valueOperations.get(\"engagement:review:summary:1001\")).thenReturn(null);\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:41:26.067Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_dQ78SmMRWgSZqMObqfO19WvY",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/ReviewControllerTest.java\n@@\n import java.math.BigDecimal;\n import java.time.Instant;\n import java.util.List;\n import java.util.Map;\n import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeEach;\n import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;\n import org.mockito.ArgumentCaptor;\n+import static org.assertj.core.api.Assertions.assertThat;\n@@\n         ArgumentCaptor<Pageable> pageableCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(Pageable.class);\n         verify(reviewService).listPublishedReviews(any(), pageableCaptor.capture());\n         Pageable pageable = pageableCaptor.getValue();\n-        assert pageable.getSort().getOrderFor(\"publishedAt\") != null;\n-        assert pageable.getSort().getOrderFor(\"publishedAt\").isDescending();\n+        assertThat(pageable.getSort().getOrderFor(\"publishedAt\")).isNotNull();\n+        assertThat(pageable.getSort().getOrderFor(\"publishedAt\").isDescending()).isTrue();\n     }\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

```json
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ModerationServiceTest.java\n@@\n import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.any;\n+import static org.mockito.ArgumentMatchers.eq;\n import static org.mockito.Mockito.never;\n+import static org.mockito.Mockito.times;\n import static org.mockito.Mockito.verify;\n import static org.mockito.Mockito.when;\n@@\n         assertThat(review.getStatus()).isEqualTo(ReviewStatus.PUBLISHED);\n         ArgumentCaptor<ModerationRecord> recordCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(ModerationRecord.class);\n-        verify(moderationRecordRepository).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n-        verify(moderationRecordRepository).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n+        verify(moderationRecordRepository, times(2)).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n         ModerationRecord finalRecord = recordCaptor.getAllValues().get(1);\n         assertThat(finalRecord.getDecision()).isEqualTo(ModerationDecision.APPROVED);\n@@\n         moderationService.triggerAutoModeration(1L);\n \n         ArgumentCaptor<ModerationRecord> recordCaptor = ArgumentCaptor.forClass(ModerationRecord.class);\n-        verify(moderationRecordRepository).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n-        verify(moderationRecordRepository).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n+        verify(moderationRecordRepository, times(2)).save(recordCaptor.capture());\n         ModerationRecord finalRecord = recordCaptor.getAllValues().get(1);\n         assertThat(finalRecord.getDecision()).isEqualTo(ModerationDecision.PENDING);\n@@\n         when(moderationRecordRepository.findByDecisionAndRetryAfterLessThanEqualAndAutoRetryCountLessThan(\n-            ModerationDecision.PENDING,\n-            any(Instant.class),\n-            3\n+            eq(ModerationDecision.PENDING),\n+            any(Instant.class),\n+            eq(3)\n         )).thenReturn(List.of(record));\n@@\n         when(moderationRecordRepository.findByDecisionAndRetryAfterLessThanEqualAndAutoRetryCountLessThan(\n-            ModerationDecision.PENDING,\n-            any(Instant.class),\n-            3\n+            eq(ModerationDecision.PENDING),\n+            any(Instant.class),\n+            eq(3)\n         )).thenReturn(List.of(m1, m2));\n@@\n         when(moderationRecordRepository.findByDecisionAndRetryAfterLessThanEqualAndAutoRetryCountLessThan(\n-            ModerationDecision.PENDING,\n-            any(Instant.class),\n-            3\n+            eq(ModerationDecision.PENDING),\n+            any(Instant.class),\n+            eq(3)\n         )).thenReturn(List.of(m1, m2));\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 816
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:43:05.459Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:43:05.459Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call",
    "name": "exec_command",
    "arguments": "{\"cmd\":\"cat > test_result_folder/fr8_engagement_reviews_20260305_071244.md <<'EOF'\\n# Test Result — FR8 Engagement Reviews & Moderation\\n\\n- Timestamp: 2026-03-05 07:12:44 IST\\n- Spec: POQ/Engagement/SPEC_8.md (Stage 8)\\n- Module(s): engagement\\n- Commit/Working State: 37bccac + local uncommitted changes\\n\\n## Coverage of Stage 8 Cases\\n- Total planned: 62\\n- Implemented: 62\\n- Passed: 62\\n- Failed: 0\\n- Skipped: 0\\n\\n## Failures Found\\n1. `ModerationServiceTest` initialization failed (`MeterRegistry` null with `@InjectMocks`)\\n   - Error: `InjectMocksException` / `NullPointerException`\\n   - Root cause: Mockito attempted constructor injection before manual setup.\\n   - Fix applied: Removed `@InjectMocks`; instantiated `ModerationService` explicitly in `@BeforeEach` with `SimpleMeterRegistry`.\\n\\n2. API `@WebMvcTest` bootstrap failed\\n   - Error: `Unable to find a @SpringBootConfiguration`\\n   - Root cause: New `engagement` module lacked test boot configuration class.\\n   - Fix applied: Added `EngagementTestApplication` under `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/`.\\n\\n3. Strict Mockito unnecessary stubbing failures\\n   - Error: `UnnecessaryStubbingException` in attendance and summary service tests.\\n   - Root cause: Shared setup stubs unused in subset of tests.\\n   - Fix applied: Moved stubs into per-test scope and removed global stubbing.\\n\\n4. ArchUnit boundary rule false-positive\\n   - Error: Rule flagged `@Scheduled`/`@Async` annotations as forbidden due to `..scheduling..` pattern.\\n   - Root cause: Over-broad package match captured Spring framework packages.\\n   - Fix applied: Changed forbidden package patterns to explicit module namespaces (`com.eventplatform.scheduling..`, etc.).\\n\\n5. `ModerationServiceTest` matcher and verification failures\\n   - Error: `InvalidUseOfMatchersException` and `TooManyActualInvocations`.\\n   - Root cause: Mixed raw args + matchers; duplicate `verify(save())` expectations against two actual saves.\\n   - Fix applied: Used `eq(...)` consistently with matchers and `times(2)` for save verification.\\n\\n## Code/Test Changes Applied\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewTest.java` — Stage 8 domain tests for review lifecycle and rating boundaries.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibilityTest.java` — Stage 8 domain tests for eligibility status/window behavior.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewServiceTest.java` — Stage 8 service tests for submission guards and event publish.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/AttendanceVerificationServiceTest.java` — Stage 8 service tests for booking/EB advisory logic.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ModerationServiceTest.java` — Stage 8 service tests for auto/manual moderation and retries.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewRatingSummaryServiceTest.java` — Stage 8 service tests for cache hit/miss/error/invalidation.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/ReviewControllerTest.java` — Stage 8 controller contract/security tests for user/public endpoints.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/AdminModerationControllerTest.java` — Stage 8 controller contract/security tests for admin moderation endpoints.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/arch/EngagementArchTest.java` — Stage 8 ArchUnit boundary/ACL/mapping rules.\\n- `engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/EngagementTestApplication.java` — Test boot configuration for module `@WebMvcTest` loading.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/Review.java` — Added rating boundary guard (`IllegalArgumentException` for invalid ratings).\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibility.java` — Added revoked unlock guard + `isEligible(now)` helper.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/controller/ReviewController.java` — Default public list sort changed to `publishedAt,desc`.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/repository/ReviewRepository.java` — Published list query switched to `OrderByPublishedAtDesc`.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewService.java` — Updated published list repository method call.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/controller/AdminModerationController.java` — Added explicit REJECT reason validation for 400 contract.\\n\\n## Final Status\\n- PASS\\n- Notes: Executed `mvn -q -pl engagement test`; all Stage 8 tests in engagement module pass.\\nEOF\"}",
    "call_id": "call_gdL7He7XuaIAo5VjdrD16WSG"
  }
}
```

`engagement/src/test/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/EngagementTestApplication.java` — Test boot configuration for module `@WebMvcTest` loading.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/Review.java` — Added rating boundary guard (`IllegalArgumentException` for invalid ratings).\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/domain/ReviewEligibility.java` — Added revoked unlock guard + `isEligible(now)` helper.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/controller/ReviewController.java` — Default public list sort changed to `publishedAt,desc`.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/repository/ReviewRepository.java` — Published list query switched to `OrderByPublishedAtDesc`.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/service/ReviewService.java` — Updated published list repository method call.\\n- `engagement/src/main/java/com/eventplatform/engagement/api/controller/AdminModerationController.java` — Added explicit REJECT reason validation for 400 contract.\\n\\n## Final Status\\n- PASS\\n- Notes: Executed `mvn -q -pl engagement test`; all Stage 8 tests in engagement module pass.\\nEOF\"}",
```json
    "call_id": "call_gdL7He7XuaIAo5VjdrD16WSG"
  }
}
```

## Entry 827
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-05T01:46:30.997Z
**Role/Type:** turn_context
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-05T01:46:30.997Z",
  "type": "turn_context",
  "payload": {
    "turn_id": "019cbbac-d0d3-76c1-a9b6-b1e2e6f1eb13",
    "cwd": "/home/dell/Multi-City-Event-Booking",
    "current_date": "2026-03-05",
    "timezone": "Asia/Kolkata",
    "approval_policy": "on-request",
    "sandbox_policy": {
      "type": "workspace-write",
      "network_access": false,
      "exclude_tmpdir_env_var": false,
      "exclude_slash_tmp": false
    },
    "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
    "personality": "pragmatic",
    "collaboration_mode": {
      "mode": "default",
      "settings": {
        "model": "gpt-5.3-codex",
        "reasoning_effort": "medium",
        "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }
    },
    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md — Reference Index\n\n> Every agent working in this codebase must read the files listed below before producing\n> any output. Each file governs a specific concern. Read the ones that apply to your task.\n\n---\n\n## Reference Files\n\n| File | Governs |\n|---|---|\n| `PRODUCT.md` | The 7 modules, what each one owns, dependency order, inter-module event map, Eventbrite ACL facade names, hard architectural rules, and where new code goes. Read this before touching any module. |\n| `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` | Eventbrite integration architecture: ACL facades, all FR flows (FR1–FR7 implemented), critical constraints (no user creation, no seat locking; single-order cancel/refunds are handled via `StripeRefundService`). Read before designing any Eventbrite-touching feature. |\n| `docs/MODULE_STRUCTURE_TEMPLATE.md` | Canonical folder and package layout for every module. The exact location of every layer: `api/controller/`, `api/dto/`, `domain/`, `service/`, `repository/`, `mapper/`, `event/published/`, `event/listener/`, `statemachine/`, `exception/`. Anti-patterns list. Read this before writing any class. |\n| `agents/SPEC_GENERATOR.md` | How to produce a `SPEC.md` for any feature. Eight confirmation-gated stages with module context and Eventbrite constraints built-in. Requirements → Domain Model → Architecture & File Structure → DB Schema → API → Business Logic → Error Handling → Tests. Read this before speccing any feature. |\n| `agents/CODING_AGENT.md` | Spring Boot 3.5.11 / Java 21 coding standards. Domain behaviour, service orchestration, mapper rules, controller rules, event rules, 10 Eventbrite ACL facades, inter-module communication (REST + Spring Events), admin/ special rules, state machine, Spring AI. Read this before writing any production code. |\n| `agents/TEST_AGENT.md` | Test agent responsibilities and best practices. Builds tests from `SPEC_*.md` Stage 8, executes tests, fixes test/code issues caused by behavior mismatches, and writes timestamped logs under `test_result_folder/`. Read this before writing or running tests. |\n| `docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md` | Four-layer test structure: `domain/` (unit), `service/` (Mockito), `api/` (@WebMvcTest), `arch/` (ArchUnit enforcing 10 hard rules + Eventbrite constraints). All rule examples, happy path, negative path, exception patterns. Read before writing any test. |\n| `mock-eventbrite-api/README.md` | **Mock Eventbrite Service:** Python FastAPI in-memory mock of Eventbrite API v3. Used for local development and testing. Exposes all 10 ACL facade endpoints without hitting production Eventbrite. See setup instructions below. |\n\n---\n\n## Module Quick Reference\n\nFull detail for each module is in `PRODUCT.md`. This table is a lookup only.\n\n| Module | Owns | External ACL Facades Used |\n|---|---|---|\n| `discovery-catalog` | Events, Venues, Cities, catalog sync | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbTicketService` |\n| `scheduling` | Show slots, conflict validation | `EbEventSyncService` |\n| `identity` | Users, JWT, profiles, preferences, wallet | — |\n| `booking-inventory` | Seats, SeatMap, Cart, Pricing, Seat Lock State Machine | `EbTicketService` |\n| `payments-ticketing` | Bookings, Payments, E-Tickets, Cancellations, Refunds, Wallet | `StripePaymentService`, `StripeRefundService`, `StripeWebhookHandler` |\n| `promotions` | Coupons, Promotions, Eligibility rules | `EbDiscountSyncService` |\n| `engagement` | Reviews, Moderation, AI Chatbot (`service/chatbot/`), RAG pipeline | `EbEventSyncService`, `EbAttendeeService` |\n| `admin/` | No domain ownership. Orchestration and aggregation only. | — |\n| `shared/` | All external ACL facades, JWT, base classes, common DTOs, configs | — |\n\n---\n\n## Hard Rules — Never Violate\n\nCanonical numbering and wording are owned by `PRODUCT.md` Section 9. These shorthand rules apply to every task without exception.\n\n1. No module imports another module's `@Service`, `@Entity`, or `@Repository`.\n  - Exception: `admin/` MAY import module `@Service` beans for READ operations only; all writes MUST go through the owning module REST API.\n2. No module calls Eventbrite, Stripe, or OpenAI HTTP directly — only through `shared/` ACL facades.\n3. ACL facade names are exactly as listed below — never invent new ones.\n4. Spring Events carry only primitives, IDs, enums, and value objects — never `@Entity` objects.\n5. One `GlobalExceptionHandler` in `shared/common/exception/` — never `@ControllerAdvice` in a module.\n6. All field mapping goes in `mapper/` using MapStruct — never inside a service or controller.\n7. `admin/` owns no `@Entity` and no database table.\n8. `shared/` has no dependency on any module.\n9. `app/` has zero business logic.\n10. Dependency direction is strictly downward — no circular imports between modules.\n\n---\n\n## Eventbrite ACL Facade Names (HARD RULE #3)\nRead the docs/eventbriteapiv3public.apib for detail API Endpoint with schema, Response, Request and errors\n\nAll 10 facades are in `shared/eventbrite/service/`. These are the ONLY things modules call for Eventbrite:\n\n1. **EbEventSyncService** — Create, update, publish, cancel events; pull org event catalog\n2. **EbVenueService** — Create and update venues; list org venues\n3. **EbScheduleService** — Create recurring event schedules and occurrences\n4. **EbTicketService** — Create/update ticket classes; manage inventory; check availability\n5. **EbCapacityService** — Retrieve and update event capacity tiers\n6. **EbOrderService** — Read orders post-checkout (admin/ reporting only — NOT used in FR5/FR6 payment flows; orders are created internally in `bookings` table when Stripe payment is confirmed)\n7. **EbAttendeeService** — List attendees; verify attendance for reviews (engagement only — FR8 attendance verification)\n8. **EbDiscountSyncService** — Create, update, delete discount codes\n9. **EbRefundService** — INACTIVE for payments — all refunds go through `StripeRefundService`; original EB refund read-only API has no submission endpoint\n10. **EbWebhookService** — Mock/testing only; not used in production registration flow\n\n**See `docs/EVENTBRITE_INTEGRATION.md` for which module uses which facade and what gaps exist.**\n\n---\n\n## Stripe ACL Facades (payments-ticketing only)\n\nAll 3 facades are in `shared/stripe/service/`. Only `payments-ticketing` calls these:\n\n1. **StripePaymentService** — Create PaymentIntents; confirm and capture payments\n2. **StripeRefundService** — Submit refunds; query refund status\n3. **StripeWebhookHandler** — Verify webhook signatures; parse `payment_intent.succeeded` / `payment_intent.payment_failed` / `charge.refunded` events\n\n**Hard Rule:** Never call `com.stripe.*` SDK classes directly from a module service — always delegate to the facade in `shared/stripe/service/`.\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Order (HARD RULE #10)\n\n```\nshared/               <- foundational; no dependencies\n  |\n  v\ndiscovery-catalog    <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nscheduling           <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\nidentity             <- depends on shared only\n  |\n  v\nbooking-inventory    <- depends on shared + discovery-catalog (REST reads)\n  |\n  v\npayments-ticketing   <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\npromotions           <- depends on shared + booking-inventory + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nengagement           <- depends on shared + payments-ticketing + identity (REST)\n  |\n  v\nadmin/               <- thin orchestration; reads from all, writes via REST APIs\n  |\n  v\napp/                 <- entry point only; zero business logic\n```\n\n**Golden Rule:** Module at level N can depend on level N-1 or lower (or `shared/`) only. Never import from sibling or downstream modules.\n\n**See `PRODUCT.md` Section 2 for detailed rationale.**\n\n\n## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```
